name: Franceschi Park
formatted_address: 1510 Franceschi Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93103, USA

google_reviews.text: Great views. I met some friends from Sacramento here and they brought the best mandarin I ever had.

It is the best view of the Pacific Ocean and all of Santa Barbara. You just have to drive up there and see it for yourself.
And take an electric bike ride up there like we do. Enjoy

The best for sunset experience.
Do not make that last left into the park. Maps is wrong.
Go up the last short step hill and look for the two white columns on the left. This is the entrance.
Two big picnic tables are there.
No cost.
Funny little park. Nice views. Not much to do. Very uncrowded and a nice place for a picnic.

Easy to access by car - less than 10 minutes from downtown Santa Barbara. Parking lot + bathrooms available. Not the highest vista in SB, but stunning nonetheless. The history of the Franceschi House and surrounding foliage is quite interesting!

yelp_reviews.text: The park is nothing to write home about, but the view of the city and ocean are incredible. The parking is limited so be patient.
Spectacular view of Santa Barbara and the beach and hopefully Oprah's house was near by and I got her good juju vibes!!

This park, though very small and not much grass, is a great park to see the great city of Santa Barbara from above.  On clear days, you can see as far as Pt. Mugu all the way to the Santa Barbara Airport.  Definitely a great place to have lunch or get away from the hustle and bustle of city life to read and/or relax.

This is one of the best places in Santa Barbara for great views of the city and coastline.  I'm surprised it wasn't already listed in Yelp.  I guess that's probably why we usually only see locals visiting. ;)

I've come here during the daytime and before sunset, and both times the view of the city and the ocean was fantastic. It's a perfect place for a picnic date, which was what I did with my boyfriend. There are picnic tables you can use, but not really any grass area. It's pretty small, but it's a nice place to just relax and enjoy the scenery.

When I close my eyes to find my "happy place"  this is IT. 


I've seen countless sunsets but none compare to this. 


My favorite memories are here with close friends and family :) 


If you want to have a romantic spot that's fun and free LOOK NO FURTHER


Bottle of wine, a candle and some hors devours should do the trick ;)


The view of sb is amazing- cannot stress it enough!


Favorite place to watch the sunset. Enough said.
ahhh santa barbaras hidden gem. most beautiful views!

I find myself coming here when I need to just relax... It's Not really a "dog park" but definitely a beautiful place to go enjoy the scenery. Probably one of my favorite little gems hidden in Santa Barbara.

This city park is a great spot to see a panoramic view of Santa Barbara!  Franceschi House is also on site.  The park is small but has some walking paths that take you around the park.  It does not have traditional park grass, but the awesome view is worth a quick stop!

A small, somewhat hidden away park up in the hills of Santa Barbara.

My friend and I somehow stumbled upon this park while driving up the hills one summer day. And boy did we discover a gem of a park. The view from the park was insanely beautiful. We sat on the bench for a while, speechless from the awe inspiring view of the city, coastline, and the ocean. It was a sight I'll never forget.

Worth 5 stars on the view alone and definitely worth a stop if you're in Santa Barbara.

Beautiful place, if you love Santa Barbara, Old homes, the view of the ocean, small parks off the beaten path, old history, this is your park! I spent a lot of time scouring the internet after visiting to learn of the people who lived here and their story (which is fascinating!) so sad to see the condition of the home. Wish it was being preserved, but its still a must see to stroll the grounds, take pictures, walk the paths, and also there is a statue in carved rock of Franceschi you need to hike over to, not to far from the house. Lovely place.

This is certainly a hidden gem for a city park.  It is a small park but the views (ocean & city) will make up for any gripe you may have once you're here sulking in the view and enjoying the day.  
We drove up here because I heard this is THE place to be to have a quiet picnic  with the girlfriend on her birthday.  I was not disappointed.  We had the entire place to ourselves for almost an hour so she was happy for our little moment in time.

This Park is unique and corky! It is not the typical park set in Santa Barbara but it is truly a gem. This place has so much extraordinary history and charm I hope the city keeps this place open to the public.

This isn't a park, it's a photo op -- but it IS a 5-star photo op.

Drive up into the hills above Santa Barbara and you have extraordinary views of the town, the surrounding country, and the ocean. But it's a maze of private roads and mansions, so there isn't anywhere to enjoy the view.

That's where Franceschi Park comes in. It's a lovely place to take in your surroundings, but it's tiny -- just large enough to sit and enjoy the view, or to pose for a fabulous photo. There's no playground, and not even enough space for a decent walk.

So this is a sitting or standing park, not a get-out-and-play park. But it's well worth a visit, because there's no better view in town.

Just a warning that gates are closed right now during shelter in place . Sad.
The drive up was pretty scenic & I wish we could've seen Franeschi Park. Alas, had to turn around on the windy road but had a nice quiet if less scenic picnic at Orpet park nearby.
Amazing view! Great for lunch except when the d bags show up to blast their crap music and smoke j's

As others have mentioned, it's more of a viewpoint of the city than a park, but it's still worth a visit. There is an old house on the grounds as well that could make for a bit of a spooky backdrop. Great views with a few benches to sit and take it all in.

This park has an amazing view of Santa Barbara. It's fun to walk around and explore the outside of the old house that is located on the property. It has a few picnic tables for you to enjoy a meal while taking in the majestic view. Just be sure to clean up and keep this place beautiful!

Beautiful view of Santa Barbara but do know that the park itself is very small and probably consisted of four benches total in a much smaller area than I would consider a regular park

Also, I missed the entrance to this park on the drive about three times!! And No, it's not because I am an Asian female driver O_o On mission ridge, they have you follow a sign that points up this hill so I take the left to get up there. Once I'm there, there are three pathways! My GPS is totally confused and says I'm already there (which I'm not!) So first I drive straight up - nope. Then I wrap around and then take the middle lane - nope. Wrapped around again, finally! The third time I saw some (small and totally unobvious) signs that may be directing toward a parking lot. Voila! The Asian female makes it to the park! Hah Still a good stop if you're every in town though :)

One of my favorite parks in the world.

It's a small place with a beautiful view.  It's a bit of a drive up in the Riviera above where most tourists venture.  There aren't even a lot of locals that frequent this park.  I am happy to sit here alone.

I've never seen the park crowded.  I hope it stays that way.  
There is small, old restroom but it is generally clean and well kept.

The main reason to visit is the view.  

The old Franceschi House is on the property but it is dilapidated and boarded up.  It must have been a grand residence back in today.  After all of these years, I'm not sure if anything will happen with the house or if it will just slowly deteriorate and crumble.  I kind of enjoy hanging around beautiful, abandoned places though.

Super nice viewpoint in SB. The drive up is about 10 minutes from State Street (although a bit steep and windy) but not too difficult. Offers an amazing view of Santa Barbara and has picnic tables and bathrooms as well.

My mom is OBSESSED w/ this park. She stumble upon it with my sister one day as they were driving around during quarantine. She lives in LA but wants to drive up here to celebrate Mother's Day! 

Sad that the house is dilapidated. 

Had men and women's bathrooms with TP and a sink!

Two picnic benches.  Trash cans and recycling. And parking. 

Great panoramic view of SB.

Less of a park, more of a viewpoint nestled in a residential area. There are a couple of benches to sit on. But worth the windy drive up because you get a gorgeous look of Santa Barbara, mountains, ocean & everything in between.

This small park is rather run down. Paths are rough, some go nowhere, stairs lack railings, and the Franceschi house looks derelict. Perhaps the public works people have decided to spend their upkeep money elsewhere.

There is very little of botanical interest here. The Santa Barbara botanic gardens nearby are not free, but they are terrific. There are free botanic gardens at nearby colleges, and a very nice and free nursery and botanic gardens south of town, just inland from the Santa Claus Lane exit off Highway 5.

And, yes, the view is nice from the 2 picnic tables.
One of the coolest off the beaten path places in SB.  Great place to have a bag lunch or just sit and read a book while enjoying the surroundings.  View is breathtaking!

Some of Santa Barbara's best panoramic views!

Franceschi Park's layout is a tad confusing, but the views certainly make up for it. As others have mentioned, it's more of a place to stand and take in the setting versus a park to stroll around in. It is on a hill and tiers down a few blocks, between Franceschi Road and Dover Lane with Mission Ridge Road cutting through the middle. 

I'd initially accessed it at the top, from Franceschi Road, and was a little confused as there was a walking path that sort of turned into a steep hill with no destination. Luckily my shoes had some good tread on them! The park itself is ancient and historic, with the rundown Franceschi House as the main focal point. There are some neat artifacts surrounding the house and it is fun to walk around and admire. I'm sure it was a great spot in its heyday. 

The best part of the park, in my opinion, is the lower "Franceschi Steps" tier. This section has a few neat stone stairwells along with some paved walking paths. Walking up or down affords prime views of both the ocean and the riviera. It's very serene with little road traffic. 

If you are looking to be impressed by some serious views, give this park a visit - but be sure not to enter from upper Franceschi Road unless you've got some good all-terrain shoes!

name: Elings Park
formatted_address: 1298 Las Positas Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105, USA

google_reviews.text:
Incredible nonprofit park.  No Government funds here.. The park includes a member only Dog access to run free without a leash. Drinking fountains for dogs and Humans. Soccer fields  baseball and softball fields ,Frisbee golf, para gliding, hang gliding,down hill mountain biking,BMX track,Tennis,hiking an much more
Absolutely the best nonprofit profit Park in the World. It's so awesome I am going to donate
Beautiful views. Mountain trail terrain. A little challenging finding the layout. But a short challenging course. Give it a shot.
Fantastic find when last visiting Santa Barbara. Lots of easy little trails and great views

Job well done.
Planning for the future with Native plants from the San Diego area.
If I lived here I would definitely volunteer.
All the best.

I highly recommend Ellings Park to anyone around the Santa Barbara area!

I’ve had the pleasure of attending the Fields of Funk Music Festival twice at Ellings Park, and both years were fantastic! The outdoor stage setup is perfect for a music festival, creating an awesome atmosphere surrounded by nature. The park itself is incredible, with scenic hiking trails that lead up and around the mountain. On a clear day, you can spot the Channel Islands across the Pacific, including Santa Cruz Island to the south and Santa Barbara nestled to the north. There are sandstone formations, palm trees, and stunning homes built into the hills. You’ll likely encounter a steady flow of hikers, bikers, and even paragliders, which adds to the park's lively vibe. All around great vibes.

yelp_reviews.text:
Just had our Wedding here Saturday November 3rd at the Singleton Pavilion - was absolutely perfect. 

We booked an "off season" wedding (Nov-Feb) so got the venue for about 1/4 the normal cost, the view was just amazing. Lush beautiful trees and well kept lawns made for the perfect day. 
They had grounds keepers there the morning of to clean the restrooms and clean the grounds, also had parking attendants there to guide our guests to our venue. 

If you do book Singleton versus their much larger venue Godric Grove the only major downside is the parking situation - you're guests have to walk quite a ways to get to your venue. And, besides electrical outlets and a sink - its pretty bare. No lighting anywhere so you need to be prepared to have all of the basic things covered. 

The staff was perfect with communication during the booking process and called to confirm everything 1 week prior. 

Very, very happy with this venue. Absolutely gorgeous!

My HUSBAND and I got married here last week and it was a great experience. We only held our ceremony in Godric Grove, so I can't speak on if there would be any issues with the reception and complaints from people in the surrounding area as other reviews have mentioned. We had our rehearsal for the wedding very early in the morning and there were lots of people hanging out around the grove and asking if it was okay to be there. I didn't mind, but I found it interesting that it said wedding parking only at the bottom of the park, yet people still went through. The ceremony cite was perfect for our small group. It was a perfect day and perfect for the intimate ceremony that we wanted and we could see the ocean from the hill. It was perfect and I would recommend it to anyone who is looking to get married in a lovely, quiet place in California overlooking the ocean. Perfect.

I recently found out that it's pronounced "E-lings", but if you say "L-ings" or "Las Positas Park" people will know what you are talking about. Technically this is a private park but that just means if you want to rent it out for an event you have to pay more. Otherwise it's a wonderful park with 2 baseball fields, 2 soccer fields a small outdoor theater that people like to have weddings at and even an RC Car race track. Spread out over a terraced hills this park is a great place to take the kids or play some frisbee. It even has a nice little trail at the top with some great views.

For dog owners they have off leash licenses that last for one calendar year and aren't too expensive. Go early though, I hear they sell out pretty quickly every year.

They also have an area to launch your paraglider from. I'm not sure if they rent them or not but it sure looks like fun. You can glide out over the wilcox property and hendry's beach for some great updrafts.

This is your park if you're seeking a relaxing and quiet place to sit, contemplate and take in some Santa Barbara views. On a spring Friday afternoon, Elings Park had a few baseball games going on but parking still plentiful. If you drive up towards the left there is parking that takes you to Godric Grove, the amphitheater and the Veteran's memorial walk. This park is made up of multiple terraces and from the top you can view the mountains and on a clear day all the way out to the beach. Lots of flowers in bloom and birds to watch, Elings Park is a small local outdoor oasis. There are a few paths to walk but not any serious hiking. This is the type of place to go to just be outside and soak in the sun. The grounds are well kept and the park is dog friendly. There were signs saying there is a parking fee on weekends but weekdays are free. After spending 5 hours in the car driving down to Santa Barbara this was a low key and lovely way to stretch my legs and take in some nature.

I actually used the park for our wedding ceremony and OMG, it was beautiful. We got married in the Godic Grove area in the amphitheater. It overlooks the city and the ocean so perfectly. I cannot imagine a pretty, nicer, more amazing spot to get married. The acoustics were good too so we didn't even have to use a microphone or anything. I rented the area our for 4 or 5 hours (i can't remember) and we got that whole area, plus the parking lot, and 1 hour of rehearsal time on an earlier day for $900 in July. That is a great deal for Santa Barbara. Love it!

Great park to bring the family! There are many activities for everyone to have a great time. This park is huge! Bring the dogs too! Great weather and beautiful scenery. The beach is less then a 5 minute drive. The people are very nice, I will be sure to return soon! Thank you!!

We had our wedding at Elings and it was absolutely wonderful.  We had the ceremony in the ampitheater and the reception on the Godric's Grove deck.  We received so many compliments - everyone wanted to know how we found the place.  The park staff were very easy to work with throughout the process.  Our only complaint was that the outdoor lights didn't come on once it got dark so the bathrooms were pitch black for our guests.  Luckily we gave out flashlights as favors.  Even with dark bathrooms, we'd do it all again.

Godric Grove at the summit of the park is an absolutely beautiful place to have a wedding or special ceremony. There is a hardwood patio area, grass area and amphitheater with naturally shaded seating (although prepare to keep things short or bake if you plan to be the one on stage during the summer months). 

Gorgeous views of the city, East Beach and the ocean. There is even a public bathroom with flushing toilets and running water sinks.

The only drawback of the venue, as with any outdoor venue, are the creepy, crawly critters. As with most all of Santa Barbara this year, the ants are vicious and unrelenting so be sure to have a plan for tight storage if you plan to bring food that will not be eaten immediately. There are also HUGE spiders that come out at night and lay huge 6 foot webs to end near unsuspecting guests and staff. 

Some guests have mentioned the noise level issue, but as long as you have a DJ who has some pre-warning they can easily get a device to measure the noise level and forego any complaints and monetary sanctions. 

Overall, a very pleasant place. I will certainly return to picnic, watch the sunset or maybe even have my own wedding there one day . :)

Not great for "out of towners" passing through. Don't expect to find a "dog park" experience. All dogs in the park must pay a $125 membership fee, and there are no visitors passes or one time passes that we could see. The south part of the park is more open, and the views are amazing, but the access road is dirt and there are no designated places for dogs to play that we could find.

We got married at Godric Grove the last weekend of September. We met at UCSB, and Elings Park definitely brought us back to our Santa Barbara days. We fell in love with the venue the first time we saw pictures online, and it is even more gorgeous in person. Christina was really friendly and very responsive to all of our questions and requests. All of our guests loved the venue, and were blown away by the beauty of the park. It was nice to have pre-defined spaces for different events throughout the night (amphitheater for the ceremony, grass for the cocktail hour, deck for the reception). We did not want to over decorate the venue, so that our guests could enjoy the natural beauty of the park. A few simple centerpieces and string lights did the trick! Plus, getting married in the amphitheater means that all of your guests have a great view!

The only negatives of the venue are kind of personal preference issues. We were fine with using the bathroom at the park, but needed to bring in extra bathrooms because we had a lot of guests. It's a park, so the amphitheater might not be an ideal location for someone who wants the ceremony location to be pristine and dust-free. It can get a little windy at night, but that is pretty much true for all of Santa Barbara. Overall, I am so happy that we got married at Godric Gove!

This place is amazing for any event. We visited this place as a potential wedding venue and just fell in love with the amazing views and great price point. You really get what you pay for. Unfortunately we decided to have an indoor wedding, otherwise this would have been the one. Randy was the one who gave us a tour and he was just so nice and helpful. I highly recommend you visit his place if you are considering an outdoor, park venue.
Such an incredible park. We use it as often as we can for mountain biking, trail running, dog walking and date night picnics.

A breathtaking view of the city and beach in the far off distance! My friend had an absolutely beautiful wedding in the park, and a fabulous reception to follow steps away on their quaint, shaded deck. 

The staff is extremely accommodating--even after a change of the wedding date to a weekend, they allowed the bride and groom to purchase the place for the original weekday price. 

Even though it's extremely quaint and apart from the city, there's still a sense of liveliness at Elings. When we went up there, there was a motocross event happening just below the hill. 

Beautiful place for a wedding or major event. Very romantic.

A hidden jem of a park. For those lucky enough to live in the neighborhood Elings is  a resource we access again and again. A lovely place for a sunset stroll or picnic. The developed north side of the park has a path and many look out areas with city and ocean views. Hike up the short hill to the undeveloped south side of the park to enjoy rambling trails and ocean views. It's fun to sit and watch the paragliders take off from here and there are great mountain bike trails.

Beautifully epic hikes, great place to walk a dog, wonderful hiking, jogging and bicycling trails. I love to go here for full moon walks. There is also a BMX track, soccer and baseball fields, and a large open space for mountain bikes. At the top of the wild untrained hilltop is parasailing club for people who like a challenge. On the beautiful manicured hilltop, very beautiful lawn areas and barbecue areas, perfect for family gatherings or weddings. There is even a small amphitheater where you might see live music. One of my favorite views in Santa Barbara for watching sunrises and sunsets!

I attended a wedding reception here of around 90 people in August 2015 at Godric Grove. The ride up to the venue was bumpy, but the grounds themselves are gorgeous with some of the best views that I've ever seen anywhere in the world. I know some people mentioned some issues with loud noise restrictions, however, we did not seem to get complaints regarding  music played loudly all night. 

I really like the fact that with such a beautiful venue and minimal decorations are needed to make it seem festive. The park also had fairly decent bathrooms and I did not experience a huge wait in line to use them. Overall, I think this is a good venue to use, but I can see how it can be problematic for large weddings. As an out-of-towner, I did feel like I got the quintessential "Santa Barbara" experience attending a reception here.

We had our weddings in Godric Grove at Elings Park last summer, and we loved it! We dressed it up with some flowers and string lights and it was so beautiful. We had a lot of guests coming from out of town, and they all loved the venue. It was great how there were different spaces within the area (the amphitheater, lawn, and deck) because it made it more fun and it was nice to set up each portion of the wedding in a slightly different setting. We loved the venue and are so happy we chose it!

My wife and I were married in Godric Grove. We really enjoyed our wedding, and the park helped us with tours and vendor recommendations.
I am removing a star because of a few sticking points:
1. We were told that music volume would have to be very low after 10pm. They contacted us at 6 pm to lower the volume.
2. We cleaned up the morning g after which we were told was okay, we had asked the janitorial service for a broom which they told us they would give after they were done with the bathroom, but no broom appeared. I ended up sweeping with an old bouquet of  flowers.

I am incredibly biased, but I cannot imagine a more amazing place to get married. We chose Godric Grove at Elings Park after visiting seven or eight popular Santa Barbara/Montecito wedding venues and it was by far our favorite venue. Elings is really set up for events and if you're imagining an outdoor wedding - this is the place for you. For one thing, Santa Barbra generally has phenomenal weather, which Elings really allows you to take advantage of, and for another Elings has the best views of Santa Barbara that you could ever imagine. On one side you have the ocean and the city and on the other the mountains. In all the venues we visited we did not find a single other one that so classically captured what makes Santa Barbara such an idyllic place to get married. Working with the Elings staff was also very simple. You pay the balance in full to secure your wedding day and then you're largely done. The only thing you really need to follow up with them about is your DJ. They have pretty strict rules about sound because of the acoustics of being up so high and their surrounding neighbors. We had to put down a sound deposit because our DJ was not on their pre-approved list, but we had no problem with using him and we enjoyed a great dance party from appx. 7 PM - 10 PM with no issues. The only other regulation that might catch you off guard is the no open flame rule (i.e. no candle flames), but I think this is pretty common for outdoor venues since forest fires are such a concern in California. Aside from these minor nits, the venue lent itself perfectly for a wedding. There is an amphitheater that many couples use for their ceremony and the actual grove is great for cocktail hour, while we used the deck for dinner. It's really a great venue that maximizes space and allows you to have your guests flow around while staying centralized. The park also has great restroom facilities which means you don't have to deal with porta-potties like some outdoor venues. One of the great things about Elings is that you also get the venue for the whole day once you rent it so you really can get your money's worth by having your whole wedding at this one beautiful location. Overall I would say having an outdoor wedding here was one of the best decisions we made for our wedding, it really made the day.

Never realized how dog friendly Santa Barbara is.

The Grove has great views and plenty of grass & trees.

It is the "Largest Privately Funded Public Park in America."

I directed a summer camp (Nature and Games Camp) at Elings Park a few summers ago and greatly enjoyed my experience in this beautiful park! We used the great play structures, the little gazebo, the many green lawns, and sometimes the hiking trials. We always saw tons of cute lizards on our hikes but I heard there are rattlesnakes out there too which are much scarier!

I also checked out one of the movies in the park at Elings Park and had a great time. We didn't sleep over but enjoyed the movie on the big screen outside. I would definitely recommend checking out the events and camps at Ellings Park! The spot for weddings is gorgeous too with a fabulous view of Santa Barbara! Wow!

Prior to my wedding day, the lack of response from park personnel, and poor professionalism worried me. However, the ambiance on my wedding day was beautiful. There's no denying how great the view and trees make this place. I was asked to book my rehearsal time a month in advance (which meant they could still book a wedding the day before my wedding and I'd be SOL.) Thankfully this did not happen. I called the office out of frustration when not receiving email responses after some time to have a woman not apologize but to vent- telling me she is stressed and working 2 positions. You definitely need to be on them- call and confirm they have received paperwork like insurance, etc. I have to say this location is gorgeous but I would of had a horrible wedding day if it wasn't for my outside vendors who asked all the appropriate questions and were much more on the ball!

Luscious, picturesque park in the Mesa neighborhood of Santa Barbara -- doggie friendly, with baseball diamonds, park facilities, radio control vehicle course, etc. Totally a community place!

SITE TOUR
We toured the park for a wedding site. They showed us the Mercedes version first (of course) and then we proceeded to the Buick and then the Yugo. Well, what can I say, the Mercedes version captured our hearts -- Godric Grove is perched on a hillside, with a backdrop of lush trees and views of the ocean. The stage is in an amphitheatre which can easily hold a few hundred, though we only had about 80 guests. They're great on negotiating prices, so I encourage you to do so! For our rate, we were able to rent the entire site for 12 hours on a Sunday in May. Oh yes!!!

DECOR:
As for decoration, we were able to keep it minimal because of all the spring blooming flowers around us. We stuck to turquoise and orange which popped against the green and brown of the natural landscape (see photo!). 

DAY OF WEDDING
Onsite:
The Park Coordinator will give you a day coordinator's phone number who will help you in case of emergency. 

Vendors:
I highly recommend working with folks who have delivered to Elings before. I used Cliff & Co for catering and Crush Cupcakes. We used Event Rents who supplies extra tables. In May 2009, Godric Grove had these brown picnic rounds on the patio.. They were planning to pull them out but waited until our wedding to do so. It really helped to have the round tables in place, we only had to rent 2-3 tables to fit everyone!

Facilities:
There is NO place for you (bride, groom, gramps, gramma, etc)  to change around here. Come prepared to get hitched. There are two clean park restrooms onsite. They do have some noise restrictions for evening, but we had a day wedding and did not have to contend with that. 

PARKING:
Plenty. Just plain ole plenty, pull up the RV, for crying out loud.

GUEST HAPPINESS
Very happy. Folks got to make it a day at the park and this was a very unique location!  Bride was happy, groom was happy. There are ENDLESS amount of gorgeous photo opps around this park. 

We had an incredibly memorable wedding! It was so gorgeous and if you go at the perfect time of year, the weather will be just right and and all the flora and fauna singing your praises to boot.

I had my wedding at Godric Grove on September 2nd, and I don't have one complaint about it. I had to reserve my wedding venue sight unseen because I live out of state, but the pictures on their website and on another wedding website I viewed really gave an accurate impression of the actual space. I was so impressed by the views and natural beauty built into this venue. It helped us save a lot on flowers :) Also, the price was really reasonable relative to other venues I researched in southern California. The staff were very patient with all of my emails and questions leading up to the event, and handled all of my concerns beautifully. On my wedding day I got more compliments about the site than I did on anything else. I would recommend it to anyone who finds it within their budget!

This place is amazing! But the owner and the staff are super rude. I use to come here every single day to take my dog, i do not want or wanted to stop coming but just the vibe that the staff gives is pretty intense and unwelcoming to the public! I am an EPDOG member I had  to pay 150 for the whole year. I've seen so much drama in this place it's so weird! I don't understand why they don't give importance to the owners of EPDOG, I payed 150 for the year and I'm not allowed to go to certain parts of the park. because their renting the spot to people to use and they want to keep you out of that AREA! They will kick you out too! I'm so disappointed in the staff, because this is not nice! What is wrong with the world this days?

This review is only for the playground at Elings Park.

I took my 2 year old toddler on a Monday mid-morning. It was pretty deserted and the playground equipment seemed especially dirty and rusty. The playground was definitely made for older kids and was placed over gritty sand. There were 2 super squeaky swings, a few slides and just gave the overall impression of being unused and uncared-for. There was also lots of trash and empty beer cans littering the ground.

The setting was pretty and it was nice to see the mountains, but that's about it.

I would rather take my toddler to almost any other playground in the Santa Barbara/Goleta area.

Goodrich Grove was the perfect location for our wedding. It was easy to bring everything in and the staff was very helpful and didn't hover. The grass area was perfect for the waiting area- drinks and lawn games. The amphitheater was perfect for the ceremony. It gets warm directly in the sun and pictures did not turn out great due to all of the shadows. The wood patio was perfect for our reception. We had 140 guests and there was plenty of room. The only problem I had was that our DJ was a friend and played music at the same level all night. Right as the night ended the owners came in with a sound check and said that it was too loud and fined us. I'm not sure how it suddenly got louder. That was a bummer.

My daughter had her wedding and reception at Godric Grove in Elings Park in June. The venue is BEAUTIFUL! We brought in tables, caterer, lighting, dj, bartenders, and etc so it was a lot of work, but well worth it! The only reason for my 4 stars is that parking lot theft is a big problem. Three of our guests had their cars broken into and items were stolen. If I had known this I would have hired a security person for the parking lot. Signs are posted that warn of theft, but these are professionals that know how to jimmy the locks on cars. Everything else was just perfect!

We had a great time wandering around Elings one afternoon. We took the trail that headed toward the ocean and were treated to sweeping views of Santa Barbara on one side, and gorgeous views of the Pacific on the other. I enjoyed watching the paragliders fluffing their sails repeatedly and then floating down the hillside. It was an uphill trail but nice and flat at the top. The views of Santa Barbara are so incredible. You just feel like you're on top of the world. It was a great way to spend a couple of hours.

Elings Park is very big, with a number of different sport fields and a large parking lot.

If you're not coming here to attend a particular sports game, just follow the one lane road that goes up to the hill's top.  Once you're there you'll be treated to some lovely views of the ocean in one direction and Santa Barbara in the other.

This is a really nice park with lots of greenery in general and a number of great trees as well.  The park is a very popular place for weddings, so if there's one going on when you go here it might be very crowded.  That said, the last time I came here we had almost the entire park to ourselves :-)

As other reviews stated, it is a beautiful location. We were there for a baseball tournament. Each team paid $540 to play. 
Our game was scheduled to play late so the field lights were turned on in time to not delay the games. However, no light were ever turned on for the bathrooms and it was very dark and unsafe. The condition of playing field were disappointing. The outfield was rocky and the bases were not secure, leaving them moving as a player slid to it. 
Glad we went but would not go back.

Ocean views in all directions! Hike to the top or park off Flora Vista for an easier hike! Tons of open space and different trails. Plus plenty of Mountain Bike trails! 

Go around sunset and take in the breathtaking scenery! 

Earlier in the day you can see the paragliders practicing! 

For a special family treat, the open space is getting cleared by Goats, Sheep and Alpacas! So fun to watch them, but don't make the mistake of touching the electrified fence LOL! I was quite a shock! ;)

I can't believe this was my first visit to Elings Park but I finally made it. This park is right here in the city on the way to lots of restaurants, grocery stores etc.  You just have to take the time to stop. What made me finally stop? The park board has brought in around 200 sheep to clear out some non native grass that the sheep will eat and help clear so the native plants can grow. What a great park for wide open spaces, views and hiking. Climb the trails up and you have a fabulous ocean view. We took our six year old granddaughter to see the sheep. She loved it and was happy to hike up to be able to see them and the two white Great Pyrenees dogs that guard the sheep. There are plenty of sheep and plenty of new lambs to see right now. I will definitely be back to hike and explore some more.

I attended a wedding recently in Elings Park, in Godric Grove.  The venue is beautiful...don't get me wrong.  The problem are the people living around the venue.

The wedding party was told that they would lose $1000 dollars if people complained about the noise level.  At 7:30pm, 3 songs into the reception....we received the first complaint.  The music wasn't very loud at all.....my wife and I were near the speaker and having a regular conversation.  

We had to turn down the music for the rest of the night and no one danced...b/c you could barely hear it.  Was a huge disappointment for the bride and groom.......

With so many beautiful places in the surrounding area....I would highly recommend you stay away from Elings Park....unless you don't plan on having any music.  Or just don't hire a DJ.....just play it off your phone, b/c that's how loud it will be for a majority of the evening.

Read through other reviews....it's happened to a lot of people.  

At the end of the day....I think the people who complain at 730pm on a Saturday are just miserable.  Stay away!
If you hike to the top, it is a perfect spot to watch sunrise/sunset. Very dog friendly. Lots of dogs off leash.
This place is amazing... dogs and people. Weddings and concerts. Can't go wrong with this location!

I don't usually review all the great parks in town, but this is one of my favorites. Whether it is a gloomy Monday morning or a Saturday afternoon there is something to enjoy at Elings. We walk from our house and often times I see new parts of the park I never knew existed, even after like the 100th time. Careful to pay attention to the signs for operation hours as they will lock you in if you attempt to leave after closing, yes this is the one draw back. I literally had to climb a fence with my toddler to get out one night.

Highlights of the park include:

-hiking trails
-playgrounds
-baseball and softball areas
-bathrooms open when covid isn't a thing
-benches to picnic lunch, and 
-HAPPY PEOPLE and DOGS (with dog membership).

We had our ceremony at The South Bluffs and we could not be happier with the location!! WOW, what a view!!!  We knew that we wanted to be close to the ocean but not right on the beach. This spot was absolutely perfect. 

We used Jump on the School Bus to transport our guests to and from the ceremony site so there were no issues getting up / down the hill.

We definitely impressed all of our guests with how beautiful the view was!

Attended a niece's wedding here in June and although the venue was beautiful and location nice, the park's sales staff regularly changed the requirements just days before the wedding.  Location was beautiful but the logistics of staging a hassle free wedding aren't worth it.

One of the most gorgeous parks I've ever seen!! It's situated on a very large hill overlooking the ocean, shoreline, and mountains depending on where you stand. 

It's peaceful, removed, and incredibly well maintained. An excellent photo op location. The couple chose the Gordic Grove area to have their wedding -- 
the ceremony was held in a semi stadium like seating (outdoor) that overlooks the bay, shoreline. The cocktail area was on the large piece of open green land adjacent to the ceremony site. The best part? Gorgeous reception area (only a minute walk away) ...with mountain views. =) 

Only down side is being aware of wind advisories --- it gets SUPER windy around 5-8pm during sometimes in the year. Limited parking in the lot. Next time will have to return for fun, not work. :)

We are so fortunate to have this large park close to us. It has a lot of different activities. From soccer and softball fields to beautifully secluded areas for picnics and weddings. Some of the area is dog friendly but you need a special permit to bring a dog. If you go up to the top, the views overlook the city and are spectacular. There are also areas for bmx riding, paragliding and trails to hike. Great asset to our city.  They do charge for parking on the weekends.

Elings Park is one of the most beautiful spaces in Santa Barbara. 

I had come here countless times as a child: watching my father play league softball, watching my cousin race at the BMX tracks, Easter egg hunts, two weddings, enjoying the playground, and taking in the beauty of the many sections. This park is unique in that it's located on a hilly terrain and is sectioned off into several tiers. 

I've hosted three company picnics at Godric Grove and the process couldn't have gone smoother. The park staff were easy to work with and they were very accommodating with my various requests. This section of the park is downright gorgeous and is truly the ideal spot to host an event. 

Elings Park is also home to a great hiking trail which meanders through the neighboring wilderness. It's well-maintained and allows you to have a panoramic view of Santa Barbara when hiking it. 

I'm only subtracting a star because of the fact that they now require vehicles to pay. This was not always the case. I don't know of any other general county parks that require $5 per car. Maybe it's to curb vehicle-dwellers from spending the night? Either way, I park at the end of Valerio and enter through the back to avoid this unnecessary charge. 

All in all, a beautiful open space that should not be missed.

Elings Park is a hidden gem of which local residents may not even be aware or appreciate more. 

My BFF and I "discovered" the park since we stayed in an airbnb offering nearby and were seeking a safe jogging / running route. (Car traffic moves fairly fast on Las Posits Road.)

Unique amenities include a BMX track (plenty of dirt mounds) and the Veterans Memorial Walk. Visitors can drive uphill and park closer to the top, where views of Santa Barbara extend for miles! A thoughtfully planned sidewalk features plaques with the names of fallen soldiers from a variety of wars. Around the "top" we noticed some level lawn areas to picnic as well as terraced amphitheater-like settings. Several trees also had memorial plaques.

After we walked downhill toward the athletic fields, we "found" another trail near some gardens that led us up to the South Bluffs. Here, vegetation seemed less maintained but not unruly. We noted bicycle tire tracks in the dry earth and yards in front of us were three paragliding parachutes. The bluff terrain was more level than I imagined; parents with relatively young children were present. Plenty of space to socially distance. 

Note: We were able to enter and park for free on a weekday. When we returned on a Saturday, we had a $5 all-day fee. Lacrosse tournament attendees took up a majority of stalls, but enough parking spaces for those of unaffiliated.

GO AT SUNSET   currently the park closes at 7:30 sharp so keep that in mind when you're there in the evenings. Now that's it's getting darker a little earlier

If you are thereafter they close the gate you will be locked in! I would definitely go in the evening and walk the veteran's memorial. It's a beautiful path and tribute with flowers. 

There are a lot of events and sports games that happen here, so they do charge during peak hours but I usually go on a Friday afternoon or during the week.

google_reviews.text:
52 acres of open space. It's between freeway 101 and the Pacific Ocean. It has fields of wildflowers and native plants, eucalyptus grove of trees, walking paths that connect to the Carpinteria Bluffs  Trail, and access to a low tide beach and seal rookery. Since the preserve is on a cliff, the sunsets  are gorgeous. Catch the sun as it dips slowly into the ocean horizon. The colors melting into the ocean. Perfection. The access and parking to the preserve is free. FREE! You gotta luv that.

Since the preserve is on a bluff, the beach is elevated below. So accessing  the beach requires some trekking skills. Plus there are active live train tracks between the beach and preserve. Trains often run through. Always have your wits about you when you're near train tracks. And keep a considerable distance from the moving train. Next to the bluffs are ball fields with actual real turf. There are public  restrooms there. I can't offer any additional info on those, I didn't use them. Both the preserve and ball fields have their own parking lots.

Nice park just before you get to Carpenteria. Great quiet place to let the kids & dogs out of the car to run around a bit on a long drive. Clean bathrooms. Ample parking. You can take a nice walk down the path to the ocean.

A great place for a seaside stroll, and your dog is welcome too. A lot of trails through the coastal scrub brush and some nice stands of trees, although you have to carefully cross active railroad tracks to view the surf breaking below the cliffs. All in all, a very pleasant place to spend some time...
Beautiful and serene trails with vibrant wild flowers. Spotted seals and dolphins

So relaxing to walk the trails along the bluff and thanks to volunteers they’re well maintained!  No fee to park or walk. We were there during pup season so couldn’t walk the entire shore line but loved seeing the seals from the bluff!

yelp_reviews.text: This place is awesome! It's beautiful! There is a lot of parking and hiking trails which lead straight to the beach. We came here because of the hot dog stand. Recommend that place for a quick bite before heading to Santa Barbara.
Heaven on earth! This is a GORGEOUS place to walk your dogs or go for a jog. I especially love this place near sunset. Romantic and beautiful

The Carpinteria Bluffs Nature Preserve comes complete with astonishing panoramic views. Through December to March, you can spy on seals and sea lions at the seal rookery below the bluffs. It's a great place to bird watch, too! Once the sun rises on the bluffs, this place resonates peace and beauty until the sun goes down.  Ample parking.  Highly recommended.

I got up early morning wanting to fill my soul with the sights, smells, and sounds of nature--away from all the Memorial Day madness. 

So, I made my way to the Carpinteria Bluffs Nature Preserve. I checked out the area the day before since I knew this place could be easily missed. It's along the quiet section of Carpinteria Ave, where Bailard ends. It's really close to the 101 freeway. It has a modest medium sized dirt parking lot. 

What's nice is that once you're there, it's easy to forget that a freeway is close by. Actually, you can't even see it once in the park. 

I conveniently found parking in the dirt lot. Since I got there early, I had no problem finding a space.   

There are several dirt trails that meander throughout the preserve. There is tall foliage everywhere. Also, a little off to the right, there are plenty of eucalyptus trees that provide shade. It's all very nice. Plus, there are the sounds of birds such as the California towhee, the spotted towhee, as well as song sparrows singing their songs.  

Going further out, closer to the bluffs, you will find the AmTrak train tracks. 
Just on the other side of the tracks, there are several spots where a person can go down to the beach. If doing this, be careful for there are several naturally occurring tar pits! Very cool. :) 

It's a lovely area for a morning stroll or even an early evening stroll. If there are people around, they're mostly locals. 

It's good to know that I have discovered yet, another lovely area to enjoy nature. :)

Great place to relax and enjoy the view. This here leads to the Seal Sanctuary where you can see a bunch of seals lying on the beach doing nothing.

Besides the ocean, there is a train rail and a lot of green.

Good for walking dogs as well
This is a nice walk with toddlers and it is short as well. The beach is secluded and clean.  We really enjoyed our time and plan to return in the future

Brought the dogs here on a Friday afternoon in November. Weather was perfect, not too hot not too cold.

It's a good little place for a hike with views.

Cross the tracks, and head down the bluffs onto the shore for a stroll along the beach. That part is closed starting December 1st to end of May for the Sea Lion mating season.

It's a good place to let the dogs run free and play in the water. The waves aren't too crazy. It wasn't crowded, and the few people we meandered by were friendly.

Beautiful easy stroll. I wouldn't call it a hike at all but its a nice spot and dog friendly. We went on a very warm weekend in March to walk the beach and see the seals (the dog can't go to the seals though). It was crowded but that is to be expected. Perfect spot to pack a picnic and take in the view.

This was the absolute highlight of my trip to Carpinteria.  The hiking/walking trails are beautiful, and the views of the ocean and coastline are breathtaking.  It was exciting to be able to see the seals in the rookery when I was there in March.  

There's plenty of wildlife to watch while you're walking, and the trails are pretty well traveled, but well-kept.

The bluffs are a great place to go for a walk, jog, or just hangout and watch the scenery. They are beautiful all the time but they are especially beautiful in the spring when everything blooms! If you are in Carpinteria this is a must see spot.

This was so nice and relaxing! 
Easy hike. The train was cool and the views were awesome. We didn't see any seals but we did see snakes. Be careful !!

I'm sitting here with my dog on a beautiful day. It is so beautiful here and there's a hiking trail that brings you out alongside the ocean. You must keep your dog on a leash but they are very dog friendly and provide poop bags along your walk. A beautiful place to take pictures.

Nice little hike. 

After a few minutes of hiking, you will cross path with an Amtrak train track which is also a great spot to take pictures at.  The train does go on this track so be careful. 


Free parking.  No public bathrooms. 
A bunch of seals

This is a wonderful easy coastal walk for the entire family. You have great ocean views, the cool breeze makes it manageable even when walking at noon and you get to see a bit of nature with the lizards and birds. The seals were not there at the seal sanctuary but I don't really care for them; once you have witnessed how annoying they are at Monterey's wharf then you will understand. I didn't realize that you get to see the tar pit beach from the bluffs trail. So we didn't have to actually try and drive to it. We didn't walk the entire length of it, maybe 1 mile down and back until we were ready to hit the beach but I did see some people walk it from the trail head to Carpinteria State Beach to avoid that $10 parking fee.

Such a beautiful hike. You have different options-- you can walk down to Rincon, go to the seal preserve, walk along the railroad tracks, or down below by the water. Whenever we go, there are always so few people. It's peaceful and serene. The only complaint is that a deep inhale won't reveal the fresh smell of the ocean. On the contrary, at times, you'll get a whiff of oil from the nearby oil rigs.

One of the better places with a view that I've visited while I was here in Santa Barbara. 

Walk through here on the way to the Seal Sanctuary and you can see some pretty nice views. There's a train track, so it's a perfect place to capture some nice photos. Definitely a huge plus since one of the main points of my trip was to take some stunning photos.

What a beautiful find in the low key beach town of Carpinteria. Parking is free and ample with a sign showing a map of the trails before you embark on your coast side walk. I found myself on a flat walk along the bluffs with an array of colorful wildflowers this past April. Wonderful bird watching opportunities abound but the main attraction for me, that day, was a walk to the seal sanctuary. From the parking lot, head straight towards the water and then to the right where you cross over the railroad tracks and then eventually see the signage for the harbor seal sanctuary area. During certain times of the year the path down to that beach is closed but you can bring some binoculars and watch the mamas and babies from up above. 
There are other paths that you can walk down to the explore other areas of the beach below, if you're so inclined. Between long walks along the bluff trail, paths down to the sand, tide pools and the Seal Sanctuary, you can spend the better part of a day here. Friendly locals seem to use these trails and I am so happy to have found yet another gorgeous area to visit while around the Santa Barbara coast.

This is a nice hike, I"m not sure exactly where the trail gears off too the only sign of it is at the entrance.  I crossed the tracks and headed to the beach, they have a sign posted it's where the sea lions gather between December and May and they close the beach access off between those months.  You would be able to see the sea lions from the cliff with their pups.  The day I went hiking we just walked along the ocean as the little ones gathered their shells and we walked from one end to the only other way out and hiked back along the bluff from the parking lot (no sign posted)  the trail is surrounded by chrub.  It eventually goes along side a rail road track and we saw the train twice while hiking.  When we came back I saw people walking on the other side of the train track in a field which I thought was private property but I"m guessing where the trail head actually is.  Either way, both sides of the track is a nice hike.  It was empty when we went exploring the area. Saw some people hang gliding above us.   I loved the quietness and I only saw one jogger when I got to the train area.  The beach access itself is empty just a few joggers and at the parking lot entrance at the far end of the beach is where some people gathered but it's a nice quiet beach area over all.  Total distance we did was 6-7 miles.
This place was beautiful to venture during a quick stop on our way to SB. Nice walk through nature. Lots of trees. Beautiful beach. Perfect spot to see the .

Solid family friendly trails that leads to a natural tar pit beach. 

We wanted to stretch our legs before the long drive back down to San Diego. The Carpinteria Bluffs are right off the 101 and there's a reasonably sized dirt parking lot. The path along the bluffs is well traveled with several opportunities to cross train tracks and get right to the edge. 

From the dirt parking lot we went RIGHT (North) and we eventually were forced to cross train tracks.  That's where the small path is located that takes you down to the beach. Much of the path  that goes down to the beach is made from tar and looks like molten lava. If you're lucky it's low tide as you'll be able to climb around the tide-pools and see marine life. There's also sea glass here too, but alas also sticky tar. 

When we first walked along the rocks we noticed the distinct smell and saw all the sticky tar  of the rocks and pools of rainbow oil floating in the water ( see picture). I totally thought this was the remnants from a major oil spill but I later found out it's actually a natural tar pit ( Like the one in La Brea). Apparently there's a beach farther north called Tar Pt Beach but it's past the seal sanctuary.

- It's a good family friendly diversion from a long drive..
- There's also a seal rookery in this same area. Check the yelp page for exact directions.

I just learned about Carpinteria Bluffs a few weeks ago, and last weekend I decided to check it out. 
It's really easy to find, just go west on Bailard until it ends in the parking lot. There's a sign at the entrance, and a really easy dirt path that goes through trees and shrubs, and along the train tracks. 
I spotted many birds I don't usually see in Ventura .. that was a treat.  
There's also a seal sanctuary to one side, & I recommend taking a peek at those cuties for sure. 
Views of the ocean here are gorgeous. You can cross the tracks for better up close views. 
When I went it was a bit busy, so I'd recommend going during the week, earlier in the day if possible. 
No need for a pack or water, it's a very easy stroll, mainly giving you fantastic ocean views.
A beautiful walking trail in Carpinteria. Really peaceful and calm. Take the trail to overlook the Seal Sanctuary for some fun seal watching. We had a lovely afternoon walk.
My daughters and I went for a walk here to see the seals. We followed the driving directions from Yelp. There are clean restrooms at this parking lot. It was a nice walk to the seal sanctuary.

This is a beautiful hike that leads down to the bluffs and tidal pools. When the tide is out, it exposes small tidal pools and rocks that are covered in algae. There isn't much sealife but the algae on the rocks look very pretty in pictures. If your lucky, you may find some small sea creatures here and there. Just be careful where you step. 

Yelp 100 Challenge (31 out of 100)

I walk the Carpinteria bluffs almost every day! It is such a great spot.  Very unspoiled with miles of trails.  It is never too crowded.  I try and pick a new route every day to get different views.  

You can see snakes, but they are not poisonous.  They look like rattle snakes but they are actually gopher snakes.  Gross, but not dangerous. 

I recommend parking at Viola Fields for a safe place to park and a public restroom.

One of the reasons why I love living in Southern California. Even though I didn't come during the mating season, I saw some baby seals and other adult seals in the water and on the beach.  Came here on a Fourth of July weekend and it was such a fun day. 

There is plenty of free parking and the hike to the lookout point to see the seals is very easy, kid friendly and without any steepness. Beautiful nature views and makes you really appreciate the awe that surrounds us in this beautiful state. It's the perfect stop if you are driving to Santa Barbara.

Staying in Carpinteria for a few days and looking for some places to go to for walks and hikes. This place came up on yelp so we thought to go see it as it was of very close proximity and walking distance from where we are staying at. 

What a beautiful place it is. People complain in the reviews that it is not a real hiking spot. People should learn the difference between hiking and walking. 

Walking constitutes more of a simple and easier surfaces like the flat that you experience going to the bluffs. While when you hike, you need to go out and experience a more rugged terrain, exert more effort (uphills/downhills, rocks, dirt, gravel etc.)

The Carpenteria Bluff Nature Preserve is flat , paved and dirt combination and can be accessed from different trailheads like the Tarpit Park. 

There's so much to see like wildlife, flowers and views are amazing. There are many trail loops that people can go on for walking or running and these are all family friendly. There's a railroad track so be careful when crossing. 

The bluffs also leads you to the Seal Sanctuary. Many people will be there so maintain social distance and keep your masks on. There are days when you don't see the seals. That's ok. there are others things to do. Go down to the beach and take a stroll! I can't say enough about Carpinteria bluffs , the experience was one of the most memorable.

I don't know if a lot of people use yelp to find hikes and beaches but, it lead me to find Carpinteria Bluffs. It's an easy couple mile hike on the coastal cliffs, and there is easy access to get to the beach. I went on a Monday, but it was really quiet and not crowded at all. There was free parking at the trailhead till the sun goes down, and dogs are allowed with a leash. The only reason I gave it 4 stars instead of 5 is because I was a little bummed I didn't see any seals near the seal sanctuary- maybe they aren't around during this time of year. Regardless, this is my new favorite beach.

google_reviews.text: I was visiting a friend in Goleta, the walk from her place to the Goleta pier and the beach was literally a short 15 to 20 minute walk. Being from Utah, i just couldnt get enough of the beach. I would walk to the beach every morning nearly, and I found some pretty cool shells. The water was very relaxing. My first real experience with the Ocean.  I really can't wait to go back. I'll post some pics from my visit and the shells i found. Possibly even found a couple teeth. Not 100% tho

Nice spot to have a walk. Sea is great, wiev ia fantastic. Sitting by the sea and listening the sound of waves incredible. You shoul visit early in the morning. Take your coffee and be there Lots of parking lot.

Goleta Beach Park is such a peaceful and beautiful place. The beach is quiet, not too crowded, and has a very relaxed atmosphere that makes you want to stay longer and come back again. The views of the ocean and mountains are stunning, especially around sunset, and the long pier and grassy picnic areas make it great for walks, photos, or just sitting and listening to the waves. It feels like a little hidden gem where you can truly rest and enjoy the beauty of the California coast.
Very nice quite beach with a long pier for fishing . And a great restaurant called The Elwood, had an amazing bar man called Frisky

The restroom not clean. The beach not clean. But there was play ground for the kids and u have to make reservations for the spot in holiday. Good spot for fishing 🎣 . There’s bike lane and spacious parking lot. 🅿️

yelp_reviews.text: Made a quick pit stop while visiting UCSB as a way of staying cool. It was in the 90s this day. There's a grassy area where people found shade or hung their hammock from some palm trees. I chose to sit on a bench next to the restaurant atop a small little mound and read a book until it got a little too hot. People sunbathed, walked the pier nearby and waded in the ocean waves. There was plenty of free parking, but the water didn't look too clean so I opted to not go in. Overall, it's a nice little stretch of beach to cool off, people watch, or read a book.
Love this beach! I've been coming here since I was a kid! It's never too crowded and it's very family friendly!

Great Beach! Rarely crowded. Bathrooms, large grassy areas with bbq facilities. Trees on the grass plus an amazing view of the islands. Lots of walking room. Perfect place to enjoy a late afternoon picnic. There is a restaurant on site as well as a snack bar at the foot of the long pier.  I visit every time I am in town to visit my UC student. Love it there!!!

This is for the Goleta pier 
Fishing here is great have bathrooms at beginning of pier bait shack on piers opens at 12
Big Enough that even with a lot of people fishing as plenty of room need two pole here light tackle for 
Small minke and bait fish then use the mm for the Bigger catch with heavier pies and or surf rods ....
All the fishermen were nice and helpful ...
Not much of an area to sun. But if you're looking to get away from the crowd and stroll along the pier or enjoy a park bench then this is your place. Plus plenty of park picnic tables to enjoy a BBQ.

Love running to this beach from my apartment in IV! This place offers a great BBQ area, pretty clean and well maintained grass area and is located just outside of UCSB and right next to Beachside cafe. Only quip is that it's quite expensive to reserve a BBQ pit area

Nice beach with a great pier to fish on. But at the moment the water is no good.  Ever since the oil spill,  all you get is tar covered feet and some nasty water to wade in.  The waves are very calm but still it's hard to do anything since the oil spill.  Don't let your kids play in it,  you never know what they might get.

One of my favorite places to frequent. I am fortunate enough to be able to come here almost every day on my lunch break. Super mellow and not too overcrowded. Big pier to walk out onto. It does get a little busier in the afternoons/evenings in the summer but its California what place isn't crowded. Free parking is also a plus.

Goleta Beach is a nice spot for enjoying the ocean without enjoying the beach. There are lots of trees and grassy areas to lay out a blanket and enjoy the sea view. The beach itself is not the most inviting...there was a ridiculous amount of seaweed bundles and kelp all over the place, and it was so rocky that I didn't want to walk barefoot. But once I spread out my beach towels on the grass and just enjoyed the sound of the waves, I didn't mind that my feet weren't freezing in the water.

Nice perk - it's a free beach.
This park is beautiful and the best place to go running.  Either in the sand or grass.  Great for familys and BBQs.  And it's still FREE to park and go here.

We also gave Butterfly Beach four stars, but Goleta Beach gets four stars for different reasons.  This beach is still very beautiful, there is a view of the cliffs to the left and of UCSB to the right.  The sand and water is decently clean, but Butterfly Beach is much better when it comes to cleanliness.  We love Goleta Beach because of the easy access to beachside barbequing, since they have many picnic tables and grills.  Although especially in the summer months, most of these grills are taken by noon on the weekends.  The parking at this beach is another plus (free!).  This is a great beach to run to, as many paths lead to Goleta Beach.

Overall, great beach for barbequing, running, and laying in the sand, but we'd probably stay away from taking a swim in the ocean.
Go to Bagel Place on Trigo then walk over to the Goleta Pier.  It is totally worth it.  I know most IV residents are sleeping til noon but it is really worth coming here.

This beach was perfect for an afternoon picnic.  Easy to get to, free and lots of parking.  Restrooms were sparkling clean.  Not over crowded.  Very relaxing with nice views and you get to watch the planes as well.
A lot of rocks with little sand. Easy parking. The kids had a fun time finding near rocks but we weren't able to swim.

This review is just for the beach/pier side, as the park looked like an exciting place but I didn't check it out personally. 

The pier is really long and sort of worn down, but it goes really far out into the ocean. There's this little staircase at one point that sorta goes down into a smaller offshoot of the pier. We couldn't figure out what it was for, but it's cool to check out. It was also really windy when we were there so it was really cold, even though the sun was out and the sky was nice and everything. It was actually so windy that you couldn't drink from the water fountains respectably as the wind was literally blowing the water off the spout onto the railing once you pressed the button. However, it's a pretty chill and relaxing place (minus the roaring wind when we were there), and you can see the cliffs up and down the beach especially walking all the way out into the pier. 

Perfect place to just hang out and waste time on a lazy afternoon!

This is a beautiful beach.   It is well kept and the trees are just now getting large enough to provide a fair amount of shade on a hot summer day.  The ocean landscape is second to none.  The waves there are child friendly, there are BBQ facilities up and down the park, a large party area with large BBQ pit, children's park, the bathrooms are somewhat clean, and don't forget the Beach-side Restaurant for those who don't like the park scene.  A great area to spend an hour or two when your friends and family from out of town come to visit.

Easy to get to location fron the airport. Also this beach is dog on leash friendly which is nice.  Picnic tables that overlook the Pacific, you can't beat that. Check out the beachside restaurant if you have a chance.
Much nicer then the beaches at IV. I prefer to come here for tanning or studying. Its right next to the pier as well which is fun to walk.  Its a nice bike ride from iv.

This beach is beautiful! We took a bike ride from UCSB and then rode down here and stopped for a Picnic lunch. We threw some bread at the birds and they were crawling back to us. Great place to take a Panoramic Photo (New photo feature on iPhone 4 or 4S+) & Family Photos. 
*Pro Yelp Tip*: Rent some bikes and go on a short bike ride toward the beach. Pack a Picnic lunch & some board games. Go swimming! Take Family Photos! Have a blast ;)

1st place we hit in the am on a heatwave day (YESTERDAY). it was dead except for the pier, where a few fishermen were at. Perhaps the emptiness was due to a sign that noted that the water was contaminated and that it was UNSAFE to swim in the water. I guess by walking along the water and gettin gour feet wet, we may have put ourselves at risk for getting an infection. After 15m, we decided to try another beach. There is a restaurant right at the Pier, but it was still early, so it wasn't open. They are open for lunch and dinner.
Lotsa beautiful scenery!!!!!!!! Very long pier... and long is good, right?! :)

This is a small beach park, ideal for picnics or fun get-togethers. There are nice picnic benches with grills and there is even a spacious communal picnic area that is shaded for large groups. Spots can be reserved for parties. 

As for me, I like to come here and chill with a sandwich but the resident sea-gulls seem to have the same idea about my lunch. Please don't feed them. They will come in hoards to fight for your crumbs.

Aside from the bird issue, it's a cool spot. You really can't go wrong with SB beaches.

Seriously gorgeous. There's a really nice grassy area right before the beach with rows and rows of palm trees and free parking. The sand is pretty soft but it's got a lot of kelp and plant shards in the sand. There's a restaurant with great views too, but I would skip it for a picnic. The beach has lighted volleyball courts and kayak rentals. 

As you get to the wetter area there's a lot of jumpy water fleas and translucent shrimp, which is really cool for kids. We saw a couple of surfers and paddleboarders but overall it doesn't seem like a place for anything other than wading or swimming - the water is super kelpy and there doesn't seem to be much in the way of waves. 

The pier is super fun - on a typical day there'll be a lot of people fishing off the side, and it seems to be overrun with some pretty audacious pelicans too. Really pleasant walk. Sadly the beach faces south so you can't really see the sunset over the water, but you can watch the sun set through the palm trees which is really gorgeous.

What a nice park!  Right on the beach, plenty of things for all ages...not too crowded, even on weekends.  The pier is awesome, and a fabulous place to watch sunset or sunrise.  I understand the reason why, but they close down parking at sunset to sunrise in the actual park.  Also - right on the bike path - another bonus!

Cool beach park but there was no swimming due to some red algae infestation, no matter we just came to look at the ocean.  Cool long wooden pier, you see the ocean through the beams, pretty cool. The pier was filled with people and families fishing for the day.  Saw a family that caught a couple of rays and the kids where super excited about it, telling us about their last catch a huge "600 pounder", their dad said it was more like a hundred but a monster never the less.  Half way through there is a bait station and you can actually rent some poles, lures and buy bait for a day trip.  We watched the beautiful views and the pelicans diving for dinner, great place to take a nice stroll.
Considering coming back here in our last day and do some fishing.

The best took the boy&I here when we visited her because well... who goes down to Santa Barbara/Goleta without going to the beach?! We went twice in two days, and both times, there was ample space [no squished sunbathing for me!]. I didn't get in the water [besides dipping my foot in] because well... I know it's SoCal, but the water is still wayy too cold for me! It seems to attract a wide variety of people [from families to college kids]. It was also really nice&clean, which I appreciated. I'd definitely come back the next time I'm in the area!
Great beach.  Went there yesterday with the kids. My 7 year old swam to the buoy with my nephew who is 18.  No rocks, family waves.

A nice little beach not far from UCSB up the road. We used to run to the pier from campus and it was a fun run to do because of the view of the ocean. There are picnic tables and grassy areas for gatherings. The beach is generally quite small but I've found it to be clean. Parking generally isn't too difficult because there is a proper lot.
Great beach for relaxing.  Lots of seaweed on the sand, which brings flies.   People BBQing...  lots of fun!  Dogs can't be off-leash though.

Well kept with rangers driving around to enforce rules. Beautiful views with a long pier that isn't safe for toddlers but nice otherwise. Ample and free parking but very narrow beach with very rocky sand. So not great for the kids to build sandcastles but there is a playground that my toddler loved. This beach isn't a must stop but if you need somewhere to stop to stretch your legs it's a good spot.

Nice beach. Pretty quiet while we were visiting.
Free parking.
The wave was pretty calm, great for paddle boarding but not body boarding.
The beach got some seaweeds (not too bad), got some rocks (my son's toes hit one.. ouch!), and the worst thing: found broken glass!!!! I saw another visitor found broken glass as well.
Maybe the college students party spot? Hope people are more responsible to keep our beaches clean!

I really love this beach. It's a people beach with lots of picnic tables and barbecues and even a restaurant, but it still feels clean and natural. The goleta slough empties here and it can be a great place to see birds. One time I saw about 20 great blue herons hanging out at the slough's mouth.  There are also lots of cormorants and egrets. And sometimes pelicans.

One of my new favorite beaches. It doesn't have a lot of things around it. Maybe one restaurant and very close to UCSB. Has good parking and just enough tables for some family picnic. 

During our stay in SB, we came and visited this beach twice. The first time, it was low tide and we walked the beach for almost 2 hours! It was a weekend around 5PM, not crowded at all and the further you walked east, it becomes slightly secluded. I guess it's connected to either beach houses or hotels, idk. But just being there with no annoying kids, couples or teenagers made it perfect for some relaxing, thinking - whatever. They also have these odd black rocks just sitting by the beach which makes for good photo op. If you're into that stuff. Lol! 

This is definitely one of my go to beach if I were ever in the area again and recommend the place to everyone :)

There's a lovely LONG pier that has wonderful views. Off to the left you have kinda like flat lands and then to the right you see UCSB. Quiet beach, nice amount of parking although right now their doing some construction so (the beach) is kinda blocked off. 
I'm sure if u wanted to touch the water or take a couple pictures you can walk through the barricade..

Bike paths everywhere so you can run/bike/walk. Nice place to swim, kayak boat whatever your plea

Very dirty but the views are nice.  I went here with some friends and we had a good visit.  I recommend going in the evening so you can watch the sun set.  There are lots of birds but they will leave you alone if you don't feed them.  There are some very nice walks along the lagoon you can easily get to from the park.  I highly recommend checking them out!

The perfect local beach - never too crowded (unless it's a holiday), people are generally pretty considerate in regards to noise, playing music, keeping kids and pets in check

Great beach for families or just to sit and read a book.

My favourite beach in Santa Barbara. More
Calm, less people and less steps and levels to walk around. Also lovely for bird watching , and a walk on the pier

This is one of my favorite beaches because it's right off the bike path from UCSB, it has a gorgeous pier (great for fishing), historical lagoon, it has a little tar (for a SB beach), I see dolphins here all the time, and if you're old enough to drink and you clean up after yourself it is legal to drink on the beach here. There are nice lifeguards, benches, grills, and pits for horseshoe. And for those who want to drive (please do responsibly) there is a lot of parking available in the parking lots and its all free.

From 11 to 3 there is a stand that sells goods and Bud Lights (in the can) for $2. There is as well a great little sea food place right on the beach called Beachside Bar-Cafe that is good for happy hour.

Overall I think this is a great beach for both college kids and families with children.

We'll be there Monday for a Labor Day picnic with a bunch of friends. ... it's a great place to spend the day! I started taking my kids there to fish off the pier 30 years ago and now they go with their friends. It's our favorite beach to let the dog run in the surf,too!

Visiting SB for a month and have gone to several beaches. This is by far our favorite. Free parking, quiet, clean, bathrooms, restaurant and to top it off, as plane lovers, we enjoyed seeing the Cessnas do touch and gos over the ocean. We will definitely be heading back again!

We stayed at the Residence Inn Marriott , new upscale nice, with the idea to visit the Goleta Beach which is like 2 miles away. Plenty of parking if
you continue past the Beach House Restaurant. The beach was pretty much empty during the week , July 9-10-11, 2018, which was great. 
The first visit on 7/7, Saturday it had more people. DO NOT GO NEAR the little seaweed piles as if they are agitated, zillions of little chigger bugs and flies swarm to you. Especially when someone walks by. After day 1, I would clear the seaweed piles away, then set up blankets, and
spray on some bug reppelant, and that made it OK. The beach is so-so
with mediocre sand, rocks, and seaweed strew all over, amidst dead crabs and birds. If you go down about 1/4 mile, it's A LOT BETTER, like to the white fallen tree and past. That part is great. Sand is sandy, and very little debris and small beach rocks. There were warnings that the
