bacteria in the water was borderline OK, but don't eat any shell fish that you might catch from the pier, etc. Again, for the nice experience, walk further down, about a 1/4 mile from the pier.

nice little beach. i am from the oc and also go to malibu and santa monica several times a year. so it was kind of weird to not see a lot of people at the beach, and also to see a pretty anemic pier. the pier is rather long ill give it that, but not very wide at all. when my gf and i went here we saw some dolphins pretty close to the shore, theres even a place really close to the pier and shore where you can rent kayaks, paddleboats. that being said id rather go to one of my local beaches as there are more things to do in the beach area. more restaurant/nightlife. at goleta i believe theres only one restaurant. and when we went there was some private party going on in the nice outdoor section. go figure

I was in the area for one night and wanted to make sure I saw the ocean. Goleta Beach is really convenient from the SBA/UCSB area. Plenty of parking, bbq/picnic facilities and a restaurant make for a great spot to hang out.

The pier was a good walk out and gave a nice outside-in look at the coast.

It was pretty chilly when I went, but when the weather is warm, I bet this would be a nice place to spend the day and go walking along the beach.
Great place with s view of UCSB and beautiful cove.  The pier is long and I grabbed a beer for the walk out.  Several fisherman on the pier. Clean clear water too.  Nice place to hang out after work.
Great beach with many memorable experiences!  A great go to for locals, implants and tourists alike.  The water is not the cleanest or warmest but the view and fishing is to die for.

My husband spent a lot of time at Goleta Beach growing up, so when we visit the SB area, we always stop by to check out the beach.  We recently visited Goleta Beach the end of April on a Sunday.  While the parking lot had plenty of spaces, pretty much all the picnic tables were being used with families BBQ-ing, having parties, etc.  We parked and then walked over to check out the ocean-- that's when my husband and I noticed all the "KEEP OUT" Warning signs posted.  The signs stated the water is contaminated with bacteria that can cause illness and the signs were posted all along the beach and also on the beach entrance sign.  The lame thing is that despite all these signs posted all along the beach, there were kids and adults sitting in and playing in the contaminated water, and people were fishing on the pier.  

Crazy.

This is an excellent spot to relax and enjoy the Santa Barbara sun. Located next to UC Santa Barbara (Go Gauchos!), Goleta Beach Park offers many amenities: barbecue grills, picnic areas, horseshoe pits, bathrooms, volleyball courts, benches, grass areas, palm trees, a huge pier to fish from, and, best of all, free parking! Easily accessible by foot, bike, or car, most of the people here are students or local families, and the vibe is laid back and friendly.

I spent many a day studying here while I was a student (okay, replace "studying" with "lounging") and I try to stop by whenever I'm in town. It's not as crowded as the main Santa Barbara beaches and there is plenty to do. I'd often meet friends or family on the pier to drink a few beers and do some fishing (tips: no fishing license is necessary and there's a great snack shack that serves a pretty good tri-tip sandwich). The Beachside Bar Cafe is also here and it's a good place to have a drink before walking around the area.

I am shocked to read these glowing reviews. We stumbled on this place a few days ago and were so excited until we went in. Nothing but seedy looking people drinking alcohol at all the tables. Graffiti and trash. It seemed almost deserted. It was weird and we did not feel safe there.

Wow!  Watching the scroll of photos go by on the main page for this park makes it look like a really special place.  Well done, Yelpers!

Though we go to Santa Barbara often, we haven't spent much time in Goleta. Yesterday, we spent the whole afternoon in the area.  After visiting Stow House and the railroad museum, I suggested we check out the coast. That led to driving around the UCSB campus and surrounding miles and miles of apartments in Isla Vista.

Eventually, we ended up near this park area and pier, with a cafe tucked away on the east end near the pier.  As we came in, there were pop-up shelters and a sign that there was as event for a local school going on.  Lots of folks were enjoying a BBQ/picnic.  Further on into the plentiful parking, we found the cafe tucked into mature tropical landscaping.

After a late lunch, we wanted to walk the pier to experience it, see the views, and catch some cooler breezes on the water.  It's a very popular fishing spot.  Dozens of men, women, and children were plying the water with their poles and bait, waiting for hungry fish.  Didn't see a lot being reeled in while we were there.

One big note of caution - rarely have I seen knottier wood being used for the flooring planks.  I was wearing secure sandals, but felt I needed to very carefully watch each step.  The uneven texture beneath my feet felt very tippy.  An ankle twisting could be one unwary step away.  Even in closed shoes, it's no easy place to stroll.

We stopped several times to take a break from the cautious walking, taking in the pretty view of the Blue Pacific. Lots of seagulls were posing for photo ops.  ;-)  The breeze was gentle, but provided a welcome break from a warm afternoon. 

I was grateful to get back to solid land after the challenging stroll that made my ankles ache.  Still, it's a nice venue near the shore with a bonus of free parking.  Several couples were parked in the sand on beach chairs, soaking up the autumn sunshine.  They looked peaceful.  Ah, the Southern California lifestyle!

I have many fond memories of Goleta Beach Park; specifically, fishing on the pier with my dad and brothers... and much later, my own kids.  The pier is insulated from large crowds by its relative distance from the highway, the adjacent slough area which is not densely populated, and the fact that there are lots and lots of beach options in the immediate area.  No need to crowd everyone in, when there are miles of coastline for all to enjoy.

Still, the place gets full in the summer, and is well-used by anglers, beach-goers, joggers, walkers, and occasionally, some folks that are up to no good.  It happens, I guess...

The lot south of the pier is adjacent to the end of the slough, and there are plenty of opportunities for bird watchers and those who enjoy nature to take it all in.  The influence of natural tar and oil seeps is apparent just down the beach where a large asphalt seep is visible.  Baby oil and a cotton ball will get the tar off your feet.

This is a good beach to watch a grunion run in season, as thousands of the little silvery fish beach themselves on the high tide to wriggle and writhe in piscatorial bliss to usher in the next generation of grunion.  Definitely something to put on your bucket list.

As for water sports, the south-facing beach generally has small or non-existent surf, which makes it popular with divers, swimmers, paddleboarders, and anglers.  The pier is pretty long, and a rock-covered pipe reef runs the length of the pier on the north/west side, creating habitat for small brown rockfish, sheepshead, sculpin, lobsters, crabs, urchins, and other critters.  Divers occasionally head out at night to hunt for lobsters, and anglers ply the waters for all of the above, along with mackerel, bonito, bass, sharks, and rays.

Years ago, I worked with an organization called UPSAC that set up an Angler Interpretive Center about halfway down the pier.  Staffed by a local "docent", Boyd Grant aka "pierhead", the center loaned out fishing gear, provided instruction, served as a headquarters for kids fishing derbies and pier/shore based angler advocacy issues, and featured a line-recycling bin along with a lot of instructional materials.  The county discontinued funding the project and it shuttered, and recently Boyd--who took it upon himself to clean the pier every day as a volunteer--passed away.  The community of anglers on the pier remember him fondly as an advocate for them, the Goleta pier, and the entire beach park system.

At night, the pier serves as a roost for gulls and pelicans; thus, the pier is speckled white with their droppings, so watch your step and your seat.  But if those sorts of things don't bother you, the end of Goleta pier is one of the few places on the California shoreline where you can see the sun rise over the ocean at certain times of the year.  If you're in the neighborhood, it's worth it to park the car and get out to enjoy a little stroll.

Make sure to check the sign that you will miss if you blink, cause your little ones are too excited to be at the beach, that warns you that the beach is contaminated and can cause illness! Beach has lots of seaweed, but worse of all is the tar! It gets stuck to your feet, terrible for your health. This beach should be closed down when it's that bad or have a lifeguard or giant signs in front of the water warning everyone who comes to the beach. Never again stopping at this beach. Packed up and came to East Beach instead, clean, no smell of tar or rotting tar seaweed. Pinchi big oil!

It has been said of many places, "You can usually return, but you can never go back." Somewhere in the midst of syntax and sentiment, those words always seem to ring true for this angler, 45 years on this journey and most of them plying the nearshore with a rod in my hands.

Goleta pier is, perhaps, one location that most elicits those feelings-- for more than 20 years, it has been my "home pier," fully 130 miles from my doorstep, but never too far for a casual evening visit.

A few weeks ago, my youngest son saw my old hoop nets leaning against the garage wall, and told me that for his 7th birthday, he wanted to catch a crab. Between distance learning, distance principaling, and a move to a new home, the thought of getting out on a pier was a refreshing notion. And so, my wife and I loaded up the truck with kids and gear, and started north on the 101. After a quick stop at In N Out, we arrived at Goleta Beach Park. Fearing the worst, I was pleasantly surprised to find that the park and the pier were clean and improved: the restrooms were in good working order, pier railings were replaced, and the lights were upgraded from sodium halide orange to LED soft white.
We enjoyed our dinner at the picnic tables, not far from the site of several Goleta Get-Togethers with fishing buddies over the years. I breathed a few heavy sighs remembering old friends who have moved away or moved on to higher planes, but all with a smile on my face.

We set up a few crab hoops from the bend to the crane on the reef side, and threw in two hi-lo rigs with sardine chunks for good measure. One of the more glorious sunsets I've seen in a while created a gorgeous backdrop for our family adventure.

The action was slow but steady on small (sub legal) rock crabs, decorator crabs, and one big spider crab to round out the variety. The birthday boy was ecstatic, of course, and even added a couple of white croaker for good measure.

I found myself looking down the pier for old friends a few times, but only saw other folks enjoying a relatively warm winter evening.

As for me and my family, we enjoyed our 260 mile turn around trip immensely, and my walk down memory lane has stayed with me all week: running's  "pier fishing school" years ago as a service project; catching all sorts of fish, crabs, and lobsters in the company of friends; nights of solitude with only the stars to keep me company. I was so glad to visit Goleta, even if only for a few hours to reminisce about more than two decades. It proved once again that while our piscatorial pursuits serve the purpose of catching (and if we choose, releasing) critters of the sea, there is so much more to the pastime.

So I will do my best to return again, even if I can't go back.

Was in the Goleta area for a family getaway at the Bacara and spent a lot of time at Goleta beach.  The beach is beautiful.  You can walk along the beach or follow the walking trail that runs parallel to the beach.  It is one of the most beautiful beaches I have been on and is a great place to take pictures.  On the days I went into the water with my daughters (5 & 7), we got oil tar on our feet.  That's the first time I experienced that but it came off easily. There were also large clumps of seaweed which my oldest daughter did not appreciate.  There was just the right amount of people on the beach that you could have your space you but not feel completely alone.

I wish this was my hometown beach. 

Parking on a gorgeous, picture-perfect Saturday was plentiful and FREE. Suck it, Huntington city and state beaches!

A mix of local Gauchos and families cooking some AMAZING smelling food on the grills here. 

It's nice to find a beach where parking is easy, doesn't cost a thing, there are grills that you can use, there are benches you can sit on, and there's a ton of shade too!

I LOVE THIS TOWN AND I LOVE THIS BEACH.

Came here for a friend's rehearsal dinner and it turned out nice. Granted, it's always cold here but for the most part it's nice and clean and the park ranger Mark, was extremely accommodating. I'd certainly suggest this location if you're looking for something reasonable.  Beware though....these seagulls are gangsta and do not respect your space.
Nice beach to sight see but not to get in the water. Water is very dirty. Beautiful place to take pictures.

This beach offers a little something for everyone. 

As a Goleta local, I've patronized this beach countless times over the course of my lifetime. Birthday parties, family barbecues, organization lunches, or just casually coming on my own, this place has served me well. 

The beach is clean for the most part and pretty well-maintained. As a kid, I used to love the abstract wind chime-ish art piece that hung adjacent to the playground and was a little sad when they did away with it. I associate this beach with the random 'dings' the art piece would emit. It was a pretty cool spot in its heyday. 

There are a decent number of picnic areas, an ample parking lot, and the quintessential Goleta Pier which is a must if you want to experience a beautiful sunrise or sunset. The restaurant and snack bar were anchors of this beach and I hope that they are able to reopen in the near future. 

This beach also connects the Obern Trail bike path with the one leading into the UCSB campus. I've biked here from my current home in downtown Santa Barbara and it's a nice destination point. Watching the gentle tide is quite serene and makes my treks well worth it. I hope to come back here again soon.

Perfect local beach with plenty of parking and no price of admission. 
Not many people were here the day I came and the sand is soft and warm. There are tables and grills and beaches located on the grassy area above, all along the coastline. Not surrounded by restaurants or cafe, but that makes it the perfect, solitary relaxation spot. 
Cute storks

Free parking and excellent spot to see the sunset and the seagulls dive for fish! Many families come here to BBQ or just to hang out and relax. It's also right next to UCSB so you'll see a lot of students exercising here.

Never a bad day in paradise. It's been a little windy, but the water is ok (rash guard=makes life easier).

Water can't be that bad if people are snorkeling!  

Note on bathrooms: gotta push those flush buttons hard.  No one likes to see floaters...but our out of town friends say these are the cleanest beach bathrooms they've ever seen.

Bottom Line: This is a pretty awesome beach if you like airplane and tar

I have been going here since I was a little kid.  The beach has a little bit much naturally occurring tar that I'd like but it's still a great place to hang out and BBQ.

There is a sweet play structure for the little ones, a pier....they have been struggling with erosion for years now but they pretty much have it under control

A very, very large beach park that can accommodate tons of people in a nicely distanced manner. In fact, it feels much larger than any of the beaches in nearby Santa Barbara, allowing lots of space for all, no matter how many people there are actually are here, thanks to a huge parking lot.

One of the highlights is the long pier, which is perfect for fishing or for launching small boats, and is covered in separate Yelp listing. But the beach itself, and its land-based resources, are large and plentiful. When I stopped by, tons of people were enjoying themselves on the sand or at multiple BBQ/picnic areas close to the parking lot. The beach park is also strategically located on walking paths that connect to UCSB and bike paths that go all the way to Santa Barbara.

Simply a pleasant way to take in some oceanside ambience without feeling crowded.

We came here at night as this was the closest beach to our hotel last weekend.
Tons of free and easy parking and not much going on on the beach or pier, perfect.
The views are amazing, the sand pearly white and super fine, perfect for a barefoot stroll.
Beach cafe right at the pier to grab some coffee and enjoy the views, great stop while in the area!

Goleta Beach is truly a beautiful spot. Although the cafe will be missed, there is still plenty of draw between the park, picnic spots, and pier. 

Fishing on the pier is great for the whole family. People catch halibut, kelp bass, shovel nose, and more. In addition, there's a great bait shack in the summer months run by a local who own Hook, Line, and Sinker. The boys are very sweet and knowledgeable!

The picnic spots have a variety of sizes and are very reasonably priced to host events. Great option especially given the in between/opening up stages is
Covid. 

The beach has gentle waves that are safe for children, and ample space to spread out and not feel crowded by other families. Heading south past the pier is a local spot known as Black Rock, which boasts some cool geological features. 

Overall, Goleta Beach is a great choice to escape some of the crowds of SB or if you're staying in the area.

This beach was located near our hotel so we drove here to give the kids some beach time.

The university is nearby so the beach was full of students more than families. I have to say I prefer to go to beaches with families then college drinking cussing 20 yr old's around.  Don't get me wrong, I was in college once too, but I watched my sailor mouth around kids.

It was kind of kelpy but the kids had fun and it was a nice afternoon to relax and hear the ocean waves.  There is a pier that runs in between the beach so maybe I should have checked out the other side because being near the volleyball courts wasn't ideal.  Overall it wasn't crowded but I wouldn't come running back.

Since I discovered this place recently, I've spent quite a bit of time here at Goleta Beach - it's beaches like this that make me glad that at end of the day I'm a California Girl through and through ;-)

There's lots of room to walk right on the beach, or you can use the trail which runs along side it.  Not only is this a gorgeous beach to walk on, but it's also a wonderful place to take pictures.

Pros:

- The times I've been here, while it's never been completely deserted there haven't been too many other people around - you'll feel like there's room for personal space, without being completely alone
- It's nice and clean, along as you stay on the sand
- There's some great bird watching to be done here
- There's a pier very close at hand

Cons:

- Like most of the beaches on this part of the Coast, it's usually fairly cold here
- There are large masses of seaweed in the water
- The first time I went wading I found oil tar on my feet when I came back to the beach; it came off fairly easily, but it's not something I'd sign up to do again ;-)

To sum it up, this is a lovely beach, and I wouldn't hesitate to recommend it or come back anytime I need a dose of sun and surf :-)

A great (and underrated) beach! 

Pro:  Free parking, Lots of BBQ/picnic areas, Horseshoe pits, pier and restaurant nearby, Close proximity to UCSB, Bike paths everywhere, Typically uncrowded, Great views.

Cons:  The beach is eroding slowly but surely = not a ton of sand, not many volleyball courts, the "park" needs some minor improvements to the grass areas and infrastructure.

I've been here countless times to:  picnic, lay out, play volleyball, fish off the pier, run / exercise, birthday parties, work events, eat, scuba dive.......pretty much anything you can think of.  Having been to nearly every other public/private/secret beach on the SB coastline, I would say that this is one of the most under appreciated beaches around.  I feel Goleta Beach is the most well-rounded beach in the area.  There's not much surf here (campus point is around the bend), but you can pretty much do everything else.  

This is also a great place to run.  If you start at the pier and head East (South in SB speak) you run along one of the most secluded and scenic stretches of beach in the area (a similar run is going West (North) from the parking lot of Hendry's Beach).  It's not long, but you can always do a few laps and you will likely find that no one else is around.  Also, you'll typically see artists painting the cliffs/seascapes on this side of the pier, pretty cool.

The fact that you can easily bike in and out of this beach is icing on the cake.  I really wish the city would look at possible remedies to the beach erosion because it's really starting to take its toll.  If you want proof, check out the pictures inside of the Beachside Cafe (near the pier).  Also, the grass areas could use some reseeding and the parking lot could use a fresh coat of asphalt.  

I highly recommend this spot.
This Beach looks nice from afar but when you actually get up close to the water you'll find a lot of seaweed mixed with what seems like nuggets of tar

One of my favorite beaches. Beautiful and not too crowded. Ucsb is a scenic walk or jog away. Pier is a great place to walk around with loved ones. Starfish and shell gems can be found underneath the pier. Dolphins and seals are at times spotted in the ocean. Kayaking is super fun here as well as a chill time soaking in the sun at the beach. Great place to BBQ and bring kids to pay at the playground.

Not the nicest beach but easy, free parking and direct access to the water from beyond the the grassy area.  They have a park with kids play structure, picnic tables, BBQs and lots of trees.   Beach is only feet from parking lot, easy to view beach from grassy area.   Kids liked playing on sand and in water.  No tar other than 2 tiny spots on one foot.

This review is for the fishing pier at goleta beach park.  

Fishing here was awesome.  The entire pier was full of fisherman on a weeknight just before high tide but the pier is large and long enough for excellent spacing between anglers (in contrast to Stearns wharf downtown where fishing is restricted to the very end of the pier so anglers end up being shoulder to shoulder).  

I tried to use a spoon however kept getting stuck on kelp/seaweed so I stuck to bait.  Tried fish filets, salmon eggs, and raw shrimp using a Sebiki rig and a bobber.  The perch, sardines, and minnows went crazy for the shrimp.  I probably caught about 10 perch in an hour in addition to a couple sardines and minnows.  

The guy next to me was fishing the bottom with heavy gear and some minnows and he got about 3-4 large 20-40lb bat rays in the same time as I got my 10 small perch. 

A group of crazy college kids came by as the sun was setting to jump off the end of the pier, yikes! Sunset was beautiful even though it doesn't set over the water

google_reviews.text: This is such a great beach! Dog friendly, great place to gather for birthdays and group events. They have outdoor showers and nice bathrooms right there

Beautiful beach with nice waves and gorgeous sunsets. There are clean bathrooms with showers. The parking situation can get hectic on hot weekends since there are a limited number of spaces available in the parking lot and no additional street parking. But because of this, it’s rarely crowded on the beach itself and is a peaceful place to enjoy the sun, sand and waves.
We went on Christmas Eve to watch the beautiful sunset and walk our dog along the beach.  There were parking and not so crowded.  It is a nice beach; however, we need to watch for tar in our shoes.

☛Quiet, peacful, especially on weekdays.
➡Decent amount of free parking by the tennis club.
➡Easy access to Ritz Carlton, one steep trail, one easier path.
➡Clean restrooms with outdoor shower.
➡Sometimes the Ritz food truck is here.
➡Main beach access trail is surrounded by flora that smells amazing (i.e. California Sage).

Beautiful beach access point with some parking. You are more likely to find parking in the morning. Food truck available at times. Public, single stall restroom with outdoor shower available near the food truck.  Some picnic tables as well. Dog friendly beach & not crowded.

yelp_reviews.text:
It's a, relatively,  small beach a mile from my house. I go there to swim in the ocean and always go alone  - unless I bring my dog.
The fellow, Brian,  who takes care of the beach, ie., deals with the rentals, the canteen, rakes the beach, helps out all the patrons and - most importantly - keeps an eye out for the swimmers and surfers - is extremely warm and friendly. Even though there is no official lifeguard,
I know that Brian is watching me in the water - and so I feel safe.
He knows how to multitask and so he makes that beach the great place it is. There are  assistants there, also,  and they, too, are kind and friendly. 
I feel like it's "my place." And I don't go anywhere else to swim. Thanks !

I needed to get out of town and out of LA! With all of the covid stuff, and then more covid stuff at my job, my anxiety was building up and I needed some fresh air and some beautiful calming views. I found this place on the All Trails app and was so happy to find  this small treasure of a beach near Santa Barbara. It's a locals spot, something kinda hidden, and a very simple place to walk and hear and see waves rolling on the shore. There are a few short trails, two that go up a little higher to see the ocean and greenery around you. I even saw dolphins swimming! This place was just what I needed. 

Quiet, with a small parking lot near the tennis courts. About a quarter mile before the Ritz Carlton resort entrance. Bathrooms also available and decently clean with soap and paper towels available. Bring doggy bags as they were out when I saw the dispensers. There are probably  around 2-3 trails attached to the beach access trail, and each are a little over 1 mile, maybe 1.2-1.4 Miles long. It was awesome to hike up into the higher viewpoint while hearing the ocean nearby.

Really cool little spot. Lots of seaweed and debris along the shore but it has some killer views down the shoreline and a fair amount of play room to set up a blanket and umbrella, etc. Also good to escape the college kid scene that you find elsewhere in Santa Barbara. Beautiful sunsets happen here, though they'll never be blatantly visible as they set beneath the landmass toward Lompoc. Parking was plentiful as well. Not sure if there's much surfing to be had here though.

Haskell's beach is a great beach in Goleta that is slightly more hidden. A private lot built by the Bacarra has a nicely maintained walkway with facts about the plants on the way down. There is a bath house just above the sand as well. 

This beach has relatively small surf, and an oil pier on one side. Near the oil pier is the beautiful Bacarra. On the other side of this cove there are pylons and the remains of an old pier that have since fallen into the ocean. 

The positives of this beach is that it's pretty uncrowned and easy to walk to. This makes it a great choice for kids. The only real downside is there tends to be a lot of wrack as well as tar. Olive oil or baby oil will take that off for you though! 

Haskell's is a great low key spot in Goleta if you're looking for a quick trip!

I love coming here because it's so secluded and great to relax with my daughter and my dog. The beach is overall really clean and easy to get to from the parking lot. It's less than a 5 min walk. The bathrooms are right over the hill before you get to the beach too. The best time to come here is during low tide because you can walk and run through the whole beach. To the right will be bacara and if you keep walking down the left, it will eventually lead you to ellwood. this is definitely one of my favorite beaches in Goleta!

Enjoyed coming here for the first time... waves were constant and great for body boarding, parking was scarce, and the beach was beautiful. The Bacara is near by and the view of the coastline makes you think you're in a different country. Really enjoyed this beach!

I can't believe I've never been to this beach before. It is nice and secluded and is in front of the Bacara. The parking is in front of the tennis court. You have to walk a few mintues to the beach. I got a little mixed up and walked towards the hotel so I turned around. The beach itself is very clean and peaceful.

name: Jonny D. Wallis Neighborhood Park
formatted_address: 170 S Kellogg Ave, Goleta, CA 93117, USA
categories: Playgrounds, Parks, Skate Parks, Active Life

google_reviews.text: Great new neighborhood park with impressive assortment of themed play areas for both young and old. Plenty of picnic tables for large gatherings, and public bathrooms to make your visit more enjoyable.

Great park for kids of all ages. There’s the skate park, the basketball and tennis or pickle ball (or whatever it is) courts, there’s a handball court, and a large grassy field for soccer or whatever, and play structures for younger kids. Picnic tables are great and the bathrooms are kept up.
A good place for family party's, BBQ's. Play ground area for the kids. Skate place and clean bathrooms.

This park is a park, a breath of fresh air,
A green oasis, where worries can't dare.

A tapestry woven with trees tall and wide,
Where sunlight dances, and shadows reside.

A place to skate,
With no space for hate.

A playground for laughter, a haven for rest,
A place to wander, where hearts are blessed.

A place for soccer,
Hopefully no injuries or need for a doctor.

With winding paths and benches to sit,
A moment to pause, where time seems to flit.

A street vendor with a fruit cart,
And a playground designed with both brains and a heart.

The rustling of leaves, a gentle breeze's song,
The chirping of birds, where life feels so strong.

A canvas for colors, in seasons' embrace,
From vibrant spring blooms to autumn's soft grace.

This place is a park, a community's heart,
Where stories unfold, and friendships start.

A place to connect, to simply be free,
A sanctuary for all, for you and for me.

So if you’re still reading this far,
You’ll know why I gave it five stars.

Nice little park with a little of everything. Equipment looks new and the small skate park offers some fun stuff for the beginner to intermediate skater. Bathrooms are clean. Shaded areas with picnic tables underneath and a few barbecue grills.  People friendly. We stopped by on a Saturday afternoon to eat out lunch we had just bought from a fast food restaurant nearby.  We were driving through the  Santa Barbara area.

yelp_reviews.text: Drove here solely for the purpose of playing some basketball. To our dismay, both hoops were gone and pickle ballers took over the area. Bathrooms were smelly and somewhat neglected (toilet paper strewn all over, suspicious brown smears on the floor). Otherwise everything else looked fine.

The Johnny D. Wallis' Park is a wonderful new park addition to Goleta.  It sits a block from Route 217 in Old Town Goleta at Hollister & Kellogg Avenues.  The park is much larger than I had expected with a total size of 4 acres and offers a large variety amenities including:
-Kid's playground
-Skate park and outdoor exercise machines
-Multi-purpose field, basketball court, handball court, concrete ping-pong table, bocce ball court, and pickleball court
-Splash pad to come in phase two
-Walking path that goes circumferentially around the park
-Picnic area with a piñata post (fun feature)

There are public restrooms with water fountains (one with a water bottle filler).  I'm happy to see this park here for the community to have a safe place to remain active outdoors.

As I was waiting for my oil change I wanted a place to sit, since covid limits where I can sit or lounge around. This park is quite nice. As others have mentioned, skate park, play ground, basketball court, pickle ball court, handball wall, ping pong table, bbq picnic areas, chess tables, exercise area, large grass area you could play soccer, mini trail around the park and more. Wife and I tried playing board games at home but she got bored. May have to challenge her to a handball game here so I can dominate her!! But she'd probably say otherwise. Nice park and didn't expect it to have a variety of stuff in the area.

This turned out to be a really fun new park. I love the layout as it is great for parties and play. The skate park was hopping when we were there. They have an ADA swing which every park should have these days! And pinata posts are ready for you to hitch and swing. There are lots of fun kids things to climb swing and spin. Bring your ping pong paddles as the concrete tables are going to be level for a while! The basketball hoops look super fun as well. The bathrooms are new so clean and the water fill stations are totally modern. We will be back plenty I am sure. The lot is small so may need to park and walk a block or so if not early on the weekend.

Awesome recreational park for kids and adults.  Open playground for toddlers -12 year old.  Skate park is open too, and scooters are allowed, I believe. Other attractions include handball court, pickleball court, stationary exercise equipment,  NO SAND, and piñata holders..WTH!  A hidden little gem off Hollister in Goleta.

google_reviews.text:
Am taking off a star, as once again the foxtail grasses are out of control. The burrs will follow.
How about a volunteer team to install some native plants and pull out the invasives, esp the ones that can severely harm little children and our dogs.  Or.... what will it take for the public parks to have time, money, interest in  cleaning up the grass and vegetation?

The stage has been completed and the kiddy pool is open again! MIssion Creak was flowing this year with all the rain, really delightful.

,................

Neighborhood park, large enough for many activities. Not fenced into parcels so it a pleasant walk with dog. Stage badly needs resurfacing as dancers and exercise classes use it even during Covid. In non Covid times is used for festivals.

There is seating for large groups and areas to BB, tennis courts, a pool for toddlers, and if it rains a beautiful creek.

Complaints are the seasonal fox tails and burrs as park services doesn't seem to have the resources to control these and how the parking can at times be overrun by non park users.

The best place ever for any type of festivity, and festival, really nice people. Crashing into a party may be challenging but worth it. Depending on the type of party holding a can of beer (an empty one works) helps to be seen as a valid guest.

Great park with nice playground area. The park runs along Mission Creek and is 2 blocks from the hospital. In better years, this is the park for all kinds of ethnic festivals. Huge BBQ pit with multiple cement picnic tables. Other wooden picnic area with grills around the park. I did notice more homeless in the parking lot, but the playground area and rest of the park is fine. Be kind to those around you.
I love bringing my dog to this park. He likes to run around and look for swirls.
Went there to join in with a Tai Chi class. Lovely location.

yelp_reviews.text:
We went to this park in July for the annual weekend of Bastille Day.
What a disappointment! This park is pretty bad, nothing to rave about it. It could use some sidewalks, you have to walk through dirt, I was wearing sandals, got dirt all over my feet and even got hurt. Pretty bad, never happened to me before in other parks even when we hiked.

We came from LA for the French Festival. The actual Bastille Day-I've heard and read for years about it, expected something great. It was quite the opposite. The vendors were barely selling anything French (more like stalls for local business owners- they were selling even window shutters!), just one stand for pastries- overpriced and didn't look fresh. We were hungry and we wanted to get a savory crepe- once again, it was overpriced ($8), the worst crepe I've ever had (small, made fast- factory level, they barely put any Dijon mustard in it and packaged ham and cheese). Compare this to what you can get at the Crepe Company at the Farmer's Market in LA! It was from Lemoine Creperie, so avoid it! It's a rip off.

The food selection -bad- Greek food, Cajun, German, everything else but French!

They had 2 stages, the whole time we were there, they didn't have any acts playing anything. The program had pretty bad numbers coming up, once again, not a lot to do with France. 

Crowded, hot, the merchandise was poor and expensive. What a let down! It's free and it should be. Just one stall selling French desserts, Napoleons and macarons, but the lady who was selling didn't put any prices up and she was pretty much yelling at customers!

Drinks- overpriced- around $7 for a Kronenbourg.

We left after less than an hour. Don't recommend. I was lucky to spend July 14th in Paris, didn't expect that here, but at least the spirit of it. The Bastille Day in LA that we went to (for several years in a row) weren't great organized, but better- they had authentic food and real French entertainment. Too bad they now charge admission!

For the Santa Barbara one, skip it!

Our favorite park in SB for our 3 & 5 y.o.s. Sand play, nature play, picnic area, play structures for all ages, and swings (not baby swings, though). Clean multi-stall restrooms. A few vagrants around but nothing unusual for this sad day and age. I have never been in summer when the pool was open so that might change things. I did see a group of 6 kids with two grownups (day care) but it was fine, lots of room for all to roam. Other than that it was quite quiet on a weekday morning. Shady and sweet.
Not so much about the park but the French Festival that took place once a year here is the best. French food, music, culture, and more. See you on 2014 French Festival!

As far as parks go, this is a good one. It's large, has large open areas, shaded areas, smaller areas with play equipment, sandlots; and during the summer a small pool for younger children. From my perspective this park has 2 issues; one is street sweeping, when it happens, it happens on both sides of 2 streets and with the parking lot closed because of the recent renovation the city is doing, parking can be a real PITA. Secondly, the park is a perfect example of parents showing up, ignoring their kids and staring at their phones for 2 hours. Some of the moms are an exception here but it happens way too much with kids who are running around at full speed throwing sand at other small children or tackling other kids at the top of the slides. Frequently, parents will bring their freshly sick kids here as well and let them slobber and sneeze all over the playground. Some babysitters even show up here with groups of 5 or 6 kids and let them go, and sadly this keeps me from coming more often as my daughter does enjoy it quite a bit.

Came here with Good Times Travel along with Pasadena and Glendale City College for the French Festival (Bastille Day) - majorly disappointed!!! :(  

Free Admission, plentiful street parking. Lots of restrooms both at the front of the park and end of the park.

Santa Barbara is known as the American French Rivera - yes, beautiful along the coast line, but at this French Festival (Bastille Day), hardly! What a major disappointment!!!

Food and Drinks:  Really nothing French - I hardly saw any French restaurants selling their food out here. Not even French influenced Vietnamese vendors (nope, Banh Mi Che Cali, Lees, Mr. Baguette, Banh Mi My Tho, etc). I envisioned French Onion Soup bowls; assorted French wine vendors or assorted French pastries or chocolates vendors - there were hardly any. At most, maybe 2-3 bakeries (sadly, the pastries sold here can't compete with the ones at The Grove on 3rd and Fairfax, and the baguettes - Banh Mi Che Cali or Lees makes it so much better).

Only two vendors I saw were selling crepes, and the lines for both of them were hella long, and overpriced. I got my Nutella Banana Crepe ($8) from Pacific Crepes - nothing special - plain and generic. Took 30mins to get my crepe along with others standing there waiting for theirs. They were totally disorganized in their makeshift kitchen. They also sold Le Hot Dogs and bbq items, but again, nothing special and overpriced foods.

Vendors: Merchandises sold there were hella expensive and nothing worth buying. They were nice to look at until you flipped over the price tags.

Entertainment: They had three stages surrounding the park. The best one I liked was the one that housed the French Polynesian Dancers/Flamenco Dancers, etc. Overall, these stages, I liked when I wanted to sit down and relax. 

I did like the Knights fighting area. All I needed was a turkey leg, and a table = bam, Medieval Times, lol! They did have some people dress up as French Royals, but none of them was Marie Antoinette, go fig!

I also thought they would have mimes walking around or a guy with a lil monkey by his side while he cranks out music - nope. 

This festival also allows dogs because they had a Poodle Parade later on. So at least it was cute seeing a lot of furballs walking around. 

The Park itself: huge and spacious. Some areas of the park have uneven dirt spaces, and they even got a lil kiddie pool for kids to play in. Since my group was there for fours hours, I've spent the last two hours catching Pokemon and that was awesome, lol!

Would I come back? Nah, I would pass. Instead, I would for sure come back for the Paseo Nuevo shopping center (think Glendale Galleria/Americana style).

I went with a friend to this beautiful park Sunday for The FRENCH FESTIVAL.  It was one of the best outdoor festivals I've been to. The setting was great. This is a beautiful park with a lot of big trees for shade and a bridge that connects the park in the middle making it a great place for a festival.  I Loved this Festival. The Venders were very friendly. The food was Yummy....we ate at the French Grill. I had one of the Best Hamburgers ever.. It was served on a Brioche Roll with Garlic Butter, Carmelized Onions, Brie, tomato and lettuce. It was $10 and very well worth it...We strolled and enjoyed all the Booths, from French Music to Parasals, Ladies Hair Adornments, Pastries, Jewelry and more. The Music was very good, they had three stages set up in different parts of the park so that you only heard the one you were at, that was nice. We saw a French Singer..she had a beautiful voice and before she sang her husband told a wonderful story about the song. They had an Accordian Stage and a Performance Stage with Can Can Dancers and a beautiful couple doing a French Dance..looked like the Tango..they were very good. We were there for five hours and still didn't see everything. We left just before The Poodle and French Bulldog Parade but we were tired by then and driving back to Long Beach. It rained the whole way home but we were still in such a Good Mood from the fun experience of the day that we didn't care. I can't wait to go back again next year..and I'll take more money next time...lots of Nice Quality ..Pretty and French things to purchase. I Highly Recommend going to the French Festival next year...if you love festivals that are outdoors and emmersed in nature with friendly people and good food and entertainment you will love it. Also parking was easy and very clean bathrooms were a Plus. And the Festival is Free.  review helps others learn about great local businesses.

Please don't review this business if you received a freebie for writing this review, or if you're connected in any way to the owner or employees.

I really liked this park for how big it is. The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars is because the park was a little difficult to find (google maps sent me somewhere totally different). The park has a pool for kids, benches and picnic tables. Additionally the park hosts a lot of festival events like the French festival and most recently the Greek festival. This park is worth checking out if only for the festivals it holds.

Nice park w camping vibes. Lots of exploring for kids and great playground. Also, dogs are welcome. The only thing I don't like are the restrooms but over all they were pretty clean . I will definitely be back

Mission Creek runs to the west of the park. To the east there is a well-travelled street, but you hardly notice the traffic at all.  There is lots of bird life and lots of wonderful old Oak and Sycamore trees. A very spacious park with lovely mountain views looking north.
There are picnic tables and the bathrooms are always clean and nice.

 If you choose to walk from one end of the park to the other in a long, narrow loop, you will be covering a total "mileage" of about 3/4 of a mile. There's a stage at the southern end, where Folk dancers and other users can sometimes be found.

The horseshoe players are at the southern end, too, and there's nearly always someone there playing that skillful game.

I highly recommend a leisurely stroll.  Just be aware that there are homeless who hang out at the southeastern part of the park.

Oak Park is a childhood favorite it's spring and summer cultural festivals, summer swimming pool, and kids play area make for adventure days. There is free parking with Natalie at grooms aardvark picnic areas great for small or big events. The mission creek runs on the side and is fun to frog hunt in during springtime. One item to watch is homeless occasionally so avoid going by yourself in earLy morning or late nights, General  safety stuff.
Update: Now there are two baby swings, but no big-kid swings. The "Mobius strip" style play structure remains awesomely unique.
Very nice park. Lots of beautiful trees and a creek that was unfortunately dry when I went, but stunning regardless. Well kept and clean. I have yet to find a four leaf clover...

Two Words: Picnic Time!


It is perfect for kids who just like to run around or play on the playground. You can definitely catch me on one of the swings! Hey, I may be 21, but I have the spirit of a curious child.

I love to roam around in this park and just explore the area. This is perfect if you like to go on walks by yourself or meditate. 

If you are thinking about taking a girl(or guy) out on a date, do it here! It is inexpensive, plenty of space to talk and get to know your date. Imagine holding hands with a significant other and then you stop on the park's bridge. Gaze into each other's eyes, and create the memory of a First Kiss. 

This park is well maintained. One of the ways you can judge is by the cleanliness of the restrooms. The toilet paper is well stocked and they throw away their trash.

There are so many shaded and open areas at this park which is why this is my favorite park. Oak Park calls for an adventure!

I don't otherwise know this park. The French Festival on Saturday, even with a couple short rain spells, and odd-timed heat on Sunday, kept folks with great sense of humor meandering delicious food, hand crafters, and a hilarious dog/poodle Parade Led by the funniest French Bulldog, who my husband and I watched giggle with children before the parade. Vendors were trading, They were friendly, and took what they were doing very seriously, but many of them were, themselves, entertaining. We come from Orange County every year, and I must say that we almost didn't return one year. The MC of the poodle parade was belligerent and offensive, to people on& off stage, until he was distracted to unfold off color jokes. Better this year finally! The gal in pink (Honey, NOT a French color!) was delightfully authentic and a bit funny. I'll bring my standard poodle next year and give that Cute French Bulldog a Tall taste of French. I hope she accepts the challenge! 
I'd like to see a wider range of French Performers, louder sound at the East stage , and could do with out the repertiore of the act involving yelling "I Hate you, Stay!" Which made everyone not near the stage wonder who was in trouble.
OH, Molan Rogue performances were wonderful, and became expertly hilarious in the rain! Nothing could get my husband and I down on this day!

name: Shoreline Park
formatted_address: Shoreline Dr & Santa Rosa Place, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: Well maintained. Lots of parking. Clean public restrooms. Fabulous views. Great playground, picnic tables and benches.

Beautiful views from this well maintained ocean side Park.  Clean bathrooms , wide walking trails with no bikes allowed. There is a stairway to the beach that is pretty steep, but well worth at evening magic hour.

Beautiful park, well maintained with a VERY long ledt to right walking path with plenty of grass, walking paths, trees and... then there is the mighty Pacific Ocean right there in front of you.  It's Santa Barbara so it's extra and so worth it.  They say to the right of the staircase is off leash for dogs and we did turn right but there is hundreds, thousands of feet of beach in either direction.  Check the tides, that matters but if you're around this place ans and want to relax do yourself a favor and stop by to bring your BP way down

This park was so nice to walk around. A lot of places for kids to play, for dogs, or just to watch the ocean. The binoculars don’t work but the view is beautiful. There are steps down to the beach and parking is limited.
Great place to take a walk and view the Pacific Ocean atop the bluff. Good view of the Harbor and Sterns Wharf.

yelp_reviews.text: what a great breath of fresh air & spectacular view.
LOVE this for a jog. I fell flat on my face down by the marina, but that was just me being clumsy. It's like Santa Monica + Venice - the dirty surroundings or strange people.

we were here visiting santa barbara and looking for various investment properties when we were told by our real estate agent of this park being nice for the view. Wow! Such a beautiful place. I love the beach and seeing all the dogs. We're from the other side of the US so to see the california lifestyle like this was just so great. I think we'll be moving here very soon.

Shoreline Park is a lovely, wonderful place to spend an afternoon in Santa Barbara, which is already a city more lovely and wonderful than most. I've been to this park a couple of times to kill time between meals and drinks in a free, dog-friendly manner. It's an ideal park to walk around for both humans and dogs, with nice pathways and a stunning view of the ocean from where it sits, on top of a mesa.

It takes about a half hour to loop from the (free!) parking lot to the beach and back, at a leisurely, sniff-as-you-please pace. The walk is pleasant, pleasant, pleasant, with the sun and the sea, the light and the breeze. The dog and people watching is also fun. There are always people out getting some exercise and enjoying the scenery. Not a ton of picnickers--the park is set up with some seating, but it's not huge in the scheme of things, more a strip of park than a spread out blank on a map.

I particularly like seeing all the old couples who utilize Shoreline Park. On our last trip, a very old man and a very old woman passed by us with walking sticks, and both smiled and cooed at our dog. It's nice to see old people enjoying each other's company. I hope this place is still around when my husband and I retire to our gorgeous multimillion dollar Santa Barbara seaside mansion. We'll take our dog--who will be in his fifties by then--and walk around and around, soaking in the beauty of the world.

This is a great family park! It's always packed on the weekends with families, having BBQs or birthday parties. You'll see people laying out on the grassy areas or people just sitting on the benches, enjoying the spectacular views. There's ample parking on either side of the park and a beach access down some stairs. There's even a playground for the little ones.

I absolutely love this park. The only one that tops it is Central Park, because it's motherf*ing CP, located in the most incredible city in the world-NYC, aka home sweet home.
Ok, back to Shoreline...
My favorite things to do here are:
1. Picnic
2. Take pictures of the view
3. Watch the sun rise
...in that order.
Yesterday I did 2 out of those 3. 
Usually, when picnicking, aside from enjoying wine, cheese, crackers, salami, a sliced pear or apple, dark chocolate, I also prefer to simultaneously be productive and get a considerable amount of work done, whether it be returning emails, reading, writing, etc. However, yesterday the only productive thing I accomplished at Shoreline was reveling in the ocean. There was something different about it yesterday. It may have been the soon to come stunningly stormy weather, or the smell and feel of rain in the air and underfoot that was still unmistakably present from the day before. Perhaps it was just one of those times when I needed to stop and smell the roses, or in this case, the Pacific. Whatever the reason, it was notably one of those unforgettable experiences that will stay with me for the rest of my days. 
Thank you Shoreline...I'll try not to stay away too long.

My favorite park in Santa Barbara, CA, located on the Mesa area & has an awesome view of the Pacific Ocean. It is a green park with many benches & seats. It's wide open but has pockets of shade & breezes. It's a great place to have an impromptu picnic as there are so many restaurants to pick up food near by. Tons of people come here for walks after dinner & have lunch at a peaceful spot during the week.

Beautiful coastal walk!  We love this beach more quiet than East Cabrillo beach and seems to be more families.  Love how everyone bbqs and the homes are just beautiful.  If we ever move to Santa Barbara we'll make sure to buy a home next to the park.

A really wonderful surprise - we were actually looking for a different park, but this was terrific.  A very pleasant boardwalk paralleling the cliff (although for some reason they had a number of bench sittings fenced-off).  A tori gate then leads down three flights of steps to the shore where there are beautiful wave-swept stone and cliff inlets.  A modest amount of parking.  The location can't be beat as you are mere blocks away from the pier with its wonderful shops and restaurants (you may even see a sign posted saying "Peter stopped by here" unless someone took it down).  Santa Barbara at its best: the sand, the surf, the breezes!

This is a park that stretches along the coast in Santa Barbara.  It is beautiful (of course) and it is  a nice greenbelt good for enjoying in many capacities; jogging, biking, playing games, children, etc ...

Convenient parking.  Nice open spaces.  And an incredible view.

I'm not on the west coast very often, and not only did I want to watch the sunset, I wanted to find the *best* place to watch the sunset! So Google led me to Shoreline Park.

It was pretty easy to get to by car, and there is plenty of free parking. There's a path that lots of people were jogging or walking their dogs on; tons of benches to sit and watch the ocean; a few playgrounds for the kiddos. It wasn't overcrowded at all, but it wasn't creepy empty or anything. Seemed like a lot of people were like me and just came to watch the sunset. 

Very beautiful sunset and very pretty park!

ooo i love cliffs! honestly though, i prefer PV, but this is nice too, and parking was easy. also easy to find. no need to walk a mile like most cliffs. you get to see channel islands, the beaches, and nice houses are behind you, there's also grassy areas, with plenty of benches

tip: climb over the fence. just doooo it! at your own risk

favourites: nice cliff view

do not want: too many sails, too many people, kinda ruins it i think =p

last words: great cliff, great view

Amazing, wonderful, relaxing. Those are the 3 words that come to mind about this place. The weather today was perfect: slight breeze, clear blue sky, around 75F...absolutely perfect to spend the day lazying away on one of the benches with a nice drink in hand, looking onto the ocean. I would definitely recommend to at least visit this park once.

A beautiful park with a walking/biking path right on the coast, this park is a small piece of paradise. 

The family walked the path for a while, then found a great kid's area with slides and swings and a sand foundation and we were a happy clan. I'm sure even my little 14-month old was like "Damn, I could live in Santa Barbara!" 

We found free parking quickly and were still only a stones-throw from a more congested area, so we easily could have just kept walking until we hit the shopping district and made a day of it. 

A great place. Anyone from Santa Barbara wanna hire me?
We biked the 5 mile bike trail to the beach. The ocean view throughout the trail was splendid.

I come here at least 3 times a week.  The park on the cliff is pretty cool but my favorite part is the beach beneath it.  Near the restrooms are stairs leading down to the beach.  Walking to the left at the bottom of the stairs takes you a short distance to Ledbetter beach.  I prefer to walk to the right.  When the tide is low enough you can walk all the way to Hendry's from here.  I love coming here on weekday mornings because it is less crowded and cleaner than Hendry's and you can still have your dog off the leash.  The tidepools are pretty cool to check out during super low tide too.

The kids park itself is a 1 but the location is a 10! We opted to go to Shoreline Beach for sunset and low tide. Which is a short walk away. Beautiful views. Skip the kids park though, it's small and not well set up for kids over age of 3

Nice open park with excellent view of the ocean. 

Located up a small hill.

Dog friendly. Must be on a leash because the street is right next to it.

Very clean.

such an amazing beach with a great view! The sunsets here are outstanding and they will stay with you forever. Whenever I'm having a tough day, I drive up to here and park and walk up to the highest place and just sit and watch the waves crash until the sun finally sets. its extremely soothing.

This is a gorgeous park- you'll be amazed! Peak over the high cliffs at little coves below, look out over Santa Barbara harbor, and sit on a whale's tail bench. Toddlers will like the playground. There are many joggers who enjoy the long, winding path and the slight incline.

Want to enjoy a day at the park in Santa Barbara next to the ocean? Of course you do!  Here's a step-by-step guide on how to enjoy a beautiful SB coastline.

1. Grab a blanket and a picnic basket! 
2. Commence Relaxation!
(How easy was that?!?!)

There are some built-in charcoal BBQ spots scattered throughout the park. You can also bring out your own hibachi which is what we normally do to enjoy some good food in the sun. We usually enjoy bringing a Frisbee and/or football to keep ourselves occupied especially on the gloomier days. On the clear sunny days the views are breathtaking and the photo opps are endless.

If you're driving to the park you'll discover that the parking is free. This park is also very accessible for bicyclist (although if you're coming from DT then you have a small hill to trek up). You'll encounter a variety of patrons at this park. Runners, dog walkers, people who stroll, people w/strollers, families, bike riders, etc. All great for people watching.  

Shoreline Park is a great place to enjoy the SB weather and ocean without all of the crowds and tourist. If you have a couple of hours to kill or have a good book to read then this might be a place for you. Best part about it is you can enjoy the ocean views without getting sand in your car :)
It's a good place to go jogging. Get off the streets and disappear here. Great views. A quiet getaway in.

This is a beautiful park to have a Bbq in, play some ball, walk your dog, or just read a book. There are tons of spaces to park and it is free! 

The only con is that the bathrooms don't have doors!! Eeek! :/ I had to take my chances and hoped no one walked in the bathroom!

I made a stop at Shoreline Park last month! It was nice! I like the walking paths! I love the backdrop of mountains! I like the paths leading down to the beach! It was nice looking at all the sailboats in the water! I'd definitely walk my dog here!

Perfect place for a picnic! I would suggest hitting up Trader Joe's (they have disposable utensils for you if you ask!) for some cheese/cold cuts/bread and heading over to Shoreline Park for a nice view.

Parking is free at the lot, but the neighborhood also has plenty of parking. Just be sure to watch out for the permit only signs!

I would consider this one of the best views of Santa Barbara. A definite must for a day trip! :D

Imagine a small, dog-friendly parcel of land overlooking the ocean with a smattering of benches and trees, lots of green grass, a cemented walk-skate-bike path, FREE parking, somewhat off the beaten path from the hustle and bustle of the more touristy parts of Santa Barbara, not overly crowded, with a playground to boot, and you've got yourself Shoreline Park.  My husband and I know this park because a friend of his lives quite close to that park, but as with all of Santa Barbara, it'd be easy to find for anyone.  It's a lovely, lovely park, and it's so nice to sit on the grass or one of the park's many benches and stare at the ocean, the people and dogs walking by, or just drift off with the mild sea breeze.  So very relaxing.  I wouldn't be upset to live in THIS neighborhood.

Beautiful view of the islands and if you feel like taking a casual stroll, head on down the steps and enjoy the beach!  

Parking is free...!
My favorite park in Santa Barbara! Great views of the ocean, lots of dogs, and walking paths! Good looking palm trees and well maintained lawns! Fresh air and calm environment by the ocean!

My boyfriend and I stumbled upon this park and beach while visiting SB and we loved it! There was plenty of open space and amazing views. The beach was basically empty, which is incomprehensible because it's gorgeous! We did have the impression that it was a dog beach, but we didn't even care because it was pristine and isolated. It's perfect for a picnic or bbq in the park.

Really nice coast side park and shortly away from the bustle of Sterns Wharf.  I stopped by here after taking some pictures at the Harbor just because it had such high reviews on Yelp.  The park itself is very clean, open, and the weather was beautiful when I stopped by.  There is green grass and a walkway that goes through and benches overlooking the water, so the park is basically a sea cliff.  If you can't find parking in the lots there are plenty of residential side street with no restricted parking. 

I think the impressive part of the park is that there is a stairway down to the rather private short beach section.  I took a look down and there were some pretty unique shore rocks in some sections that made it different.  If I'd more time I would have taken some photographs down there but I had to make my way up to Solvang soon after this stop.  Good place to check out if you are in the area or passing by.

Great place to watch the sunset and walk off your dinner on the walking path. Don't have to pay for parking so I imagine in the summer this place is PACKED! You'll see a lot of the locals walking their doggies here. Right along the beach, I climbed rocks to sit on so I could enjoy the shoreline breeze and ocean view =)

This park running alongside the coast at Santa Barbara is an absolute beauty and this find has to be all thanks to Yelp. We wanted to catch the sunset and I checked yelp to see the best beach to do so. And oh my this was the best sunset I have ever witnessed, atleast in recent times. Luckily the sky was clear and not even a single cloud to mar the vision of the the sun. The park is clean, a very nice place to picnic. The toilets were also clean when I used them, which is oh so important. All in All, an entirely delightful place.

There's a lot of parks in Santa Barbara and most of them have people without homes camping, sleeping, drugging, etc.... in them which makes impossible to bring your family unless you like to mix it up with Hobo's and your children. Shoreline Park is clean, green, great view of the ocean and surfing, windsurfing, great place for kids, great place for dogs,. Did I say,. it's also close to Leadbetter Beach where there is not much surfing but it's a great Beach for it's views and swimming and barbecues but you do have some hobos. "A hobo is a migrant worker or homeless vagrant, especially one who is impoverished. The term originated in the Western--probably Northwestern--United States around 1890"

I just stumbled upon this park while cruising around the Santa Barbara shore. It looked like it had a great view of the Marina, so I stopped by. 
Unlike just about all parks along the shore in Los Angeles/Orange County, you don't have to pay to park! No meters or outrageous fees! Just park and enjoy the beauty of the park!
I walked around to the edges of the park and the vistas were great. It was a beautiful pre-noon morning. You can see the whole Marina and then some. I stood there and soaked in the sights. I watched the boats moving around, divers diving into the kelp beds, some dude paddle surfing, people playing on the shore, just a very serene setting.
As I explored the park some more I came across a staircase going down to the water. I walked down and it was sort of High Tide, so I didn't walk too far along the shore. I took a few pics as I dodged the water rolling up the sand. 
I walked back to my car and really took notice of how clean the park was. No graffiti, green grass and restrooms that didn't look like you'd get mugged or raped in. Just a nice park to hangout, jog, walk your dog or have a picnic. 
A good place to come and enjoy the beauty of Santa Barbara.

We took a beautiful drive up and down the main road along the park and it is so beautiful here. So green. Nice restaurants all along/across the street. People were in the park with their hammocks, leashed dogs and loved ones. Saw weddings happening in the park just before sundown. People soaking up the sun, some playing sports, running, biking, walking low tight ropes. I would love to come back and spend a full day or 2 here. Of course, there are many hotels across the street from the beach to cater to tourists.
There were a lot of people out but it was Sunday, pretty quiet. Lots of families.
I had only been here once before to walk the pier on the beach and it was just as impressive this time seeing it from the street. There seemed to be lots of parking available.
100%, we will be coming back here. Good vibes happening and I was to try some of the seafood around here at the nearby fish restaurants.

If you want a public "private" beach drive to shoreline park!

it's free parking and you can either sit up on the grass area or continue along the path cross the playground to some stairs that bring you down to the beach, walk along the beach until you find your spot, this was the cleanest, most isolated beach ive come across so far!

Nothing in SB is worth 5stars!

Happy hunting
yerts and the lake,the best getaway

OMG. To have a long park on the cliff overlooking the ocean and the Santa Barbara pier and a sweeping view of the beaches and cruise ships bringing tourists to Santa Barbara and little do they know the sweet part hides about a mile away . It is a busy park with lots of walkers at least when I was there. There seems to be plenty of parking. Restrooms are so handy to have their. As not all the parks have them. I was lucky to catch a sunset here it is memorable very memorable. So don't tell everyone and will have the park to ourselves. Oh No this yelp review is public. Now the word is out. See you there

Such a lovely place to walk around! I saw a lot of people walking their dogs which was pretty neat. The weather was amazing and it's a great place to take pictures. There are tables to even have lunch.
Cherished childhood memories before the corrupt left liberal scum raised our rent and forced us out.

This is my go to spot when the tide is too high to walk on the beach. It is also my daughters favorite park to play with and we always meet locals and tourists relaxing on the swings, slides, or bench for parents to check the view and their kids. Easy parking and great sunset views to boot! Who can beat that?

Beautiful views, but unavoidable fence. Nice ocean pictures and lavender-colored flowers. Plenty of parking. Good for kids, pets, etc. Gets cold in May so bring a jacket for SURE! Besides the fence... Beautiful. I suppose fence is unavoidable because you can't let people just jump off the cliffs LOL

Beautiful views of the shore. Rode my bike up the steep hill to see the stunning scenery. No riding of bikes is allowed in the park area so I parked mine by a tree and walked around. Lots of lookout points and benches. Dog friendly park for everyone to enjoy.

Really, really pretty beach! Great photo opportunities here, so bring a camera ;) I arrived during a local running marathon, I believe, so it was interesting to see all the runners strut their stuff!  Beautiful sea cliffs, rocks, and trees. The top part of the park was really nice with soft, green grass with very little trash to be found.

The only setbacks were all the flies hiding in the seaweed on the beach and the signs warning visitors about danger of a suspected shark in the area.

It's a very pretty cliffside park with a nice walk path down to the beach.

Parking, when available, is super easy and free.

A great place to stop and take in the beautiful views.

This was definitely an unexpected treat! My BF and I took our mothers to Solvang for Mother's Day this past weekend and drove through Santa Barbara for nostalgia sake. My mother kept suggesting this spot but I was on the fence. After visiting I thanked her for the great suggestion. Stairs are in abundance but a fairly easy climb. Dogs are allowed. Sitting at a bench in the sun while admiring the view? Don't miss it.

A guy walked by and commented, "Enjoying the view? There's probably no where else you'd rather be in the whole world than here...." Eh. I mean we are from Redondo Beach and there are definitely similar, more rugged spots nearby me in Palos Verdes . Not to mention we have a view of Catalina Island and coastlines galore but I politely smiled. LOL Shoreline Park is a hidden gem but I could think of a hand full of other places I'd enjoy just as much if not more closer home. I'm glad we stopped though and you will be too.

Stopped here for lunch with our boxer Lucy. There are plenty of tables to eat at with trash bins right next to them. The view you can't complain. Every table overlooks the ocean. We are planning on traveling soon with Lucy and wanted to take some quick trips to see how'd she'd react since she lost her brother a month ago. There are dogs all around but all are on leashes. Plenty of people walking on the pathway. For me being in Santa Barbara the big perk is free parking!

I can't believe that I lived in Santa Barbara for 6 years and only found this park a few months ago. It is a perfect place for a walk. I come here every weekend with my pup. It is a good 30-40 minute walk. The views are phenomenal! The park is often busy but still is rather peaceful. The only down side is the parking a bit limited because it is free. However, I can always find parking on the nearby streets if necessary.
Very nice area! Lots of grass to play on! Endless beaches!! Ship to play on! Bathrooms!

What a gorgeous, cliffside park :) Stop #2 on the Spring Break 2016 Doggie Road Trip...

I had really hoped to be able to take Roxy to the beach down the steps below, but 1) it was already crowded with other dogs down there, and 2) it was slightly overcast/chilly. Major brownie points for the overall cleanliness of the park and clean public restrooms as well where I could take Roxy in with me!

Easy parking, easy access, friendly folks (omg a family was setting up a Santa Maria style bbq rig that smelled amaaazing!) and wide open spaces/sidewalks/grassy areas. Just beautiful views.

Roxy rates it: Three happy smiley paws up :)
Absolutely beautiful, never seen anything like it! You MUST go here if you're ever in Santa Barbara!

Great free place to enjoy the SB beaches. You get a beautiful view of the ocean while walking along the path. There is a place to go down to the beach with a Japanese style gate but it's in the middle.

 I didn't really see the beautiful sunsets that others have posted since it came down on the other side....Maybe those are sunrise pictures? 

Oh and did I mention free parking? Yep.

Great park to stop and take a break, on your way to Northern California. 
Beautiful views of ocean. Good playground equipment for kids. Safe place to walk dogs. 
We pickup lunch at local restaurants and eat at park. Plenty of picnic tables and bathrooms.

I discovered this place by accident about 17 years ago and always stop by whenever I'm in SB.  My initial reason for coming here was for free parking by the ocean but it's actually a pretty peaceful place.  When I was a cheesy goth I used to come here just to brood and write bad songs.  Now I just come to clear my mind. I don't see as many people around this area because most people are parked by the harbor or by the pier.  The parking lot isn't huge but I never had trouble finding a spot.  There's no bbq grills, swings, or anything but you can get a great view of the ocean and has a wide open grass area to just chill.

For Mother's Day, we went to Lilly's Taco, ordered our food and went to this park and had a nice lunch! Great park, lots of public tables and free parking! Great view of the ocean and it was fun to people and dog watch!!
Shoreline Park is a nice & quite park with beautiful views of the coast.  We'll go back.

Shoreline is one of the most beautiful park of the city. It's located upon the cliffs of the Mesa and gives you an amazing view over Santa Barbara, the mountains and the harbor. Perfect place to end up your day and watch the sunset while having a walk.

Small, chill park to casually stroll along. Some people were biking along the path, but it's honestly a very short park that seems more like a family-friendly place that locals come to - and not exactly a tourist destination. 

For the most part, you're walking next to a gate because you're on a cliff, but there is a staircase that leads down to the ocean. It's a short walk down to soft warm sand and the waves. Plenty of people were just lounging around the large rocks, soaking in that California October winter of 80 degrees. 

A good place to stretch out your legs and for a relaxed pit-stop in your journey!
We come here with our dog all the time and it's always so peaceful and great to people-watch. It feels like a hidden gem in the coast above the water,  and not as busy as down by the wharf.

Definitely amongst my favorite locations as a tourist in Santa Barbara. If you visit this place on a good day, you're bound to get some amazing views of the Pacific. If you're even luckier, you'll get to witness a ton of Dolphins with a clear view without needing any equipment. 

I came here with my partner for a walk and we were thrilled to experience multiple sightings of dolphins. If you're into surfing then the beach is a 5 minute walk and is a surfer's paradise for sure.

Free parking at La Marina. 

Take a walk to the grassy area or to the beach and enjoy the day.  You can also go for a run with the beach view.

This is a nice and quiet park along the CA coast. They have two small parking lots, both near restrooms, with a nice view of the water along the entire park. In between the two lots is a stairwell that apparently leads down to a small beach, but the tide was high when I went down so all I found was a tiiiiiiiny bit of sand and rocks with waves crashing in. I can see this secluded area being really nice when the tide isn't so high.

All along the park are benches for people to sit and enjoy the view, although the placement of some of the benches was odd since some benches were right behind a tree so your view of the ocean is completely obscured. There is also a small playground and a couple areas for grilling. This would definitely be a great spot to hang out if you lived in the area. I saw a lot of people running/jogging through the park and also a lot of people with their dogs. I tell myself that if I lived in an area like this I would definitely be more active, but who am I kidding... :P

The park is just a short ways away from Stearns Wharf and Brophy Bros. and that whole area, and if it's on your way it's worth stopping by to relax and enjoy the ocean breeze.

Dog friendly!

Beautiful long park with walking paths through grass and with views along the coast. There are even steps down to the beach here! There's also a children's playground if you're into that sort of thing (children). 

There are 2 free small parking lots on either end of the park, both of which get very full during the weekend. 

The park itself does not have much shade, so you may want to bring your own EZ-ups or umbrellas if you plan on hanging out for a while. At the East end of the beach however, is a perfect shady spot with a bench but its surrounded by wood chips.

Love it, Love it, Love it.  Even on a foggy-ish day, it's beautiful here.  We get spoiled here, or maybe I just feel that way because I head home to MI in less than a week.  Loads of folks here using the park all of the time.  It's probably closed at sunset like most city parks but that wouldn't stop the walkers and joggers and cyclists and dogwalkers from strolling through.  Great location right near SBCC and the Mesa neighborhood, close enough to downtown.  Santa Barbara feels like a small town at a park like this, because there are lots of friendly folks and touristas snapping their photos, there are plenty of photo-friendly scenes so locals get in on that action too.

Well-maintained park with the best of views. 

Regardless of the time of day, Shoreline Park is a beautiful and serene space to come to. The park itself spans several blocks and is comprised of a large green belt anchored by two smallish parking lots. The lots will typically fill up during the day but you'll find a spot if you're on the lookout.
