I've frequented this park for family gatherings, an Easter egg hunt as a kid, and on Sunday mornings to enjoy my coffee with an ocean view. It butts up to the ocean and views can be seen from all angles. The ocean breeze is refreshing and it's the ideal spot to relax and recharge. 

There's also a walking path (bikes aren't permitted) which meanders from one end of the park to the other. It's level and mild, and connects to Leadbetter Beach and eventually West/East Beaches. I've walked it from the western tip of Shoreline Park to the Bird Refuge and back - an invigorating 7 mile trek. 

Check this park out if you're looking for a peaceful spot to see the ocean.

What can I say.. I love this place! My husband and I were married on this very beach 15 years ago because of the beauty it holds. 

Stunning cliffs, spectacular tidepools and amazing views make this one of our favorite beaches on SoCal.

These beautiful views can be enjoyed with a quick bike ride or walk up from the main boardwalk. Definitely worth the views of crystal blue water, nice sandy beaches, and the occasional paddle border or surfer. There are plenty of benches in a large lawn as well.

I would highly recommend renting a bike from the bike shop near the boardwalk-- around $11 per hour, and heading up towards this park for a great view!

This is a nice place to walk your dog.  It has a sidewalk that runs along the top of a cliff with a chain link fence.  There are nice flat grassy areas on the other side, where people were having large picnics, reading and training dogs.  I brought my German Shepherd and we walked along the sidewalk at sunset.  There were many dogs, mostly smaller who were not friendly and scared my boy some.  He is a gentle soul and dogs being mean to him confuses him.  The park offers a nice elevated view of the ocean, but there is no easy access down to it.  A big plus to this park is the ease of parking; pulling right into a free lot, off the road is very appealing.
Great place to sit and have a picnic. I had read online that it was a good stop for whale watching, but none while we were there.

Honestly so beautiful! There's a good amount of parking in the parking lot. I walked down to the beach area to take a stroll on the sand. The sand is very flat and easy to walk on and dog friendly!! It was very clean and the waters were very calm in August. It was so lovely. It was very peaceful as there weren't many people and everyone was very far from each other like about 100 feet away from each other. No trash either! Yay! I'll definitely be back every time I'm in the area!

My favorite place to park my car when touring Santa Barbara.  

It's a great park on a cliff overlooking the Pacific. 
What more could you want?

Watch out for poop all over the grass--people let their poop factories run all over and take big dumps.  Who knows how many people clean it up?  You don't want to come home with smelly shoes.

Oh well what can I say  about this place 
Mmmmm so much start with a time that I been here numerous times  since 2003 yes I can't belive it is been 16 years  ago wow!
This us an unforgettable  place start with the amazing view you have all the ocean,  the waves ,the sound of the birds, the sound  if the waves,very cool people good drinks good appetizer  and great great breakfast my favorite is 
CHILAQUILES here is the best chilaquiles on the whole Santa Barbara I promise come and try it.
My mother and I love walking this beach. It's beautiful, easy to navigate and safe. It's often crowded but still feels roomy.

Beautiful and clean park, very breezy. The restrooms are a bit far and apart.

Came here a couple times for picnics and  potlucks. The tables are a bit awkwardly placed throughout the park but the views are incredible. Because of the altitude of the park, it can get really really windy. Make sure to bring weights to hold all your stuff down and some jackets when it isn't summer.

A park ... with a view.

The bluffs along the coast of Santa Barbara are home to this free public park offering a sea breeze stroll ... with a view. 

If you are a tourist, it is an easy and cheap way to see the Pacific Ocean. If you are a local, it is a coastal park with free parking. Either way, Shoreline Park is popular ... with a view.

The park is nearly 15 acres stretching west of Leadbetter Beach along Shoreline Drive. A paved sidewalk with park benches is perfect for a relaxing walk above the water while beach access stairs provide the opportunity get even closer. Whether you are looking for a place to look back at the city, gaze up at the Santa Ynez Mountains, or take in the Santa Barbara coastline, this is the place ... with a view.

Alcohol is prohibited, but dogs are allowed on a leash. A playground for kids is underutilized as are the barbeques and picnic tables. If you want my advice, bring someone special to Shoreline Park for an afternoon stroll and quiet ocean side picnic ... with a view.

Where you and your dogs and you can roam free by far My best find yet great balance of country and city beautiful for your furry friends tents and hiking everywhere great food wonderful hippie style soul people that offer you peace wherever you go

Shoreline Park is one of my top recommendation spots for family Barbecues and get togethers because of it's wide open grass space areas and BBQ pits. It's an awesome location with a gorgeous view and pretty ample amounts of public parking. There's also a beautiful beach accessible at the park in between the two main parking lots. Beach -BBQ - grass areas -kiddie playgrounds - parking... Perfect family meet up spot! 

If you looking at taking a walk to clear your head but don't want to get sandy or wind blown at the beach, this is an awesome spot as well. I would recommend parking at the northern end of Shoreline Park and walking south as the view is a lot better that way and you will get a more relaxing and rejuvenating experience out of that. 

The closest beaches nearby are the one I mentioned above and Leadbetter Beach. This is a great spot to have a picnic at or between classes at SBCC or watch the sunset after class on date night. Easy walkable access from the SBCC campus and completely free.. Can't argue with that on a student budget! 

Parking, as mentioned above, is decently easy to find. There are two private lots, on street parking, and neighborhood parking across the street. I have yet to have a problem finding a spot even on the busiest of Saturdays. 

Give it a shot! Plan a family football match, load some hot dogs on the grill, and watch the sunset fall over the water creating a beautiful memory for all in this beautiful, local park.
This spot is great for families and dogs. There is a beach access for a more isolated walk. Would definitely come back on off time when there's no crowd.

A long, narrow stretch of park located atop an oceanside bluff, Shoreline Park is also known as the "mesa." Quite a scenic one at that.

The park itself offers a nice, long, uninterrupted footpath for runners, walkers, or bicyclists. Don't get too distracted by the views, whether of the ocean or shirtless variety, lest you collide into others. While mostly narrow, the park is wide enough at certain places to accommodate picnic areas, play structures, sculptures, and overlooks. A Japanese-style gate opens onto a staircase providing access to the beach down underneath.

It's undoubtedly a popular, scenic spot, so parking at one of the lots is going to be super crowded. When I stopped by, I was fortunate to find a parking space right in front of a bunch of fun folks who were engaging in a pickup game on a compact open lawn. So be prepared to circle around area streets and whatever streetside parking there is along the perimeter.

So relaxing.

I'm not sure how many times I've dozed off laying in the grass here.

It's up on the mesa, just above West Beach.  It is an easy walk from the Harbor but it is uphill.  If you're feeling lazy, there's plenty of parking.

There is plenty of room to exercise, jog, play frisbee, etc. but my favorite thing to do is relax and just enjoy the scenery.  The waves down on the shore below are usually mild and consistent so it is a great spot for surfers and many people go there to learn.  I just sit up on the cliff and watch.

I enjoy sunsets here and I enjoy watching the city & the harbor get dark and see the lights coming on, even the lights on the oil rigs out in the Santa Barbara Channel.  If you are looking at the city, the sun will set behind you (because you are looking east).

There is beach access but since the park is up on the cliff, you've got a lot of stairs to walk down.

What a great park to chill and take in the SB vibe. What an awesome park at the top of the mess over looking the ocean and Wet Beach, Sterns Wharf and East Beach all to the east on this east to west jog along the coast. So lucky to have such amazing weather on a recent visit. The restrooms are very clean and the grounds are so well kept. We enjoyed a couple beverages and played a few games of cribbage while taking in the views and the ever refreshing costal air. Parking can be rough but we lucked upon a spot rather quickly on a late morning during the week. The walk along the pathway is so beautiful and truly envelopes that central coast experience. 

Much gratitude to the SN city staff that work so diligently to kept this and other parks clean and picked up for guests and locals to enjoy. In my college days and now the park system in Santa Barbara has always been top notch in comparison to so many other cities.

Great place to watch the sunset and walk off your dinner on the walking path. Don't have to pay for parking so I imagine in the summer this place is PACKED! You'll see a lot of the locals walking their doggies here. Right along the beach, I climbed rocks to sit on so I could enjoy the shoreline breeze and ocean view =)

name: Manning Park
formatted_address: 449 San Ysidro Rd, Montecito, CA 93108, USA

google_reviews.text: This is one of the most beautiful parks I've been to! My daughter in law reserved area 6 for my Grandson's 3rd birthday. The park ranger came by to greet us as we were decorating, and he let us know where the bathrooms were, the water spigot, and showed us the beautiful shaded meadow for our games. He wished us a wonderful time and asked if we had any questions. All of our friends and family had a blast! Everyone commented on the beauty of this park. How clean and safe and green! I will remember that day forever! And I'm sure all of us will be back.
Such a beautiful park with gorgeous foliage and walkways. Went for a baby shower and it was absolutely perfect there.

great place to sit and have lunch. plenty of benches and shade. if all the shady parking is taken up by pest control trucks and pool guys just go to one of the many benches and enjoy the beautiful quite because everyone here is sitting in their cars.
Best place to stop and have a quick picnic while you're out shopping.

Well taken care of.
Old school park.
Bathroom across from the school track.

yelp_reviews.text: Really cool park a little ways off the beaten path in south Santa Barbara.  I came here with my friend and her dogs (on-leash only) and was pleased to find there was a lot of green grass, trees for shade, picnic tables, areas you can reserve for parties, and so forth.  Several parking lots depending on which part of the park you're going to.  Actually the park has a couple sections on either side of San Ysidro Road.  Really beautiful and idealic.  We're planning on coming here again to relax and hang out!

Lovely park....it's actually two parks. One is known as Upper Manning and the other as Lower Manning. Lower Manning is a large open park. Upper Manning has multiple locations for little BBQ picnics etc.
I love this park is amazing very clean very beautiful for family gathering park rangers are very attentive this area is beautiful great playgrounds for the kids

Beautiful!  

If you have kids, go across street to day use picnic area where there's a little park as well.

That bench that you see in the picture is dedicated to my great grandma so i have to right a review right? Ive been coming here since i was 2 and it has served me well for many purposes. Making out with girls, skateboarding in the parking lot, shortcut home from school, and stashing my moonshine just to name a few. Just dont piss off the park ranger guy

This park is great. No hobos or drug addicts walking around, no riff raff, etc. Just a clean family friendly park in a rich neighborhood. Note: there are no swings or playground equipment, this isn't that kind of park.

It's a beautiful park.  Once a private estate.  My girlfriend and I joined other friends for a wedding in the park.  It was a very informal wedding accompanied by the bride and groom's dog and many others, including my girlfriends.  They are allowed at the park as long as they're on a leash.

They have bathroom facilities, picnic tables, plenty of shade under large beautiful trees, and a grassy knoll.  Plenty of parking and just off the 101 in Montecito.  Great for picnics, a kid's bday party, or a place for a quick exploration as the park isn't that big.  A very beautiful area in general!

name: Campus Point Beach
formatted_address: Campus Point Beach, Lagoon Rd, University of California-Santa Barbara, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Nice walk and beautiful sights! The path I chose from the apartments was mostly paved with only a small sand strip halfway through the walk as you get closer to the lagoon. The pictures are from an early morning walk in January.

This had got to be THE primo UC location, and I wonder how any students made up their minds to go to UCSB because of easy access to this beach. When we came on a mid Jan afternoon, there was hardly anybody here. Beautiful.

Absolutely Awesome. I enjoyed the Welcome Reef Center. Very friendly and helpful student's explaining the sea life. The beach is nice. Tide pools as you walk further north on the sand. I recommend this place. However if you park your car. Make sure you follow the rules as to where you park. Nobody wants a ticket. On a recent visit I got a $48 ticket. But it was dismissed. They failed to notice my handicap placard.
Beautiful beach at UCSB with nice waves, tide pools teeming with life, and stunning ocean views. Totally worth the parking fee!

We walked here from the neighboring beach with free parking because we weren't sure about the campus parking. Still, there was a nice beach, rocky bits, and cliffside area. There were some trails that wrapped around that I hope to go back to explore as well.

yelp_reviews.text: One of my favorite spots on campus to relax. The atmosphere is beautiful when you're surrounded by water from the ocean to the lagoon. This is a great place to spend sunset or sunrise.

This is the beach that's seaside to the lagoon, where you have the Marine Science Institute on one side, and Manzanita side on the other. It's wheelchair accessible from the Manzanita side, while the Marine Science Institute is also wheelchair accessible and it's closer to the parking lot. Most of the surfers hang out on the MSI side, while the Manzanita side is much less crowded due to its distance from the lot. There are some really nice trails that connect the two sides, so enjoy some nice walking as long as you can climb stairs. 

Just note that you may or may not have tar on your feet if you step in the water here. This beach sadly, is still heavily affected by it.

We probably missed some views due to the weather; but still a gorgeous place!!  try to come before 2-3pm before the high tide comes so you can hopefully get to walk on the beach  instead of taking photos from the top like w did!!

Overlooking the ocean on the cliffs is one of my favorite spots to study at UCSB. There are benches for people to sit on and it's generally very quiet and relaxing. One of the best views on the campus! The more you walk to the right of the cliffs, you will find the Labyrinth! You have to do the Labyrinth when you come to UCSB, you will feel so accomplished! 

Right before you enter the beach, there is UCSB's Reef. You can come here and touch a variety of sea creatures from, starfish to fish, they have it all! Super super cool experience! 

Depending on the day and the tides, this beach and Sands is the best to get your surf on in Goleta. You can always rent paddle boards, wet suits, kayaks and surfboards from UCSB's Recreation Center right off the water! It ranges from $10-$30 an hour depending on what you're renting or $30-$60 all day. Check out the link for more information! 

https://recreation.ucsb.edu/sites/default/files/2020-01/ucsb-campus-point-rentals-2016_bc7fd9fa-9062-40ab-86df-e405787716cf.pdf.pdf

This review is not so much for the beach, but for the walk around the campus lagoon. If you park at Lot 6 and get a coastal permit, you can either walk straight to Coastal point beach, or there is also a path to the right of the parking lot that also circles around the lagoon. It's quiet, peaceful, with great views, and mostly flat. The path is mostly paved with some mildly sandy portions. It's nice that the students at UCSB have such easy access to such views! 

The lagoon island labyrinth is also along the way, which looks cool, but wasn't that enjoyable in practice. Probably because we went into it thinking it was a maze, but actually it's just a contemplative path where there's only one was to complete it. Apparently it's 0.6 miles in total to walk the whole thing. Would be good for introspection, but you also have the bluffs overlooking the ocean for that.

There are some benches up the steps next to campus point beach that overlook the ocean which is nice.

Do yourself a favor and grab some Freebirds after!

This is truly iconic Central California! I come here to escape the daily stresses and observe gorgeous views. For me, Campus Point is just as postcard worthy as the Santa Barbara Harbor or Mission. You cannot beat watching the surfers (look up surf/tide hours online), shorebirds, waves, and boats, plus if you are lucky you may see dolphin pods or whales. Although the peninsular faces south versus west, the sky's palette of colors at sunrise/sunset are spectacular. Whenever folks visit, I am sure to bring them here. There are various hiking trails that lead to the bluffs, beach and lagoon--all extremely scenic with shadow views of the Channel Islands in the distance. It is not accessible to wheelchairs due to a staircase, but there are benches to rest.

A little history: This was once called Goleta Point and was used by Portuguese and Italians as a whaling station from 1870-1890. Later, when UCSB purchased the land in 1950, it contained a defunct United States Marine Corps Santa Barbara Air Station. When visiting at low tide, you can still see and walk on the building ruins to see starfish and sea anemones. Please keep them healthy by not touching them and be cautious of sticky natural tar that will stick to your feet.

If you are fortunate to be visiting on a Saturday between 11 am to 2 pm, be sure to stop in REEF (The Research Experience & Education Facility) created by the Marine Science Institute. They have humongous aquariums with all sorts of creatures from the Channel Islands National Park you can watch, touch and get up close and personal.

Curious what those odd large tarps are on the ground? These tarps are used to cover an invasive specie of plant known to locals as "Ice plants" (Aizoaceae Martynov) that have gone rampant. Yes, they are pretty when they bloom with yellow, orange, purple and pink flowers. Using tarps to deprive them of sunlight is a natural, chemical free method to kill them off.

The closest place to park a car is at the UCSB lot #6 (here are directions: https://goo.gl/maps/jPW2QArgS9Bv86g89). There is a "Coastal Access" permit fee required. However, if you don't mind extending your walk a bit with one large incline, park at the Goleta State Beach for free.

name: Leadbetter Beach
formatted_address: C832+3MG, 801 Shoreline Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93109, USA

google_reviews.text: Breathtaking Beach. Good vibes. Lots of people just enjoying their day out here. Plenty of restrooms and tables. Grills. Sitting areas with grass and shady palms. Incredible views all the way around. Accessible via bike trail and plenty of parking otherwise.
Very nice beach, I saw pelicans flying there, early morning 😊

Was really interesting landscape in the tide pools. Lots of sea urchins and hermit crabs. Love the the huge tree with tons of cormorants. I'll post some pics of them along with the review. Lots of traffic to this beach and there wasn't as much wildlife as i expected but still very with the trip.

Just had an amazing paddle session at Leadbetter Beach in Santa Barbara! If you’re looking for that perfect intermediate spot with easy beach access, this is it. We brought our Pau Hana Endurance boards and had a blast paddling through glassy waters, spotting dolphins in the kelp forests. The views of the coastline are incredible, and being only 1.5 hours from LA makes it a perfect day trip. Great for fitness paddling or just enjoying nature, but definitely bring your PFD and some paddling experience - this isn’t a complete beginner spot.

Location: Positioned north of the Marina, with East Beach to the south, Leadbetter Beach offers a quintessential Santa Barbara paddling experience. The beach provides stunning views of the entire coastline.

Accessibility: Incredibly convenient parking, just 20 feet from the sand. Easy gear transport with minimal hassle. The gentle slope of the beach makes for straightforward launching.
Conditions: During our visit, we enjoyed perfect paddling weather - winds under 5mph, air temperature around 70°, water in the low 60s. The glassy surface created mirror-like reflections, turning an ordinary paddle into something almost transcendent.

Skill Level: Best suited for intermediate paddlers. The open ocean setting requires some experience reading swells and navigating light breaks. This isn’t your protected beginner’s pond - you’ll need to understand basic ocean dynamics.

Board Recommendations: Pau Hana Endurance VFT and Endurance Ricochet performed beautifully here. Their displacement hulls cut efficiently through the water while maintaining stability - ideal for both fitness paddling and nature exploration.

Experience: Paddling north around the point reveals the full panorama of Santa Barbara’s beautiful coastline. We encountered dolphins and fish weaving through kelp forests - the kind of California paddling experience people dream about. We paddled north specifically to have the wind at our backs on the return trip. The rhythmic paddling, warm sun, and salt air create a natural escape from everyday stress.

Safety Considerations: Always bring a PFD and leash. Check wind and swell forecasts before heading out. Being open ocean, conditions can change, so maintain awareness of currents and wind direction.

Progression Path: Start at the protected marina for basics, graduate to Leadbetter for intermediate skills, then tackle East Beach if you’re seeking waves and surf.

Post-Paddle Amenities: Convenient outdoor showers for rinsing off. Walking distance to excellent State Street restaurants and cafes - the perfect way to refuel after a session on the water.

Proximity: Only 1.5 hours from Los Angeles, making it an ideal day trip destination when city life becomes too much.

This spot delivers that rare combination of accessibility and authenticity that’s increasingly hard to find in popular coastal towns. Well worth the drive.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Great place to kick back and take in the beautiful ocean and islands, go for a long walk, get wet, kayak, or sit and have a quick bite to eat and a drink. From here, you can take a nice long walk up along connecting Shoreline Park and get a better ocean view from the Mesa.

yelp_reviews.text: This beach is good for the waves and for its relation to State St. and local businesses but I give this beach 3 stars because the last time I came here (early June 2013) the sand was scorching hot, there was a bucket load of tar, and despite the fact that the beaches are cleaned for seaweed each day there was still a ton of seaweed on the beach. Plus there was a crazy amount of sandflies and sand fleas. Overall not the best beach I've been to in Santa Barbara.

Super cute beach , windy so nice to
Watch the wind surfers . The restaurant on the beach is really good food , reasonably priced and tasty . We sat inside and could see the beach well right by sand . Dog friendly for our pup . Great pit stop & place to eat with your dog !
Good for letting your dog run since is a pet friendly beach! Is not that big to find a spot though
Dirty beach, seaweed everywhere! I'm mean huge mounds of seaweed. And it smells! So far we've been here for 3 days and during this time the city has not bothered to clean it up. Very disappointing.

There are so many better beaches in either direction. Literally walk in either direction and enter the sand somewhere else. You won't regret it. Maybe if you're in between classes at CC and you don't have time to walk to the sandbar. Otherwise do yourself a favor and go somewhere else. I guarantee it will be less crowded and more beautiful.

We meant to go to the beach "next door", but my husband walked the wrong way and we ended up here. With a 4 year old in tow, we were not going to backtrack - although we should have.  The end of the beach where we were had lots of flies and clumps of seaweed everywhere.  There were 2 dead lobsters surrounded by flies as well.  Although not the worst that I have seen, it definitely was not nearly the best.  The sand was nice - not rocky or anything - it just needed to have seaweed and the like picked up.  The water seemed cleaned and my son and husband sat in the waves while watching a stork dive for fish over and over.  I won't be back to this particular beach, but I do look forward to other Santa Barbara beaches in the future.

I was coming to Santa Barbara for an orchid show and decided to devote some time to the beach. I did a quick Google search and found a ton of places that people have recommended but this one came out on top. When I initially plugged it into the GPS and found the parking lot I was disappointed because there was really not beach around. It was a cliff walk, a nice one with a gorgeous view with a park and all. Then I saw it... the staircase that went down. People were coming back up wet in bathing suits so I knew that this was the location. We went down and found this tiny strip of beach (high tide at the time) where people had managed to plant themselves. Not nearly as busy as the beach down the road and was a nice little secluded area that people have devoted their afternoon to. 

All in all, a great little find. It's about a 40 min walk from the main downtown so I opted to drive rather than walk but definitely doable. Plenty of parking and we came on a Saturday afternoon. Loved it!

I do agree with Joan H. this is one of the more crowded beaches but it's because of the location and how much they cater to crowds.  They have lots of picnic tables, bathrooms, and parking for people to use.  We came here to see this beach and it was a very sunny and beautiful day.  This beach would be great for groups and kids because it caters to them.

I really liked this beach and would come here again but if you're looking for something more low key I would not choose this beach.

The beach is very close by my friend's house. We had a wonderful time here for one of our best friend's baby shower today. Nice weather great beach plenty of food and drinks and an amazing vagina cake!! Such a nice beach to hand out with lovely people at this Labor Day weekend (^o^)/

Easy access and parking
Long beach which is good for walking
Open picnic areas with grills plus a large grouping for reservations
Can get very busy when you add up the sun baskers, surfers, SUPers family get-to-gethers and tourists. 
The kelp can get bad and you can smell it down wind
It's conveinant but there are better beaches in the area.

I never got a chance to experience this place simply because there was no parking. Came in on a Friday afternoon and drove 2 hours out of the way to see a dog friendly beach because apparently a lot of the Bay Area in California doesn't allow dogs. They had 2 parking lots but both were full it seemed with locals. Most of them were just simply sitting in their  s

It was very dirty with sea weed. The water was also filled with sea weed making it annoying to swim in. The beach was large with volley ball nets and lots of space for  you're daily beach activities but it's nothing special. There are much better beaches in the area.

Leadbetter Beach is one of the more crowded beaches in Santa Barbara.  It is located right near the harbor and is usually packed with lots of tourists and families.  

There is plenty of parking in the lot nearby.  The free lot allows up to 90 minutes, or you can choose to pay for parking and stay as long as you want.  Shoreline Cafe is close by if you get hungry or want a scoop or two of ice cream for a snack.  And there are plenty of restrooms and shower facilities for you to use if needed.

We headed here for a day of fun in the sun, and there was a rowing competition which was really fun to watch.  But with all of the people who beat us there, there wasn't much room for all of us to play volleyball or Frisbee.  There was also a lot of washed up seaweed, and we were too lazy to pick it up so we decided to head over to East Beach.  Overall, Leadbetter is a great beach, but really.. what beach isn't?

I like this beach. The sand is good, but there is a by of seaweed due to high tide. It doesn't smell bad, but the water is a bit murky at times. Parking isn't so great if you don't plan it and it can get a bit busy. Still, it's a solid option when you're looking for sun and sands. 

One thing...saltwater SUP can be dangerous!  I had my behind handed to me trying to get it out there on my own...toppled and terrorized. I had no idea that the waves came in irregular and thus did not judge the break well. I had a nice male bystander offer to help me finally get out there. 

I suggest if to your first time SUPing on this beach get some help and know the waves are irregular.

Stop over in Southern Calif., i can deal with the seaweed clumps but WATCH OUT for the TAR BALLS from previous oil spill, still Tar Balls everywhere, both my Wife and I step on them.  Tough to get off, needed to be scrubbed with a pad.  No signage warning of the Tar Balls!!!

Beach was a good place to relax after hitting up some wine tasting rooms in Santa Barbara, being about a mile or so away.  

We went on one of the quieter days so there weren't too many people around.  Perfect for watching some kitesurfing (plenty of that going on) and we even had a few dolphin sightings.  

Lots of dried seaweed (and bugs around the seaweed) that you pretty much have to cross if you want to get into the water.

Good parking and clean restrooms. Showers also available, if you don't mind washing off around bums.

My family had our reunion here at this beach and I was very impressed by the amount of space and for a Saturday it was a little crowded but everyone was so nice at the surrounding tables. They have a huge area where a wedding reception was taking place and I really love how close you are to the beach so you really get the picnic/beach experience. We had a wonderful time here!

Amazingly, gorgeous and stunning Southern California coastline. On thanksgiving weekend, 2018, this beach offered a peaceful, serene and quiet escape to the beach. It was clean and had plenty of parking. The sunset was beautiful and there are coves that help shelter you from the wind on breezy days.

This beach is decent. There's really never any good spots to lay since there's always an abundance of seaweed scattered all over. Usually empty on weekdays. Great place to let your dogs run though. Just watch out for dog poops everywhere!

Great beach. Come here early if you want parking. Although we did come during peak August and stayed through the day for $12. Plenty of parking not sure if it just wasn't busy or not like the crowded beaches of Santa Monica. The Shoreline cafe is right there. You can use theirs bathroom which is nice. You can stop in and have a drink but can't bring to the beach. Clean, not a long walk to water but the water itself is a bit muddy.

We hosted a 80-ish person BBQ at the large picnic area at the end of Leadbetter--what a great spot for friends and family to gather!  There are approximately 12 or so large tables, plenty of space for lawn games, and a shaded area under the big palm trees.  The concrete picnic area is adjacent to the sand, so unlike Shoreline park (which has particularly amazing views and a playground), Leadbetter provides easy access to the sand.  The restrooms are a tiny bit of a walk down the parking lot - about midway next to Shoreline Cafe.  Parking is a few bucks an hour, but you could definitely walk down from downtown or take an Uber.  We also used a Bose Bluetooth speaker to play music which was plenty loud for guests to enjoy.

So how do you throw an amazing party here?  Contact SB Parks and Recreation and ask them to reserve the spot.  The reservation covers the entire day and allows you to bring alcohol as long as it's not in glass.  We had a catering company bring a trailer BBQ - something like this you arrange with the Parking Authority.  In retrospect, it was all very simple and I think I worried way too much before hand.  For wedding events, birthday parties, etc...this is an awesome spot.

Please, remember to clean up your mess and keep Santa Barbara beautiful!

Love being able to walk across the street from SBCC to take a walk or jog along Leadbetter to the Mesa or to the Wharf or Harbor! This is a great beach to also bring a book, pull up.a chair or towel.and sun bathe. There aren't many waves so if you want to do ocean swimming or just wade in the water this is the spot!

I have no idea why people rave about this beach! My husband and I were walking at the harbor, on a lovely day, and I thought it would be nice to shed our shoes and go for a beach walk on what LOOKED like clean, soft sand. We walked from the harbor to the end of the beach, and back again. It was only when we sat down to put our shoes on that we noticed our feet were covered with thick layers of tar. Disgusting. The insides of my shoes were so messed up (I wasn't going to walk back to the hotel barefoot) that I had to throw them away. I am a coastal Californian by birth, and I live within spitting distance of a gorgeous, wild (clean) beach. I am used to enjoying a shoe-free stroll on the sand. I don't get the shrugging-off attitude of Santa Barbara residents when they say "it's natural seepage," as if there is nothing wrong with walking on a pool of hidden crude oil. Beachgoers were even swimming and running their dogs in that muck! It can't be good for the dogs. California Riviera indeed. I don't see the appeal. And the view of the rigs offshore? No thanks.
My favorite beach in Santa Barbara. My backyard... The surf here is good for all levels. Great for anyone just starting. And sailing alongside a dolphin can't be beat.

This beach is a nice place to sit and have lunch or even bbq since they have grills. It also has a cafe on the beach called shoreline Beach Café. It's a great spot to have breakfast on the beach literally on the beach toes in the sand and all. I highly recommend to anyone new to the area.

Enjoyed a nice job back and further the cafe is great and they have picnic areas with bbq pits. They have people wind surfing and it's a clean beach not a lot of homeless. They have some volleyball putts and a water way that runs into a little river. We love coming here dogs are allowed and I just love the beach honestly any beach I can hear the waves crash and take a nice barefoot jog is a ok with me - I was surprised how crowded it is, though.

GOOD GOLLY!! THE TIDE WAS HIGH!

BACKGROUND:
Leadbetter is an artificial beach, built by accident. When Santa Barbara Harbor was built, it blocked the downshore current, holding up the natural sand flow. The college is built on the original coastline's front cliffs, now about 30 yards offshore; the original beach front lies where the current SBCC football field is located. The blocking of sand from downshore cities prompted several lawsuits against Santa Barbara.

STATS: 
Short shoreline
Rocky in areas

OUR ROUTE:
After filling up at Los Arroyos in Montecito, me and The Crew head back to Santa Barbara, only this time by way of the beach. Walking back on this short shoreline at high tide as the sun was setting on this chilly day, made for a fun run from the cold ocean water as it rolled up and down the shore. 

While parts of the beach are sandy, others are rocky. Some of us walked the beach barefoot, while others with more sensitive feet kept their hiking shoes on. Walking in the sand and on the rocks for about five miles makes for a great glut workout and if you walk the sand barefoot, it's a great way to exfoliate the feet. 

There are stretches along the way where the beach is longer than others, however, be warned, there are a lot of peeps hanging out in these areas. In fact, when we were strolling, wedding decorations were going up for an Indian wedding. 

No lifeguards were called during our trek. The high tide wasn't really all that high.  I think a couple toes got wet from the incoming water. But by the end, they were all ok. 

If you are expecting for a beach to hang out on Leadbetter Beach is a pretty cool beach, especially if you have sand crabs (small fries) that like to race round. With the beach being relatively short, it's not overly hard to see the small ones. Nevertheless, if you're attending for a secluded spot to spend some romantic quality time with your other half, this is not for you.

OK beach, but there was A LOT of dried seaweed littering the sand, which made it difficult to find a spot to sit or lay down.
Nice views though. And it was fun watching the little sandpipers run around the tide. 

Just watch out for the seagulls! They are HUGE, aggressive, and not scared of humans in the least!

TAR Warning to all ! ! ! - - - SO Sad but true:((( 
Just came back from a wonderful weekend in Santa Barbara, great food, beautiful scenery, but what's the story w/ their Beaches? Spent the day yesterday on the beach, just N. of Leadbetter Beach, believe it's called 1,000 Steps Beach as we accessed the beach from Shoreline Drive & Santa Cruz and walked down the steps (btw, not 1,000 steps, only +/- 150). Once on the beach, we strolled south to the point for a little body surfing & sunning, which is the N. end of Leadbetters where all the surfers go to catch waves. The ocean, the views & the beach were absolutely stunning & spectacular, with a capital "S"!
However upon our return, walking barefoot on the beach back to the steps, both my wife's feet bottoms and mine were completely covered with "black tar" (I feel so remiss for not taking a picture, but believe me, you would have been disgusted). We were so repulsed ourselves that we sought out the nearest pharmacy (CVS on Cliff & Meigs) and rushed there to get a bottle of Nail Polish remover and a scrubbing sponge; and spent the next 30 - 40 minutes plus removing tar from the bottoms of our feet!!! All I can say, is if you walk down there on these beaches, keep your shoes on at all times & be prepared to clean off the bottoms of your shoes!
What really bewilders me is why we are the only people, that I can tell, to note this recently, that mention this warning? I am assuming that this is a "natural phenomenon" and not caused by the off-shore oil derricks. Enjoy the scenery, but do beware, and take care of your feet.!

OK beach, but there was A LOT of dried seaweed littering the sand, which made it difficult to find a spot to sit or lay down.
Nice views though. And it was fun watching the little sandpipers run around the tide. 

Just watch out for the seagulls! They are HUGE, aggressive, and not scared of humans in the least!

It was also nice that parking is free for the first 90mins.

Beautiful beach! Parking is easily accessible and the first 90 minutes are free. The surf isn't consistent but when there are waves, they're chest high at most. A good point break for long boarders. As for the seaweed everyone is complaining about, that's just nature doing it's thing. I wouldn't want my tax dollars wasted on "seaweed removal".

What:
 - Family friendly beach
 - View of Santa Barbara Harbor

More Info:
 - Free admission
 - Free parking; dedicated parking lot for the beach

Being a family-friendly beach near the Santa Barbara Harbor, the Leadbetter Beach attracts a lot of crowd. The mellow beach tides also make it popular among surfers. It is a long sandy beach that stretches from Santa Barbara Harbor till the Leadbetter hills.

Leadbetter hills as seen from the beach - http://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/leadbetter-beach-santa-barbara-2?select=b0pTw9ef_FYTTC7urBujfQ#b0pTw9ef_FYTTC7urBujfQ

It is a nice place to spread your beach blankets and relax on the sandy beach or have a picnic. A stroll along the beach is a pleasant experience. While my friends were relaxing on the beach after a long day in Santa Barbara, I decided to stroll all the way till I reached the Santa Barbara harbor.

Santa Barbara Harbor, seen from one end of the Leadbetter Beach - http://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/leadbetter-beach-santa-barbara-2?select=b0pTw9ef_FYTTC7urBujfQ#kp4XWMSXg9nB5V_gnrJ8zg

Kids playing with their sand kits, families taking pictures, people ambling along the harbor and sunbathing are a common sight in this beach. There is not too much to do here, we just wanted to hang loose at the beach and this just served our purpose.

A yatch sailing on the beach - http://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/leadbetter-beach-santa-barbara-2?select=b0pTw9ef_FYTTC7urBujfQ#BF6B8-jrTaZJnlve5Balfg

Leadbetter Beach isn't exactly the type of beach that most people picture when they think of Southern California beaches.  There's a lot of kelp on the beach, and the temperature is usually a little colder than you'll find in La Jolla or Malibu.

As they say in France: "Vive La Différence!"  This is the perfect beach for pulling up some chairs, unwinding, playing in the sand and/or enjoying the sun and the salt sea air :-)

Tip: On a clear day, you can see the Channel Islands right from the beach!

There's convenient self-pay parking available here.  There's a machine that allegedly takes credit cards, but it wasn't playing nice when we visited so I suggest you bring cash just in case.

I'm writing this during Covid times so I'm not sure what this place is like in normal times. Parking was wide open. We arrived at 9am but even by 12:30 there still were plenty of spaces. Our rv fit in a normal space but there are also oversized spaces available. You pay at the kiosk and put a tag on your dash. This beach is a melting pot. Young, old, families, couples, and homeless. The picnic tables are quarantined off but several people were still sitting inside the temporary fences. This beach has a grass area but the grass is not healthy and watch where you step as people have not picked up after there dogs. The sand has a lot of debris from the ocean and other plant life. Set up chairs on the sand. Cool wind here. I didn't notice until we biked south on Shoreline and it felt so much warmer. 4 for amenities, parking and location. 3 for the beach itself. Around 3 pm on this Sunday, it became quite crowded.  Shoreline Cafe is an easy walk and you have my favorite Brophys at the other end of the parking lot. You can hop on the bike path and go either direction for a nice ride.

Leadbetter is a magical, leaning towards local spot. The cove and adjacent cliff make for a picturesque scene. It has a nice, accessible point break for families and those learning to surf, paddleboard or just frolic. It's generally mellow and friendly in the line-up. The cove doesn't seem to get as much of the out of town crowd, perhaps because it's a little bit of a walk from the pay lot; although an easier walk from Shoreline Park (free) above. There's plenty of parking at the lower pay lot, BBQ/picnic sites, bathrooms and showers, and the Shoreline Café if you are hungry. I, and then later my kids essentially grew up at this beach. Summers are balmy and calm, winters crisp with dark blue-green water and beautiful sunrises. One can often spot dolphins and seals. I get annoyed sometimes by the number of off-leash dogs that are technically not allowed (I'm a dog owner), these people don't seem to have any consideration for others - it's more a recent thing and not the beach's fault. Leadbetter is a natural and ungroomed beach, with rocks, tidepools, kelp and grass at low tide, and sometimes piles of seaweed on the beach. We like it that way!

There are plenty of beautiful beaches in the Santa Barbara area. When I stopped by, Leadbetter Beach was by far the busiest. It stretches along Shoreline Drive from the bluffs of Shoreline Park in the west to the busy marina and harbor to the east. Its existence is a man-made accident, in fact: the construction of the harbor altered currents and sand accumulation to such an extent that it created a beach here, extending the shoreline well past where it used to be at Santa Barbara City College.

So today, it's a very wide, expansive beach that can accommodate lots of folks in a distanced manner. There are picnic/BBQ areas and restroom facilities closer to the street, plus a perpetually busy and crowded parking lot. And, thanks to the harbor, the currents here are muted, allowing easy introductory surfing, kiteboarding, sailing, and windsurfing. Not to mention lots of shirtless scenery in varying states and conditions.

Loved this beach, my kids and I can't wait to go back. Sand, waves and water were perfect. The waves don't get big, which is perfect for my kids. They were able to play in the waves without worrying if they were going to be big and make them tumble lol. In all this beach is my go to from now on. Especially because we can bbq there, I took my little portable bbq. Great beach to experience.
Cool spot for our car show!! AND you can drink beer in the picnic areas!  Nice back drop for pics.
This was a nice beach. You pay to park at a little kiosk. It was $10 for the day. There is a restroom and small restaurant.

This Beach is right next to Stern's Wharf. It is a must see and enjoy tourist attraction. From wale watching, to kayaking, to playing beach volleyball, Sand Piper is the most fun sandy beach. Unlike the other beaches in Santa Barbara, Sand Piper does not have seaweed, but the water is shallow. The view is pronominal, a perfect place to tan, and a true place to learn about the luxurious life so-Cal people enjoy. Please stop by

This is a great place to take the whole family and your furry babies. Love that you can have dip with you on the sand. Yes you have to still clean up after yourself and your furry babies. There is a cafe near by if you are feeling hungry. What a great place
This is a perfect, easy to access local beach walkable from several hotels. It was clean, with no seaweed/rocks to disrupt my swim, and lots of affordable parking! Love it.

Leadbetter Beach is one of my fav beaches in Cali. It has a lot to offer, and I think that each one of us (Assuming all of us love the ocean) can find cool things to do here. You can relax on the sand, or walk by the rocks, see some sea life and smell the ocean (Sometime too much when it is very weedy lol). I know one thing, they will see a lot of me here in this coming Summer. Go out, if it a sunny day there aren't many nicer things than the ocean. You know what,also if it is cloudy.

Spent a nice afternoon in august at this beach.  Found parking without any problems.  You pay the parking at the meter.  The beach itself is small makes it easy to carry stuff from car to a nice close spot near the water.  Everyone kept their distance both on the sand and in the water which was good. Water didn't have a lot of seaweed in it which I despise on socal beaches.  The sand also was rather clean, seems everyone picks up after themselves despite large family groups.  People were out paragliding? I think that's what it's called.  People running through with their dogs too.  5/5 for my first Santa Barbara beach experience.

name: Alameda Park
formatted_address: 1400 Santa Barbara St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful urban park. Well maintained. The mature trees form a natural barrier, making visitors forget the busy streets nearby.

Such a gorgeous, vibrant, marvelous park and meticulously maintained. Really enjoyed my morning walk there before I hopped on my flight back east. If only Jacksonville, Florida took a page from Santa Barbara on parks!

This park is so nice and beautiful. I’d definitely recommend taking your kiddos to play here. Lots of space for them to run around. The playground area is completely fenced in which is great! There’s also bathrooms near the playground.
This is a gorgeous park. When we went, it was clean and well maintained. The trees are stunning. The playground is beautiful. I wish I had a better picture of the actual playground.
Beautiful park for events, no kings day was awesome!

yelp_reviews.text: Not our favorite playground in SB for our 3 & 5 y.o.s. Too big and too dark; way too many opportunities for a child to "hide" when mommy doesn't feel like playing hide & seek! I do like the whale & shark, and the many balance beams (don't see enough of those on playgrounds).  Lots of vagrants around. When we were there this weekend, the restroom structure was closed for renovation. Two portapotties were there, better than nothing but not awesome. The swings and tire swing were most popular with my kids. Lots of parties going on at the larger park, lots of street vendors, lots of bodies to crash into. I prefer a more tranquil park experience for myself, but it gets the job done if you're right there and can't make it to Oak Park.

Accidentally found this park while vacationing in Santa Barbara. 

My 3 yo loved it the minute he saw it. He ran into the park so fast and quickly disappeared. There are so many turns in this huge park, it's easy to lose your child in just a few seconds. However, it is a lot of fun from a child's perspective. My son was having so much fun running up and down, pretending to be pirate in a big pirate ship one minute, then pretended he was in an elevator and quickly went downstairs, he even quickly made friends with other kids and got them to follow him around. This is not his normal behavior. He was just having such a blast and it really peaked his imagination. 

My 1 yo also loved the park, there wasn't as many things for him to do, but he just liked walking around and watching all the other kids. He enjoyed sitting on the shark.

When my daughter was young whenever we'd come home to SB she could hardly wait to go to Kids World.  It's a great place to play because kids can spend hours of intense activity crawling, climbing, swinging, hiding, running, sliding, in a safe, contained area, close to the parents, but still independent.  That's what this space was intended for - and I remember this was a major issue when it was built back in the 1990s.  It's the antidote for overly safe, sterile playgrounds where parents constantly breathe down their kids' necks and don't let them have any fun.

If this park was deeper in SoCal, there would probably be graffiti everywhere.  Thankfully, it's not, so there was no graffiti on my visit. Sure there may be a couple homeless folk roaming the outskirts of the park,  but the fortress playground is good times for kids and the young at heart! Played hide and go seek with the kids for hours. I only wish I lived a little closer and could visit here more often. Stay young at heart my friends...

There are only a few Kid Friendly places in Santa Barbara but the Alameda Park is a must stop if you visit Santa Barbara with family.  There are two parts to this park, one has a very large wooden playset, the other has a large duck and turtle pond. The kids loved this park and we spent over an hour why they ran through all of the wooden playset. 

Parking is located on the streets around the parks but be mindful of parking hours and limitations via the posted signs.
There was also a women selling mangoes on a stick for $3. She'll add some type of seasoning if you ask.

Creeper Alert!
- There's a creeper in this park, a giant with gray hair. It was hard to tell why this person was in the park, when he wasn't lounging in the dirt next to kids park, or rummage in the trash bins, he was spending a lot of time in the bathroom or off trail in the bushes.. Because he kept staring at the kids area for no apparent reason ( i.e. he had no kids) I was mad-dogging him pretty hard. Eventually he got in his car and drove off... Or so I thought. When we walked  across the street to the duck pond park he showed up again?!?! This time he was walking in the back bushes off trail and then he was crawling around in the reeds next to a young couple having lunch...( see picture)

Something tells me that people that live in SB and go to this park often know about this giant. Keep your head on a swivel if you have kids.

I brought my three year old and 17 month old here on a recent trip to visit family. The big draw here is the massive wood playground structure that resembles a castle. My kids loved climbing around and never seemed to be bored. There were dozens of kids there the two times we visited. There are picnic areas that can be reserved for parties. We were able to find parking on the street right in front, but that was during the week.

Great Park up on the Upper East Side. Has slides and jungle gyms for the kids, large open space and they always seem to have something going on there. Also today there are setting up for summer solstice which is always a great time.
Went to this park at the earth day event! The park was big and there was parking on the street. Lots of trees, good place for bringing dogs or for a walk!

I love Kids world.. The gardens, the turtle and koi pond... ahhhh so beautiful. 


Great memories here :) 


It's a beautiful area to take a stroll, read a book, drink some tea or take the kids to play :) 


You definitely want to spend some leisurely time just for you or the entire family here!

Amazing place for kids and adults! The playground is a wooden fortress with neat obstacles and plenty of things to keep your young ones occupied. Just across the way is a garden and a pond littered with ducks and turtles. Can't wait to go back to the PPP (perfect picnic place)!

A very elaborate play area, that I'm sure the slightly older than our son crowd must LOVE!  Our son enjoyed climbing around everywhere, following all the older kids that were around.  It was a bit tough trying to keep him safe, as some of the structures were a bit tighter for me to get through, but overall a great spot to hang out for a picnic.

Great playground.  My daughter is almost 2 and loved it. The parking was difficult, there is no parking lot and also some of the slides are metal and get very hot. Too hot to slide down. It's great that even the little kids can safely go up to the top of the castle to explore.

A gorgeous day in SB and an awesome park to spend it in.. Google search helped us find this place on our halfway point to LA. 

Easy parking for a Sunday ... Free... What??? And an amazing play structure. These days it doesn't qualify as a park without the requisite play structure, so it passed the kiddo's standards for a fun playground. 

Ladies selling fresh ripe mangos on a stick and homemade potato chips and shave ice... Yes, real shave ice, not that snow cone cr-p, from a cart. Tried them all, and food permit absence aside, our tummies are not full of crummies and we are ok. 

 Amazing picnic area adjacent, clean bathrooms nearby and overall, a wonderful experience.... Worth a visit for any family!

A great playground and park off just off the downtown. The centerpiece is the "Kid's World" play structure. Its rather big and complex with lots of nooks and crannies for exploring and pretending. (Parents be forewarned: the structure isn't really made for adults, so you might very well get stuck in a nook or a cranny.) Nice touch is a little theater -in-the-round stage in the mix for parents to sit and watch and/or for kids to pretend they are star performers. Also swings and sand near by.

As part of the same block, there is an expansive grassy area for playing catch, running around and barbecuing and picnics.

Across the street, there a garden with lilly pads, koi and ducks. Its nicely secluded form the hubbub of the neighborhood by trees and bushes.
Fantastic park close to State Street. Really fun playground for kids In a great setting. Nice area to walk around and get a more local vibe for Santa Barbara.
Very fun park for kids of all ages! Almost reminds me of a pirate ship/castle. Definitely worth checking out. Daughter is almost three and she loves it.

Very nice park for everyone! Enough space/paths to take a dog for a walk without having to run into other dogs (just take alternate walkways). Children have their own play area that looks pretty safe, clean, and fun. The playground is fenced (short wood fence) so there's one entrance/exit. Not many benches to sit at, it's mostly to sit on the grass or walk around. It's not a huge park, but I found it to be nicer than some bigger parks. Grass is very patchy, there's a lot of mostly dirt areas, but the good parts are nice and green.

Best feature of this park is "Kid's World" which is a really cool, community-built, play area to climb on, slide down, balance on, chase through and just have a great time with your kids.  We lived right across the street from this park when we lived in Santa Barbara as newlyweds 13 years ago, and it's pretty cool to take our kids there to play when we are visiting Santa Barbara now.  I would give this place 5 stars, but the restroom is disgusting.  Take care of your business before you visit this park.

This park is like nothing we have ever seen (we have seen loads of parks)! The castle is huge and has lots of hidden little stairs, paths and slides. With hat said you kind of have to go in with the little ones or I can see them getting lost. That wasn't a problem for us but could be if you have more than one little one. We took our 2 year old and she loved it! The location is vey central to state street and has a wilderness area right across from it. Once we were a little tired we were able to walk across and picnic right alongside the ducks and turtles. We loved this place and would go back anytime.
Best apparatus in SB for the kids to play - just ask my 6 yr old.

This was a nice playground to find in Santa Barbara.

The playground is included as part of a nice park near the downtown area.
The park itself is rather pleasant; paths lined with different types of trees and some beautiful ponds in the area.

The playground itself has the odor of a hamster cage needing cleaning, likely due to the fact that wood-chips composed the grounding.  The equipment is also composed of wood, and through it's definitely a splinter trap, the equipment was plentiful with slides, bridges, ropes and climbing areas.  Lots of kids in the area, so it's easy to find play-friends.

There are picnic tables near the playground and lots of park benches scattered throughout the playground.

Sometimes I imagine it from many years ago.

This place was so overgrown with trees hanging to the ground, palm and bamboo forests. It was a jungle. Sometimes we'd hop the fence at the El Mirasol property but we were scared of being caught...it was a fun place to play.

It's great now too as I sit here feeling the warm sun shining through the tall trees and hearing the sounds of happy kids.

It is a wonderful park.

Visited this park as suggested by the staff in our hotel this week. Wow! This park is so awesome. It has LITERALLY everything a child needs to encourage all aspects of development ! I mean social and emotional via doing ! ( running, climbing, swinging, balancing, FREE PLAY all the way) I am truly impress . Our kids played non stop for over an hour , they were having so much fun. Santa Barbara seems to really put much thought into its parks . That's one reason I love coming so much !

Great park for little ones. My daughter is 2 and loved the small swingset and small slide. I appreciated that the area for the small kids was separate from the big kids area. I also enjoyed that there were a few other kids her age there that she could interact with.

I love this park!  It spans three city blocks, with one section having a huge and awesome wooden castle-play-structure and one section having beautiful botanical gardens, gazebos and a fantastic turtle pond.  So, in reality it's not that cool that the pond is full (overflowing practically) with turtles since it's due to people dumping them there, but if you just want a quiet place to relax and enjoy some nature, this is a great park.  I've been here on several occasions to use it as a photography background and last weekend I stopped by just to chill out and kill some time.  I saw lots of turtles, ducks, gulls, and coots.  The botanical gardens are beautiful with plants still in bloom.  

The day I was here taking engagement photos for some friends we saw no fewer than six other photo shoots going on for families, couples, and modeling.  It's really such a lovely spot.  

I can't recommend it enough.  If you have an hour to kill and aren't sure where to go, come here.  It's pretty easy to find free parking spaces on the streets and you'll have a really nice time.

What a beautiful park!  Even has a koi/turtle pond!  I don't have kids (and I'm not a kid myself), but there is a really cool looking play area for kids.  Good for picnics, strolls, or lounging out with a book.
awesome park for kids.  best place for hide and go seek!
Is it weird that I drive all the way from LA to come to this park? It's amazing! There are no shortage of fun activities for kids and adults alike!
Awesome park with  tons of fun stuff for kids to play with. The main play area is really big so as the parent plan on moving around to keep an eye on them.

Very nice park for everyone! Enough space/paths to take a nice stroll. Children have their own play area that looks pretty safe, clean, and fun. The playground is fenced (short wood fence) so there's one entrance/exit. Not many benches to sit at, it's mostly to sit on the grass or walk around. It's not a huge park, but I found it to be nicer than some bigger parks. Grass is very patchy, there's a lot of mostly dirt areas, but green grass makes up the majority of the park.

Just bought property in the area. My daughter is living in one of the units. Her unut overlooks the park. Amazing mature  trees and winding walkways A kids castle made of all natural wood with so many bridges, mazes slides. Surrounded by historical buildings and churches.

Awesome! Our family highly enjoyed this park. Beautiful and huge wooden play structure in excellent condition. Son got quite a good workout navigating it. Nice shade and pretty clean overall. Don't miss the pond at Alice Keck Park across the street - it's full of turtles!

Amazing wooden castle playground. Something interesting for kids of all ages and their parents. There's whales and sharks sculptures that the kids can climb on. There's also kids' fingerprints towers the kids love to compare themselves with. there's central benches for the parents to see all the kids. There's a giant tree the kids love to climb all over and play around.

very clean and well shaded.  With nearby picnic table and recently renovated bathroom, it's the place that families love.

I came to this park today with my five grandchildren and had a wonderful time. The wooden extremely large playground was incredible and the kids had a blast running around for over an hour. I don't live nearby but if I did I would be coming to this park all the time!

This looked like a very well loved park by the locals, the wooden play structures are extensive and unique, albeit somewhat worn.  My kids had a good time.

The bathroom was not very well maintained.  Use only in case of emergency!

Kiddo happily played to her heart's content today afternoon in the Kids World- a welcome respite from sightseeing.  This is one of the more extensive kids playground structures I have ever seen and as a parent, it gets two giant thumbs up!  My only struggle is that it is so massive that it can be tough to keep track of your kid unless you follow them around.  The entire play area is surrounded by a gated fence, which keeps kids from running off but doesn't necessarily keep all the creepers out.

Definitely worth a visit for some kid-friendly fun!  The giant fig tree next to the playground is pretty neat too ;)

This place is fantastic! Acres of soft green grass, trees and places to BBQ or hang out with friends and family. My nieces and nephews love to come here and play at Kid's World, which is huge. When I was in college, the whole community built the structure, kind of like a community barn-raising. On the other side of Kid's World is Alice Keck memorial gardens, amazing and very beautiful. A spectacular way to spend the day.

OMG, I wish I could turn back time just to be a kid and get lost in the playground. This place is a kid's dream come true as well a great spot for parents to tire them out! lol Keeping an eye on kids is could be competition at this playground. It is so huge you can easily lose sight of them especially little ones. Scary!

I took my nephews (2, 5, & 7) there on a recent trip to SB and they had such a blast they were all smiles and laughter the entire time!  When it finally came time to leave they barely protested so I knew they were pooped out! The 3 boys knocked out the whole way back to the 310.  

Parking: Street only

Food: You can easily spend hours at this place. The playground is in a residential area so I recommend bringing food with you. We stopped at Lilly's Tacos on our way in.
For anyone with kids, who are in SB, this is the place to stop.  Covered, cool features, and plenty of room to watch them run and play

Really neat play structure aptly called Kids World is highlight of this park. Well shaded by beautiful large tree. Playground has tunnels, mazes, bridges, climbing apparatuses, swings and more. 4 year old refused to leave and all 3 kids worked up a good sweat from exploring and playing. Lots of kids of all ages but parents seemed nearby and was impressed seeing bigger kids watching out and not running over little ones. A good family friendly park even for an out of towner.

We initially went to the park because they had this cool castle like play structure. The kids loved it. Across the street was a duck pond and as we were looking at ducks and turtles in the pond, we could hear what sounded like folk Gaelic music coming from under a huge shaded tree. We walked through to find a large group of musicians who gather each Wednesday to play. Random people just show up with their instruments and join in on the tune like a drum circle.
Great place to spend an hour during our visit! And the clown show was an awesome surprise!

It's a nice park and nice location plenty of parking you could do your walk around the block you look across the street for the yard park and feed the ducks in the lake and it's a kid world is to said he did a good job for the kid world OK thank you I'll give it five stars

Such a beautiful park! It's lots of green space and benches. Our fave part was kids world which is a gated large play area for kids. 

Easy free parking. Lots of areas to walk around. Overall we had a great time!

Awesome park with an amazing play structure. I love seeing unique structures. It's like a giant castle with lots of hidden areas. A garden with a nice pond and walkway is next to it. My only concern is that there were a few creepy looking older men just sitting their looking at the kids play.

What a cool park! It's huge with lots of grass and a large wooden jungle gym where kids go wild. There are also large marine animals to climb on and take fun pictures. 

Lots of trees and tables if you want to bring the family for a nice picnic or host a party.

Street parking surrounding the park too if you come from afar!

Can't say much about the restroom, but I really love this park.  On one side of Micheltorena is a nice pond with lots of turtles, koi, and ducks.  You're not supposed to feed them, but everyone does it anyway.  It's pretty funny seeing the turtles getting shoved all around by the koi and kicked in the head by the ducks.  Looks like they used to have some cool speaker things here to tell you about the local flora and fauna, but the speakers don't seem to work anymore.  Also there's usually some hippies or ravers chilling in the gazebo or having "workshops" or whatever people used to do in the 90's.

If you cross the street, you'll find the Kid's World that Maryanne R. previously mentioned.  Really cool play structure, with tons of fun things to balance on and climb.  I don't fit through a lot of the play area structures, but the ones I do fit through are a blast.  Plenty of things that are way too shaky for me to balance on.  Only downside (I guess it would be an upside if you're a kid?) is there's usually tons of little kids running around here, and unless you're a jerk it's not really fair to the kids to hog their play equipment.  There's also a giant sculpture thing of a whale and a shark to play on.  Last time we were there some kids squished a bunch of berries or something in the sharks mouth, and it looked like guts and blood were pouring out of it's throat.  Really cool.
Great park with gardens and a pond. Perfect for a picnic. We visited during Covid-19 while playgrounds were still shut down. Perfect for a walk around the park and to let our toddler play!
Top marks! There's about 8000sf of play structure here to run, climb, and explore. Lots of slides and swings. Plus many unique attractions like the marble maze and huge "finger maze".

When I stopped by, I found this to be a vast public park with large, open, if very patchy lawns, diagonal footpaths, and tall, mature trees providing plentiful shade. It stretches across two city blocks and provides plenty of room for folks to picnic, read, or, as is more likely the case, flail about aimlessly or participate in open drug usage.

The best feature of the park is a gigantic playground on the northwest corner called "Kid's World." It's so large that it ought to have a distinct Yelp listing of its own, not only to distinguish it from the forlorn rest of the park, but also because it's comparable in scale and ambition to noteworthy large playgrounds I've seen elsewhere in my travels. It's built like a fortress and therefore doubles as a massive set of play and climbing structures for the kiddos to explore and conquer.

Great place for kids to run around and get their wiggles out. We were in SB for a family vacation and the kids needed to blow off some steam so they had fun running around the whole playground. It's nice bc the kids area is enclosed by a gate and there is a separate toddler section geared for the smaller kids. There are lots of pathways the kids can take though so it can be hard to keep track of your kids.

Kids growing up around here are so lucky. This is playground in this park is SWEET! It's a huge area with so many things to climb on and under just perfect. I couldn't resit it and I had to play on it, swinging from the hanging rings like a monkey. =p 

On the bottom level of the structure there are crawl spaces built in, it's a great place to get away from the hot mid-day sun and look at all the "art" kids drew inside. Fun stuff it's covered like the Sistine Chapel, I don't know how many adults get that vantage point but check it out yo.

Then as if that park wasn't cool enough there is a whale and shark chilling, great picture taking spot (I hugged the whale). The rest of the grassy park is shaded by trees, the coolest ones are the large Moreton Bay Fig trees that have twisted roots and stretching branches begging to be climbed on. Those trees just evoke happy memories of me in elementary school playing "tree tag". =)

Santa Barbara has it's fair share of vagrants, but in this park most seemed friendly and was there to enjoy a comfortable nap. Since I was here for the Summer Solstice celebration, there was vendors, food tents and a music.I bought some food and had a little picnic listening to music and watched barefooted hippies dance along with a couple of hooping girls. What a colorful place. =)

Most amazing big wood play structure I've seen. Not just another playground in this age of cookie-cutter bright colored plastic ones. No wonder they call it "Kid's World."

Highlights:
- Play structure: huge maze of a castle with multiple levels to explore. Incredibly impressive and fun. Seems to me like it could fill half of a football field or more. 
- Variety: several slides, balance beams, tires, driving wheels, and monkey bars
- Age levels: a smaller version for the little ones off to the side
- Sea creature statues: a whale and a shark to play on
- Picnic area: good for lunching, taking a break for a snack, or chilling as a parent
- Steps away from the garden across the road with a pond and turtles, ducks, pretty flowers, and a gazebo
- Shaded area: not too hot

Midlights:
- Tanbark: I'm undecided about this filler but reminds me of my childhood

Lowlights:
- Need to be extra vigilant: lots of kids running around and bunches of crevices so easy to lose sight of your kid

Hard to find a solid old school diamond like this, so and I'll come back as often as I can.

This is a great park! Kids can have a real adventure here: exploring the labyrinth of towers in the castle, playing hide and seek next to an enormously gigantic tree, getting balloon hats created for them by balloon artists, swinging on a big yellow tire or enjoying a variety of twisty slides. 

What a fun place! We will definitely be back.
Such a great park!  Lots of shade and different seating options.  Not to mention the stunning tree.

My little one LOVES coming to Kids World. After the modifications they did, it became a lot more safer and nicer. The castle has many sections and is divided by a bridge, wish I had a similar playground growing up because I would have loved it.  Best part is there's a seating section for parents to just watch their kids. I can just relax while my little one plays with other kids and gets tired lol then the next stop is downtown. There's also towers of fingerprints of kids from all different schools, very admirable how they involve members of the community into these areas.

Visited SB for a day and stopped by this playground to let my daughter get her energy out. I was really impressed with this play ground. It was big enough for kids to run around and my daughter really enjoyed the maze-like castle for kids to climb and explore. Def stop by if you have kids!

I wasn't really a fan of this playground because the way it's built is so dangerous. There's a lot of narrow spaces all over that children and tots can fall into while playing or climbing and overall it's just poorly designed. I'm also not a fan of wood chips over sand because the whole purpose of a park is to take your sand toys along. Good thing we're not from the area and we won't be coming back.

There is a BIG wooden play fort/area called Kids World. It is free and in the Alameda Park in the middle of town. It is also clean and well kept.  Plenty of shade by mature trees. Your kids will LOVE this place!! You could spend hours here. There is also a nice park just across the street from here that has a large turtle pond. You could also stroll along the nicely manicured walking trails this park has to offer. My only complaint is the public restrooms here, not clean. But then again, what public park restrooms are clean?

Beautiful park. Easy to find free street parking. The kid's world playground was so fun. My one year old toddler loved running around here and seeing the big whale and shark sculptures. The public bathrooms were clean.

On our way down from our central coast trip, we decided to stop in SB. We wanted to find a park to spread our picnic blanket out enjoy the beautiful warm weather. This neighborhood park was perfect! Completely surrounded by homes, this park is tucked into a residential neighborhood but it's big! There's an entire playground, which looked like it had more than your typical slides & swings. Had I been a little one, I'd have loved this place! There were people walking their dogs, having picnics like us, watching their kids play & just strolling.

I adore this park and it is truly a Kids World!  It is so well designed and has children at the heart of every detail.  I heard that volunteers helped design and build the park and that they still take great care of it and it shows!  I absolutely love it and my kids do as well.  Thank you for this wonderful park!
When ever I'm in Santa Barbara we have to stop here with the kids.  Best park I have ever been too and I'm 33.

With two toddlers who wanted to play, we were returning from our trip to Solvang when we found this huge playground that resembled a tree house, playground and obstacle course in one. My boys loved going up and down the slide and they also had fun watching the entertainment at the center of the play area. He was doing four shows that day. A little bit of comedy, magic and story telling. My boys loved it. The park is quite large so make sure to park at the northeast corner of the four quads. There's only street parking but still had enough space for us to find parking. There's also a fruit and ice cream vendor there on weekends. I would highly recommend the spot to people passing through.

One of my favorite local attractions!

I'm fortunate to live right across the street from this beautiful, two-block park. It's a peaceful space with a lush lawn comprising the majority of it. Even with Covid in place, the grounds are maintained on a regular basis. 

The main attraction is the Kids' World playground which debuted some 28 years ago when I was in kindergarten. I have had countless fun times here as a child, and it's wonderful to see it still being utilized just as it was when new. There's really nothing else like it in Santa Barbara. 

The other block of the park is anchored by a large gazebo area. It's an attraction for the annual Earth Day and Summer Solstice festivals. I can't wait until we able to celebrate both again. The tall shade trees and meandering paths really make this spot feel serene. 

I am looking out to the park from my window as I write this. I think I'll go head across the street and enjoy its beauty right now!

My wife had her goddaughter on this day and after an errand and food we took her to kids world as that's where she wanted to go  it was during the week so not as crowded and sure enough she finds her friend from school there so they both played together which was good and they fixed it up nice

name: Butterfly Beach
formatted_address: Butterfly Beach, 1233 Channel Dr #1101, Montecito, CA 93108, USA
