google_reviews.text: A narrow yet beautiful beach (inaccessible during high tide), just off Channel Drive. Since parking is within a stone’s throw of the water, the site is well suited for people with walking limitations.
Relaxing morning at Butterfly with sea lion passing through at edge of wave break.

One of my favorite beaches! I was bummed that it was a lot harder to get to the beach after all the storms we'd had. The stone stairs were down in two places and bolders and rocks are impeding the other stairs and ramps.
Still, if you can get to the beach, this one has sand to walk on in a low tide. Butterfly Beach is a gorgeous place.

Beautiful area, soft sandy beach, clean.  Street parking can generally be found although you may have to walk a bit.  You can also park near the Coast Village shops as their is a walkway under the freeway thru a nice neighborhood to the beach.

My favorite beach to visit when I want to be alone or take my dogs in a quick walk. I have been going here since my childhood. There are a lot of quiet benches, nooks and crannies in between the rocks that offer some peace and quiet if you're looking go meditate or just listen to the ocean.

There is of course a connection to the bike path near by too that will lead your to East Beach and eventually the Pier as well as the Bird Refuge.

Butterfly Beach is also right in front of The Biltmore which has a beautiful bar and restaurant. Sometimes I will meet my parents for dinner at the Coral Casino too - they are members there. But the beach is lovely all it's own.

yelp_reviews.text: Awesome beach, great sunsets directly across from historic Biltmore Hotel.  Iconic location for pre and post wedding photos.  Just romantic

Best place for sunset and also for small walks on the sand!
very romantic place.

A cozy place to get your beach fix. 

Cons: no public bathrooms and no showers. 

Pros: intimate, not crowded, and beautiful.

What could be better than a FREE DAY AT THE BEACH. Yes no paid parking. There is Free street parking here. What a beautiful picturesque beach. I love the bike path above the beach dotted with tall palm trees. It's Very relaxing here.

This is by far one of my favorite spots in Santa Barbara!  I almost don't want to say good things about it so that less people know about it...lol  Dog friendly with a nice view in both directions.  Parking is not that easy, but there is a good amount of it on the long stretch along the side of the Four Seasons hotel and some condos beside it.  Once you park the steps down lead to a pretty secluded little beach area. Pretty cool look with some light rocks formations against the wall and lined on the water.  If you are in the area make sure this is on your list!
Great beach as long as it isn't high tide :)

This is very much a locals beach.  Probably because its so damn hard to find.  And despite the fact that I used to live 3 blocks from another sand and ocean water filled locale, I always drove 20 minutes to get to Butterfly because its just so much better than everything else in the area. 

The beaches of Goleta are filled with college students, and the beaches of Santa Barbara around the pier are filled with bugs, tourists, volleyball players and have dirty sand. 

Butterfly (which is in Montecito) is everything the other beaches are not.  It can be hard to find and parking is often painful. But butterfly beach is one of those rare beaches where you can bring the family or you can bring the leftover keg and nobody cares. The sand and the water are clean. And I might just pack up my towels and be there before dawn tomorrow morning.

Beautiful beach, beautiful name...My college roommate actually got married on this beach in an impromptu ceremony. Across the street is the tony Four Seasons resort, it's a very upscale, peaceful beach.
Beautiful. A perfect spot to watch the sunset no matter the time of the year - would highly recommend recommend to visit if you are in the area!
Good clean beach! Parking was a bit tricky but just go earlier.

Beach is narrow. Had to relocate to another part of the beach because the tide was coming in. Beautiful beach. Water was warm. Waves were small. Not crowded, even on a warm Saturday afternoon. Parking was easy. Only problem is the lack of public bathrooms. You need to hop in your car and go to a gas station/coffee shop nearby. Beautiful sunset/moon lit evening. Will definitely come back.

I've spent my life in San Diego, one of those scenic, picturesque photo magnet/postcard sorta places, and I still have no problem finding beauty in my city's landscapes.

That said, Butterfly Beach is kinda ridiculously gorgeous. A small strip of sand hidden away from any major road, no surf to speak of due to the Channel Islands keeping it quiet, plenty of free parking, seems to be devoid of the homeless that mob State Street, and at sundown is the sort of place at which you wouldn't mind having a heart attack and dying if it meant the view being your last sight.

Also, take the bike route south from the wharf, and when you come around the cemetery and see the hill, keep going - my girl and I went this way not exactly sure if the bike path was going to lead to the beach, and the site of a hill for an out of shape guy like myself on a bike too low to the ground was enough to get me to turn around. We drove around there later in the day and approached it from the south, and not until we left the beach and headed north did we realize how close we were to the beach on our bikes. Lush greenery on the car and bike path just north of the beach/cemetery if you're in to that stuff, too.

Beautiful views, clean Montecito family beach, sheltered and a little hard to find.  It's helpful if you live or are renting nearby because there are no restrooms on the beach itself.  An alcohol ban is enforced but some couples can be seen trying to circumvent it by imbibing discretely from paper cups.  Dog friendly. Occasional celebrity sightings.

MOST AMAZING SPOT IN SANTA BARBARA!!!!
Your really must go here and take a walk along the ocean if you are in SB.  It is a must see. The palm trees are gorgeous, the Biltmore is glamorous, and the view is even more divine.

There are few beaches in the world as beautiful as Butterfly Beach.  You can see some spectacular sunsets from this beach.  Parking isn't ample, but if you go early enough, you can secure a street spot easily enough. There is beach access from the road.  This is a safe, beautiful, scenic beach that, more than most beaches in Santa Barbara, shows off the culture of intense beauty.

Bring an umbrella! This beach is great, the distance from the tide to the top of the beach is shorter than on any other beach I've been on, which is different but kind of cool. There are kayak and paddle board rentals as well.

One of the most beautiful beaches anywhere.  Just across from the Biltmore in a secluded nook of Montecito this gem of a beach has it all; sun, sand, surf, people (beautiful people), and the sounds of shoreline pleasure.

Don't forget your pale, shovel and towel!!

Great beach in a beautiful neighborhood. Very kid friendly. We went mid week in June and had a huge section of the beach to ourselves. Clean water! We found great street parking really close to the beach!

We went to Butterfly Beach on a rare 90+ degrees day in Santa Barbara, and it was semi-crowded but our overall experience was good.  The beach was very clean, but the water was freezing.  The water at this beach has a lot of seaweed, but much less than Goleta Beach which is a plus.  Some attempted to surf, but the waves broke pretty close to the water.  Casey enjoyed jumping in the waves, and I enjoyed relaxing in the sun.

Make sure to bring your own water, food, umbrellas, etc., because as many say, this is a "locals beach," so there are no amenities like bathrooms or tables - just sand and water!

Spent Sunday overnight in MontecitoInn which is just 5mins walk to ButterflyBeach. Tis my 2nd time here-1st time was on NewYear'sEve a few years ago and the beach was all fogged up which gave it a surreal atmosphere which was still special in its own way. This time round, got to the beach around 3pm and the tide was so out that there was no more sand and instead, we got ourselves a rocky beach. Actually laid out on the  pedestal 'steps" and watched the surfers waiting for waves. The next morning, it was a completely different beach altogether-the water came up to the walls and we even got splashed at the lookout point as the waves were simply huge!! Walked up to the famous 1000 ChannelDrive estate and around to the MusicAcademy and back to the beach in time to see a school of dolphins ( with their babies) swimming from one end to the other with the babies sticking very close to the mom!!My 1st time seeing a dolphin-much less 8 of 'em!! ButterflyBeach rawwwkss!! Extra star is for the "relax factor" which gets FullMarks here!!

Gorgeous beach!

 Sand is soft and there are great sights along the beach (including the Four Seasons Biltmore).  Also, sometimes there will be some excitement (we saw a fire blocks away the day we went)!  Plus, and I'm not sure if Chicago or medical school has dropped my standards, but the slim and tan California women laying out along the beach are lovely as well.  Oh, Santa Barbara I love you!

Would get 5 stars, but parking can be a b*tch for this beach.

Be sure to check out the shrine in the meadow about 100 yards south from the Four Seasons!

HELLO.  I'm here to review Butterfly Beach, in Santa Barbara (CA).  

My girlfriend (whom I love very much) and I just returned from Butterfly Beach.  We had seen the effusive enthusiasm all over Yelp for this "best beach on the West Coast".  

We arrived at evening, with a low-hanging sun still giving its warmth.  We found the beach certainly pleasant, enjoyable, and beachy.

However, 5-star-status it cannot deserve.   There were a number of swarthy locals huddled around a thumping dj/turntable setup on one end of the beach -- they were blasting horrific techno pulses that we couldn't get out of our brains.  

Lest you think us curmudgeons, we are ourselves young and vibrant and good-looking and open to the occasional beach party.  This was not the only factor in the one-star markdown, however -- the beach itself was narrow, lacking in stunning vistas or extraordinary privacy, and overall was enjoyable, but not spectacular. 

Thank you very much, and I hope you will read more of my reviews in the future.  Just search for "Douglas K.".  That's me!  

Douglas K.
Fantastic beach -- great for sunset views and for a swim in the ocean.
Very nice stretch of beach. Quite clean and very beautiful. And it's quite empty and peaceful, so it's the perfect spot to get away from the rowdiness of the beach by the pier.

(Post 2/5 on our "Get the heck out of LA for a day" road trip)

Lovely beach!  We wished we had come later in the day to catch the sunset.  From looking at other peoples' pics, it looks to be a stunning view!

Very dog-friendly here too!  We were regretting leaving our little Weezer at home.

We had come straight from the Alice Keck Park Memorial Garden and saw many Monarch Butterflies there.  I had a vision in my noggin that we'd be seeing many more at this beach.  It's got to be named Butterfly Beach for this reason, right?  Aw well, we enjoyed our brief visit.

I am pea-green with envy because the local beaches in the LA area are so..............not as lovely (polite wording here).
Love this beach.  It is great for a long walk and just magnificent on any day.  It is not as touristy as well.
A very nice little strip of beach. It was very windy, so not a lot of people. We had an easy time parking close, and enjoyed our time.

Perfect sand. Little or no breeze. A tree to take cover under, if necessary. Right next to the Biltmore, so you don't get the gross beach bums and trash everywhere. Minimal tar deposits (a curse in Santa Barbara) and overall my number one tanning spot every summer.

We went to butterfly beach on a busy day at high tide, so my review might be a bit biased.

This beach is in Montecito across from the Biltmore, so you will get a glimpse of some fancy people. There is free street parking all up and down in front of the beach. Unlike some other Santa Barbara beaches, this one is probably the best if you want to spend a day swimming, sunbathing and playing in the sand at the beach. It is very smooth sand (not rocky) and many people had towels, chairs, umbrellas, etc. laid out. It was a bit too crowded to take our dog off-leash because of the crowds, but I did see some locals running and playing ball with their dogs so that is an option too. 

Anywho, if you want to swim and enjoy a day at the beach this is it.
Gorgeous stretch of sand along the California Riviera. I ran into Anthony Kiedis, Kylie Minogue, Boyd Tinsley, and Julia Lois-Dreyfus. I have only seen famous people here, no butterflies though.

Great spot for alone time. It wasn't crowded on a Tuesday evening. There were a few good spots to sit down on benches, ledges, boulders, stairs or just the sand. Many locals bring their dogs. Sand is clean but not many rocks or shells to collect.

This is my favorite beach. It seems every time I go here I mostly see Golden Retrievers, not sure why but it makes it all that more interesting. One of the best benefits of this beach is that it's not as crowded as the one more downtown.

Toute grosse déception en arrivant à Santa Barbara, en voyant la plage principale...pas spécialement propre, que ce soit sur le sable ou dans l'eau. Je dégaine mon appli Yelp et cherche un endroit plus décentré, cosy. Hop direction Butterfly Beach, à quelques minutes en voiture, juste en dehors de la baie. 

Cette plage est un vrai petit coin paisible, pour ceux qui désirent être au calme. Ouvrez les yeux pour les stars, il y a quelques-unes qui rôdent ici, en mode incognito. Ma main à couper qu'on a croisé notre bon vieux JCVD, qui faisait son jogging. Bref. 

Possibilité de louer des SUP, Stand Up Paddle quand la mer est calme...par contre ne faîte pas comme moi. N'oubliez pas votre chapeau :)

Nice beach in front of some very nice homes.  We couldn't figure out how to drive down so we just parked and walked down.  Nice stroll past mounds of lantana plants filled with butterflies.  It was a foggy day but I can see why people love this place.

My memory of Butterfly Beach goes back to 1969 when Platform A blew and the infamous Santa Barbara Oil Spill largely sparked the modern international environmental movement!

I really don't remember how long it took for butterfly beach to regain it's beauty but it most certainly has.    It has been the location a hundred weddings and I am sure countless marriage proposals, it is that kind of spot.  To walk along the bluffs above Butterfly gives you Santa Barbara's most beautiful look at the Pacific ocean.   Ty Warner did an excellent job when he created that bike path.   

A number of years ago it was becoming a scene during the summer months with too many parties, too much alcohol and amplified music.   The City (or County) passed an ordinance banning alcohol and amplified music and it again became mellow.   As I recall there was supposed to be the option of obtaining a permit for alcohol on the beach but from what I have heard that has never happened.   

Dogs are allowed on the beach but bring a leash!   Animal control seems to love giving tickets to dog owners who let their dogs run free.

I actually hadn't been to this particular beach in many years until we were at the Four Seasons Biltmore this past weekend.  It's a lovely little stretch of beach, not overly crowded both in terms of cars or foot traffic, and it's ultra easy access..... not usually something one would say when talking about a beach.  We were there on a Sunday in July, and the weather was perfect along with it not being overly crowded.  Now that I know it's like this, I'd make the drive more often just to avoid all the typical beach nonsense; this is such a pretty beach.

This is a nice secluded beach. Not too many people here.

Not too dirty. Right in front of the four seasons hotel. I guess this is the hotel's personal beach.

The best thing about the beach is that you don't have to take a bunch of steps to get down to the beach, like so many other beaches out there. A few steps and you are there. 

After the beach, you can go inside the hotel for food and spa. Yep, what a life. 

Free street parking.
Great sunset, gentle waves for kids, lots of little sand crabs (harmless) feeding in the surf.

Beautiful beach in a beautiful city of Santa Barbara. On both occasions we visited on a weekday. Parking wasn't a problem at all and same goes for the crowd. Few people were out there tanning or walking their dogs. Other than that, there wasn't much traffic at all overall. What I like about this beach is the free road side parking. You can easily access your vehicle. Also, the water was warm and all sand. No sharp rocks whatsoever. I can see why So Cal folks love them beaches!

I.Love.This.Beach.
I went to this beach after looking up top beaches in Santa Barbara, CA on Yelp. I would have to agree with the other the reviews and say its a really beautiful beach. It's dog friendly, which can be hard to find. Street parking is limited, so I recommend getting there early to get a nice spot. If you do not mind the occasional dog running past you then you'l do fine oin this beach. Sunsets are a must see!

Sorry to go against the grain, folks, and, maybe I went on a bad overcast gloomy day... but, what is the big deal?  The view isn't that great, there weren't that many people or dogs for that matter. The sand isn't all that... this beach is small... there are HUGE tangles of seaweed all over the damn place, hmm... maybe I need to come back to re-evaluate when it's a lot warmer.
Beautiful and romantic beach in Montecito. Small and quaint. A perfect place to take a break from a coastal bike ride and dip into the ocean.
Awesome beach, you can rent out those big bike thingees and explore!, Fun, Nice Weather. Gorgeous. 2nd best to San Diego, Parking SUCKS.

Pretty beach!

Tons of sun, and space to play frisbee!

I liked that it wasn't overcrowded!

So glad we went to this beach while in Santa Barbara! It was clean and had nice soft sand! There was ample space to lay out and watch passerbys, families having picnics and dogs playing fetch.  

Parking can be tough especially since it is right across the street from The Four Seasons. But if you come early you will find a spot.

It is a relaxing beach with gorgeous palm trees swaying in the wind. The tickly soft gray sand was evermore comforting on a hot summer day!
Came here for a friend's wedding and it was a perfect place for a sunset wedding!  Parking can be hard to find so come early.

This was definitely a great find (thanks, Yelp!)  After an afternoon of shopping/dining on State street, my mom and I decided to go to the beach.  We drove towards the nearby shore and it was CRAZY packed with an art show and tons of people walking around.  We decided to hop on the freeway and hit up another beach... this is when I found out about Butterfly Beach.  Parking was pretty easy, and it was super fancy -- right near the 4 seasons.  The stretch of beach itself was pretty quiet, and there were people with their dogs walking along the shore.  My mom and I were so relaxed that we dozed in and out of naps over a course of 2 hours -- something I wouldn't be able to do at a busy beach like Santa Monica.  The sunset was very calming.  Cali beach beauty at it's finest!
Beautiful beach. Been around a long time. Rarely come across such a gem. Peaceful and beautiful. Great for individuals,  couples and families.

Nice little beach.  The waves were calm and it was a short walk to the beach from the street.  There is only street parking so it can fill up.  If you don't like dogs, you may want to stay away.  There were lots of dogs the day we went.

My favorite beach in the area. Park by the Four Seasons and walk east towards Hammonds Beach. It's pristine. Go at low tide so the beach is bigger. It gets narrow around Hammonds. I've found a few pieces of sea glass there. The best part is that it's not crowded. A few surfers west of Four Seasons. Nice retaining wall just by the hotel to use as a base if you're going to be there all day. No rentals, so you have to bring your own beach toys.

breathtaking, but i am biased. 

i'm a so cal girl at heart who absolutely loves beaches, sunsets, and more... and even though i've lived along the coast from santa monica, sf, newport to sd, i have to say, nothing beats SD beaches...

BUT ... 

it definitely is a must go place when one is in sb...

Amazing beach 
Super clean and so nice 
While driving along the shore there are big beautiful homes all around 
The water of the beach is so blue and pretty 
The sand is super white but hot hahahah it was actually kinda burning my feet stay close to the water or leave your shoes on lol 
Parking was super easy a lot of parking along the road
A beautiful area with nice scenery. I picked up a picture for my office of the nice view

So I've been to this beach many times through the years but I've never actually walked on the beach!  Could be because it's typically windy and a little chilly whenever I've been there... Whatever the case, it's a beautiful spot.  And the Biltmore is right across the street.

I have lived in Orange County my whole life surrounded by Newport and Laguna beach and nothing compares to this beach. This definitely has to be the most beautiful beach in California. The locals come out at night and are great to talk to and everyone brings there dogs to play in the water. Local photgraphy students use the beach for photoshoots and the atmosphere is just surreal and something out of a movie. I can not say enough good things about this place.

A beautiful beach in Montecito, near the Biltmore, Music Academy of the West and Coral Casino. This beach is stunning and due to its location, is often empty. Gorgeous views, clean beach, ample street parking and pet friendly, what more could you ask for? I do miss the days back in college where you could go and watch Paul Walker surfing with friends. I love SB!

One of my best memories ever was one particular afternoon at Butterfly Beach - watching the dolphins swim by, lying in the sun with friends, taking a dip in the warm water and actually not getting covered in tar. 

Heaven!

This is the most beautiful beach I've seen in Califorina, and I've been to many. Except that pretty one I always see when I drive by San Luis. But yeah, it has wonderful ledges that you can sit on or walk on. There's not a lot of sand, so I guess it's not good if you want to run around and play on the beach a lot. But it's long and has an amazing view, as well as beautiful hotels and mansions all around it.

By far my favorite beach in SB. This is coming from someone who lives a block from Hendry's! Butterfly is the best place to catch a sunset or to go late at night. Weekdays are pretty empty. Weekends can be packed during the summer! Get there early these days to find parking, as you might end up having to walk a good distance. Will also be tough finding a spot to lounge.

This beach was recommended to me by a friend. It's across from the Four Seasons hotel. Parking is free and easy to find on a Saturday afternoon.

It's a sandy, beautiful beach that isn't overly crowded. This was my toddler's first time at a beach, and he had a great time!
Love this beach!! Best beach in the area, park in residential area and walk to the beach, free parking, perfect! The neighbors are friendly.
so pretty here and not as crowded as other beaches.  can see the 4 seasons hotel nearby which is really an amazing spot.  great for a beach stroll

This is another one of my favorite beaches in the Santa Barbara area. It is in Montecito across from the Four Seasons Hotel. At the top there is a sidewalk and grass. 

Nice sandy beach that isn't too crowded. Check the tide table though. I went when the tide was in.

Being near a beach most of my life, I would like to go out on a limb and say that this is seriously one of the most beautiful beaches I've been to, ever.

My boyfriend and I enjoyed a lovely break from sightseeing here, and it was very romantic despite it being relatively hot.  The beach was clean, no oil rigs in sight, no huge crowd of 909 tourists *insert HB native laugh here*, and a beautiful view of the clean water.  Wished we could've watched the sun set at this beach before we left SB.

Parking was relatively easy for being a Saturday afternoon; we found spots along the road and walked less than a few minutes to the beach.  Dog-friendly beach, but still very clean (as they always are).  Use Google maps to help you locate the beach -- we looked up the Four Seasons Biltmore in Montecito, as it was across the street from the beach.  

Highly recommended for a romantic date!

Oh, I've heard that celebrity sightings are common here.  Didn't see anybody famous, but who cares when you've got the most handsome man cuddling with you while wiping sweat off your brow from the heat? :)

Whoever gives this beach less than five stars is not a true born and raised Californian. What's not to like about the beautiful vastness of the Pacific Ocean? It makes you feel so small, yet you can appreciate it all at the same time. The best beach ever!!!

This is a cute beach to visit with your family. It's very clean.
Would totally recommend.

Pretty beach right across from the beautiful Four Seasons Biltmore. 
You can run on the beach, or walk above it on the paved sidewalk, or enjoy the view sitting on the wall (or even go wandering around the Blitmore like we did)

 Don't forget your well behaved dog! Its an off the leash beach :)

An amazing and special place. The sand is warm, the parking is easy and doesn't require more than 100 paces to the water. I loved the palm trees swaying over the beach while I swam in the warm pacific. Can't wait to return.

My favorite beach in Santa Barbara area! Very clean, nice trees, dog friendly, kids friendly and great to hang out especially on a warm sunny day! There is also a four season resort next to the beach.

My boyfriend and I walk to butterfly almost every day. The beach is usually clean and the view is always incredible. This beach is dog friendly but unfortunately they must be on leash. It's also a great place to ride your bike to. There is a biking and walking path near the beach.

Come here if you're looking for a fun and busy atmosphere. Good for people watching and drinking with friends. Not so good for quiet walks and romance. When tide is high there's dramatically less beach.

This is fronting the Four Seasons Hotel, it's clean , quiet and relaxing . Not a lot of tourists know about this place so that's why it's just either the guests from the hotel or the locals of Montecito goes here . I give it a 5.

This a nice, quiet little strip that isn't overcrowded. There is a bike path along the beach that is cradled by flowers on each side, attracting alllll the butterflies. It was a magical bike ride and visit.

Beautiful and clean.  I may be biased because we brought really delicious sandwiches  from Metropulos to eat there, but no, the consumption of This Little Piggie sandwich aside, I remember being on the beach, digging my toes into its soft sand, and thinking, "If only I had another sandwich to eat on this gorgeous beach."
Amazing beach I love everything the weather was beautiful thank you for amazing time I will be back as soon as possibke
Nobody there on a weekday.  A few Dog walkers but otherwise nice.  It is a small beach.

Tourist alert - worst beach in SB.

Stay far away, nothing exciting. 

Matter if fact, I can't recommend this to anyone....  

Yeah....it's awful.....I hear Goleta beach is awesome....You should check that one out.
The most beautiful beach I've been too. Located right in front of the Four Seasons Hotel in Santa Barbara, not too crowded, clean and peaceful. Loved it!
A beach perfect for enjoying the vast open water. Few people walking their dogs, taking a stroll, or getting a run in. Nothing more than that.

Butterfly Beach is an iconic Montecito staple.  I read the tide reports daily (pick an app) and when it's very-low tide; you can venture out for a 6-mile walk up this coast-line with yr pup, it's heavenly.  Subsequently, if you want to hydrate head to the Biltmore for a matcha-latte at the bar, sans diary products.  Also, there's an extensive bike path adjacent if you'd rather cycle thru yr calorie count for the day.

Such a beautiful beach right across from the Four Seasons in Santa Barbara.  My second fave resort after Ojai Valley Inn and Spa.  The beach allows dogs.  A couple of interesting rock formations, flowers, and a sail boat to look at and take photos near.  Worth a nice long walk when at the resort.
Love this beach, now one of my favorites. Definitely recommend it is wheelchair friendly has a ramp all the way down to sand. Relaxing and peaceful

Oh so beautiful... Emphassis on beautiful (Typo here but works! Brilliant!!) Surreal beach in Santa Barbara. If possible, I highly recommend some Bud Light Tall Cans (You don't want to deal with bottles). Although it is illegal to drink on this beach, people do it all the time, just move underneath the cliffs to the right when you walk up so you'll have some time and they'll bust the other people first. And bring your guitar:
"Hmm Jenny, where should we sit?"
-"Let's sit by that guy playing guitar"

It's nice and sunny today  It's definitely high tide and there's big & nice waves  

There's side street parking where it's closest to the beach side.

Beautiful beach, easy access (street parking no lot), fun for a quick stay.  No public bathrooms.  Must drive a few minutes to shopping center.  Tame waves, kid friendly, cold water.  Stairs and ramp access.

Beautiful beach with street parking and located between resorts so in a weird way certain parts of the beach is secluded or "nicer." The waves are right, the sand is grainy. The section that I was laying on, had very small ratio of sand to ocean, but everyone was friendly and there were no vendors or boats out as it was just people and the ocean.

Prepare for tar stains and dead bees in the sand. I unfortunately stepped on both!  great sunsets with views of the rigs, not too busy, nice quiet area, lots of lady bugs, and a really cool sailboat wreck to take pictures of if the tide allows. There were some sweet dogs off leash and plenty of free parking too, btw. It was relaxing to sit on the the rocks and ledges above the sand and just look out.

Gorgeous beach. Slightly hard to access. Our family loved that it wasn't crowed. We were able to relax and enjoy the weather. 
What ended up being the major drawback for us was the tar/sap on the beach. All of our feet were black on the bottom and it took vigorously scrubbing that night to get most of it off.

Mediocre at best, very little beach and it's way too crowded even on weekdays , there's no service and I saw a needle on the beach from the homeless encampment.  Not worth the drive , I'm staying in Malibu next time.
Butterfly beach is beautiful but be aware that parking is limited and there are a lot of dogs (off the leash).

This is not a very long nor deep beach, but that is part of why I liked it ... it was a good place to bring a chair and a book and just chill.   

No matter where you sat, you were quite close to the shore, and there was the continuous nice lapping sound of the small waves that continuously hit.

A few people walking their dogs (some on leash, some off), a few people playing with their kids; but most people were just chilling, creating a nice relaxing setting.

Oh, and to my surprise a dolphin jumped out of the water right in front of us..... that was a nice bonus.

It is street parking only, but assuming you find a spot you are literally steps to the beach.

My favorite beach! 

Super convenient parking and you can bring your dog. The beach is pretty clean. It is just lovely at sunset as well!
Possibly the best beach on the West Coast.

This is one of the most chill beach I've ever been to. I came here with my girlfriends on a Tuesday during Spring Break and parking was super easy to find and it was also free!

Separating the street & the parking lot is a wide ledge that stretches as far as the beach goes which is great for sitting/picnic if you don't want to be on the sand. 

A lot of people bring their dogs here without a leash. I'm not sure if there are any regulations but I thought it was pretty cool. There were many dogs in the water as well. Even though there were many dogs, the beach remained clean.

A friend who lives in SB actually recommended this beach to me and told me it was actually her favorite beach out of all the ones in SB and I can see why. It really is a beautiful beach located in a private street across the street from a resort and not as much traffic as the other beaches. Parking is somewhat limited along the sidewalks of the resort and the beach side, but if you're patient, you'll be able to find someone leaving soon enough.

The beach is very clean even with all the dogs that are let loose off their leashes by their owners since the city also has dog poop bag dispensers located conveniently close to the entrances into the beach. Besides that, it's a great place to bring families for walks along the short shoreline to the public sidewalk or for kids to play in the sand. With a wonderful skyline filled with clouds and boats, it's very serene indeed at anytime of the day.

This is a beautiful stretch of beach.  If you are staying in Goleta this is your spot.  During the winter the high tide can wash the beach out, but it's still a beautiful spot to visit, even if you walk on the paths above the beach, the views are scenic.

One of our very beaches in SB. Simply stunning and amazing! A beautiful beach full of people taking it all in and the atmosphere was perfect for our family on this summer day. Parking is available on the street and even though the street was line with vehicles we always found a place to park. Free parking and a beautiful place to spend time.

Growing up, I enjoyed going to butterfly beach. This is a very family friendly beach where many locals still come to watch the beautiful Santa Barbara sunsets. Many tourists also end up here as the Montecito Inn is just up the street and the Biltmore is across the street. This is literally my go-to beach. My absolute favorite for laying out or picnicking. There's a beautiful path that goes up the side of the bluff if you keep going down the beach as well as lots of great skipping stones on the beach. This place is classic SB!

Traveling along the coast, one of the beaches my handsome navigator chooses to stop at was Butterfly Beach in Santa Barbara. Palm trees line the beach front, along with colorful flowers, but we didn't see any butterflies. Maybe it was the time of year. 

The beach was quite the adventure, we walked along and saw washed up sea critters, interesting rocks and parts and pieces of shipwrecked sailboats and yachts. It was truly fun to explore and relaxing.
Such a beautiful beach. My husband and I happened to catch the sunset here and it was a breathtaking view. Highly recommend visiting here if you're in Santa Barbara. Absolutely gorgeous.

This is one of my favorite beaches. It's located right in front of a beautiful Four Seasons Resort The Biltmore. There is street parking along the Channel Drive. It is sandy but I recommend wearing water shoes if you want to walk as you may step on tar. If you go towards Santa Barbara, you will find beautiful hills with rocks. I always take tons of pictures as it's hard not to! :) 

PS note that it's a dog friendly beach :)
Amazing spot! Very secluded. Little walking baths. Absolutely loved all my visits here. Takes you away and you forget where you're at.

There are few places in the world with such charm and such impressive powers to burn an everlasting memory. Butterfly beach, named because of the seasonal Monarch butterflies that stop on by, is located right in front of the Four Season's Biltmore Hotel of Santa Barbara. The view of the California coastline mixed with the view of the Santa Barbara Montecito area is indescribable. The beach is all sand and the water is about 65 degrees Fahrenheit. Parking spots are off the street and are always difficult to find. I recommend eating or having a drink at the Four Season's while enjoying the phenomenal views. This is a must see for not only tourists but everyone. I recommend all to stop on by.

I've been coming here since March of this year, when I was in the area and happened to find the beach. It's located in ritzy Montecito, and seems to be a popular beach amongst Santa Barbara locals. It's a good beach for swimming, surfing, sunbathing, walking, running and biking; the beach is dog friendly and dogs must be on a leash. There are no amenities here such as public restrooms so plan accordingly. The beach is perhaps a bit on the narrow side, and at high tide even more so. 

To get here, exit Olive Mill Road on the 101, and follow the road down which will turn into Channel Drive and you'll see the beach near by. Parking is all along the road and is free. 

There is a paved bike path bordering the beach with nice scenic views; the pathway borders private property that is filled with plants and beautiful flowers.

Coast Village Road nearby has various options for lodging, food, drink and retail.

The best beach in Santa Barbara! Serene and such a beautiful beach located just across the Four Seasons hotel. I really envy their guests because they have such easy access to this gorgeous beach. We loved the beach so much that we visited it twice in the same day! We walked on the road parallel to the beach and you can see huge mansions on one side and the garden with beautiful flowers onlooking the beach on the other. It was such a calming experience to just walking at the shores letting the water tingle your feet. A must visit if you plan to visit Santa Barbara!

One of the best beaches I have been to.  

I decided late in the afternoon that I needed to see the sunset into the ocean.  So, I looked on Yelp and found Butterfly Beach.  Done deal, lets roll.

It took about an hour and 20 minutes to get there from my home in the West San Fernando Valley.  I got a little lost, so i stopped at a gas station to ask where I was going.  They guy told me I was literally a block away and that parking may be a little tough because most people like to go watch the Sunsets.  

Anyhow, there was tons of parking along Olive Mill road and the beach wasnt nearly as crowded as I had expected.  I kicked my shoes off and marched the 20 feet to the ocean and danced along the onset of waves.  Remarkably, this beach is dog friendly and my lil Rudy had a wonderful time running around with other dogs and jumping over tide pools.  

As the sun began to set, I found a log that was perfect to sit on and watched its majestic splendor go to sleep into its watery bed.  Almost immediately, the tide began to rise and we were forced to move on. 

It was a wonderful afternoon.
Happened upon this special place one late afternoon for some peace and solitude. It was perfect.

This is where I take out-of-towners when they want to experience a breathtaking Santa Barbara beach. It's usually bustling with activity, ranging from walkers, people running with their dogs, high school kids playing around, even the occasional DJ daytime party. It also has some of the best sunsets around, and later in the day makes for a great moonlight walk with a special someone.

With the Biltmore across the way, you can hop by for a nibble or a drink after you're done, which is handy. Thank goodness beaches are public land in CA, unlike some other states!

Beautiful scenery. We had our wedding there and couldn't ask for a better experience. Everyone who passed by was incredibly nice and a few took the time to congratulate us and chat with us. Not overcrowded. Sand was soft and warm. Easy parking all around, but also easy access by bike or on foot. We'll be back.

We liked how beautiful it was. How clean the sand was.
Nice and peaceful beach for walking and relaxing. Chill beach vibes.

It's a beautiful beach.

It's a 4 start because it's not easily accessible for a family with small children, towels, chairs, and  beach toys. We used our wagon so it wasn't too bad but beware you will have to haul it down stairs and then back up.  Additionally, parking is a bit of a hassle.  

If you can work past accessibility and parking you're golden!

My all time favorite bike trail/walk.  One of the most beautiful beaches in California.  The bike trail is pretty short, with a few hills and dumps you on the road so be careful of people parking, people opening car doors.

the small coastline is absolutely gorgeous!

the women cruising by, even more gorgeous :P

its unbelievable how awesome coastline can be when theres no pollution to mess shit up. im sure the natural ethos will be more pimp daddy if the oil thingys werent around. im sure the navy base pollution doesnt help either. nothing against military...

on a good day, u can see channel islands towards south east. well i think those are channel islands. 

theres decent flat level hiking around here and some intermediate ones 20 miles northwest from here to overlook the farm and hang out in wind caves. either way, the coast is just good gosh have mercy! :D 

do not use the Lord name in vain (xx), #4. its before dont murder for a reason on the mosaic laws (ten commandments).

What a gorgeous beach! What a heavenly bike ride!
While we were driving from LA to Santa Barbara, I searched to find an easy and beautiful path in Santa Barbara, butterfly beach was one of them and we decided to go there and start our bike ride from butterfly beach. Once we existed we noticed a very cozy and gorgeous neighborhood, we drove towards the beach and parked our car. We got our bikes and started riding, a little bit further the scenery was breathtaking, we kept stopping on our way to the downtown to enjoy the view and the breeze. It was an unforgettable ride and scenery.

Stunningly beautiful beach situated across from the Biltmore hotel. You can see why they picked that location for their hotel as it's a ten for views. This sunset was particularly spectacular and a serious gift to the two brides who were getting married there that evening. 
It's not a hugely wide beach but then again it was high tide. We spotted dolphins off the shore, probably within a hundred feet. It was so special. 

It's sandy and the people were friendly. There's lots of free parallel parking in the area. People also had their dogs though I cannot say definitively whether it is a "legal" dog friendly beach. It was busy but everyone was friendly and enjoying the evening. I'd definitely return for a sunset here

Beautiful! Not sure where the butterflies were, but I didn't look, I was too busy laying in the sun like a dead fish. 

Not too big. People social-distance. 

Influx of dogs. Must love dogs. You don't want to be here if you have a thing against God's given animal. 

The water has seaweed. Duh. 

Would recommend
Love Butterfly Beach! Soft sand, dog friendly, warm water. Great easy bike trail and not too crowded.

Fantastic beach for a walk in the evenings. If you're lucky enough you'll get some great snaps of the sunset. I'd definitely recommend coming here for a walk as a tourist given how clean and well maintained this place is. There's ample parking on the street right opposite the beach as well.

Great for a light lunch snack and gaze at the wondrous prism of nature and earth, the sands of sweet America, I love the light that springs up and into the eyes of the person you are taking a picture of .....literally, or maybe, just your mind.

Yes, handle with care. Travel light. And may the wind guide your back and hold your dreams above fate.

You. Love. This. Beach?? 

Went here today after seeing the glorious reviews, and it was beyond disgusting and was a colossal disappointment; I will not hold back. 
If you love being on the beach with never-ending dog walkers with unleashed dogs that shit all over the beach, right in front of you and in the ocean, then by all means go to "Butterfly Beach." 
We came here to kick back and to do some skim boarding, but left after about 20 mins. First, one man let his dog down the stairs by itself and while he smoked his cigarette, the dog left a huge steaming pile, went up to the jackass owner and then they were gone. Then, another unleashed dog romped by us and then stopped and took a giant shit at the water's edge. The owner came over laughing with a bag and proceed to attempt to pick it up as the dog shit pile mushed into the ocean water. OMG, that is so freaking funny. How does that sound, folks? You're wading and swimming in dog shit and piss. Obviously, idiot dog owners know all about this beach and this does not have "occasional" dog walkers, as others have posted. It has a lot of these scumbag idiots on a steady stream. "Dog friendly" means owners of dogs come here with unleashed dogs and you get to see this kind of piss and shit parade plus you get to swim in dog piss and dog shit water.  If you overlook that fact and make excuses for it, then you are part of this problem. 
You'll never win the argument that 99% of all dog owners are NOT typically rude assholes who care more for their stupid dog than they do for regard toward their fellow man. These scumbags who I witnessed here at Dog Shit  Beach are perfect examples of that lowlife attitude. I feel sorry for the morons who ignore that and go to this beach, and for the ignorant people who pay hundreds of dollars to stay at the Four Seasons Resort Hotel directly across the street, and come over to this ocean cesspool with dog shit and piss in the water. Yeah, yeah, I know the ocean is not sterile but I don't need to see dogs shitting and pissing in it while I try to relax. I'd rather be subjected to a smoker than dog shit and dog piss ocean water. I can move away from the smoker.
"Butterfly Beach"--what a stupid name. Who exactly are the assholes who get to designate a beach to allow dogs? Effing brilliant, must be the same rude slobs that think dog-friendly human restaurants are okay, too. It's really not even that great of a beach even if the dog shitting wasn't prevalent. Didn't see one butterfly so this place is now re-named "Dog Shit and Piss Beach."

Its so so pretty here!! 

It's super clean and right in front of the four seasons hotel.

Parking is easy and doesn't require any payments.

There are beautiful dolphins swimming around for the past hour.

Lots of people picnicking on the beach.

This is an amazing beach. It's narrow - so narrow that a high tides forces you off of the beach. The people who hit this beach tend to be on the attractive side, those rich Montecito people take good care of themselves. You can walk along the beach all the way to East Beach.

There is an abundance of parking along the street but on some really nice days it can be very difficult to find a spot. I've eaten lunch here on a few occasions. It's really peaceful.

Great sunsets. Park along the street (Channel Drive in Montecito) next to the Four Seasons Biltmore and walk around or wait for the sun to set. You'll have to walk over rocks to walk on the beach. When there's a King tide, the waves sometimes splash up onto the street. 

Not too crowded in the winter. Definitely worth the trip.

This is the place to go for a panoramic view of beauty.

Butterfly Beach is one of the most well-maintained in the area. It's on the western end of Montecito and resembles the city itself when it comes to tidiness and overall maintenance. 

The beach itself isn't too large but is consistently clean. The sand is unobstructed by rocks or seaweed. The tide is usually low so it's not a spot to surf, but the sand area is large enough to sit down with a blanket and enjoy the ocean air. 

This beach borders a well-landscaped bike path and the now-shuttered Biltmore hotel. There is a large retaining wall which is a great spot to sit and enjoy the view without having to set foot on the sand. There is no private parking, but the street spots are a'plenty. 

This beach is also popular amongst celebrities. While I've never seen any of them here personally, I've heard that many frequent it on a normal basis. 

Definitely worth checking out for the beauty alone. Who knows, you may just happen to spot a celebrity yourself!

I've been visiting this beach since I was in college and I am still in awe of it's beauty.  This must be the the cleanest beach in all of Cali, a wonderful day to just spend the afternoon with good friends.

The monarch butterflies are way up in the tall eucalyptus trees in bunches but if you look up you can see a lot of them flying around. Hard to see but still pretty cool. Bring binoculars if you have them. There is a small souvenir truck as well and some signs about the butterfly life cycle. Went there for a couple minutes and took a short walk to the beach that's pretty nice. There's some parking on the street/road and portable toilets (gross but there in an emergency). Overall a nice quick pit stop if you like butterflies or want to head to the beach.

You know how I found this place, through Yelp. I just typed Beaches in the search box while I was in Santa Barbara. For awhile I meant to come to Santa Barbara because, I spent four years of my life living there while I went to UC Santa Barbara. I wish I knew about this place. It's pretty secluded and it seems only the type of beach where the locals hang out at. I didn't know about this beach even when I was living in Santa Barbara. It's a small beach when you go down the steps. Plenty of free street parking.

name: Coal Oil Point Reserve
formatted_address: Slough Rd, Isla Vista, CA 93117, USA

google_reviews.text: Beautiful stretch of beach north/west of UCSB. A reserve which protects native plants, dunes and shorebirds.  Especially the adorable Western snowy plovers.  Docents are on the beach, available year round, to provide background on the history of the nature reserve and point out shorebirds and baby chicks during the snowy plover nesting period which is( March-September) . Area for recreational  play, surfing allowed, but leave your dogs at home.  New policy as of 4/24, to help the nesting and thriving of chicks who are on the State endangered species list.  The fresh water, Deveroux slough provides additional birding and a beautiful Open Space for trail walks. A special place for beach walks, surfing, and birding.  Rules for a protected habitat are posted.❤️

A lovely place for a beach stroll. Lots of birds and beautiful stones, just watch out for those pesky oil spots that stick to your shoes! Loved the cliffs and saw some wonderful artistically constructed drift wood platforms and shelters.

Beautiful less traveled public beach access.  Parking is a little scarce, and you may get tar on your feet. Bring expendable water shoes & enjoy the views of the ocean, adjacent trails on the bluff, wide variety of sea birds and other creatures.

Great spot to watch the surfers, but there is a lot of tar that will stick to your feet. Interesting trails along the slough with lots of information about the wildlife. There is also part of the beach that is sectioned off to preserve the Snowy Plover habitat.
One of the most breathtaking beaches in Goleta

yelp_reviews.text: Beautiful spot to catch a sunset. People like to surf and do this thing with a parachute looking thing  in the water. It's interesting here... if you enter from this one street, you can lock up your bike,. Then go down the stairs and there's all these rocks and then there is sand. It's a great view of the sunset either from below or the top of the cliff !!!

There is a parking lot here, which you can get to by following Slough Rd from the intersection of Storke Rd and El Colegio Rd.  But I would recommend getting there via a trail that goes along the beach cliffs from Isla Vista.  You could park near Del Playa Dr and Camino Majorca, and it's a beautiful view and not too far of a walk. 
The walk is fantastic. Quiet except for the sounds of the waves.

What a magical place!! As UCSB staff, we got to tour this small, PUBLIC preserve with a docent as part of Staff Appreciation Week and learned so much!! It's an amazing place full of birds and rabbits and other small animals. 

It took us about two hours to walk the whole loop. That includes many stops to peep through our binoculars to see nests and birds. 

We learned about the Snowy Plover habitat and got to see migrating pelicans and a SEAL! 

It's a beautiful preserve and Polly helped me appreciate how much work has gone into restoring and maintaining this special place. 

You don't need a docent to tour the preserve. It's open 24 hrs and heavily signed and the trail is easy to follow. 

If you bring your dog, keep it on leash as many birds and habitats have been lost to loose dogs.
Oh man this is a great beach. It's even got some great logs to sit on.

This is a gorgeous space for a walk. It's very close to UCSB, meaning you may see many students here. Unfortunately right now, with Covid-19 and social distancing, it could be a difficult place to visit. People may not be giving each other room to follow public health guidelines. If that's a concern, you might want to stay away. Otherwise, be sure to bring your mask. 

I visited last week around sunset and the area was moderately populated. There was a fog rolling in across the Naples Point Marine Conservation Area and the view west along the coast was beautiful. I had to be cautious because I'm taking social distancing seriously and some folx on the beach and paths weren't giving me the space I needed. That said, I was mostly able to navigate with a wide enough birth to feel safe, and was glad for the fresh air, scenery, and exercise. It probably gets nuts when it's warm out, but for now as weather remains cool, I feel alright walking in the area with a face covering.

A nice beach with good views and tide pools if you come at low tide.

There is a port-o-John at the top of the hill here -- good to remember -- and parking is sparse. It seems that a lot of people walk here from the nearby housing, and even more run here and turn around at the point. It is a beautiful run along the bluff above the beach, or along the beach for part of the way. 

The snowy plover nesting area is along the beach, just beyond the high water mark. You should heed the signs and leave them alone. 

Like other beaches in the area, watch for the little tar blobs that get stuck to your feet, shoes and clothes.

name: Carpinteria City Beach
formatted_address: Carpinteria City Beach, Carpinteria, CA 93013, USA

google_reviews.text: Always a great beach to go to with friends and family. Got there at around 4 pm and found a parking spot right away! The waves were super calm, it almost felt like we were at a lake.
Good beach. I like the vibe of the town and this beach is pretty cool
Love standing on the beach watching sunsets here.

Awesome place to relax in the sun. Lots of rock though, my nephew badly scraped his leg so bring a first aid if you ha e wild kids. Over all a beautiful beach to head out to and y ok u just might see a few sea loins.

A nice, clean beach with very few rocks, waves are usually gentle and are very fun to boogeyboard in. Only downside is lots of seaweed floating in the water, but it never blocks swimming. Remember your sunblock!

yelp_reviews.text: I haven't been in the summer so parking hasn't been an issue.  The beach is definitely less crowded than most and compared to the state beach I much prefer it.  The only problem is rest rooms are not easily accessible, so why I give it 4 stars.  Great for kids, not exceptionally deep in terms of distance to beginning of sand to the water line so you can be safe on the sand then venture to water with the kiddies. I also like the fine clean sand and garbage cans were accessible so that helps people actually use them and keep the place clean.

Really great beach! The sand is clean and there are attentive lifeguards throughout the beach. Nice bathrooms are a huge plus, and the sand is not rocky at all. Definitely gets crowded, but nothing crazy. There are also some volleyball courts. There's a part of the beach that allows dogs (on leash) and a part that doesn't. Definitely a lot of families in the area. There's a lot of free parking but it can be a huge pain to actually find an open spot. 

Awesome, clean, and close to a lot of food spots on Linden Avenue.
Nice beach with beautiful views a carpinteria. Headto this beach during the avocado festival so you can get a nice beach front walk or run after indulging in some delicious avocados.

A top-rated for safety, good family beach.  Diving platform anchored 30 yards out from the beach.  Spectacular views of water in one direction, Los Padres mountains in the other.  Junior lifeguard training program on summer mornings is very well run, primarily for local youngsters.  Saltwater marsh adjacent to beach that is very interesting and well tended.  In June and July, tends to be cloudy in the mornings and sunny in the afternoons.  Crowded on weekends, when parking is difficult but workable.  Good area for biking (although nearest good rental place is several miles away @ SB beach.)  As you enter the beach at the end of Linden, the beach to the right is dog-friendly (and beautiful beachfront celebrity digs are partially visible), but dogs not allowed in area to the left.  Great destination on train along the Coast from L.A. or Santa Barbara, or direct from the Burbank Airport; beach within a few blocks of the Carp Amtrack Station.   Lifeguards friendly and helpful and the large restrooms clean and monitored.

MY HOME. Carpinteria beach is gorgeous, and if you go near sunset, there are always dolphins or a beautiful sunset to experience. The beaches are never packed with people even in the height of summer. I love this place!

This is the best beach in Southern California. Only Hawaii has better ones. It has soft white sand and excellent water for swimming and boogie boarding. This is a great place to bring kids and family for the day. Only in the evening is the beach is okay for dogs. There is amazing food a few blocks away from the beach sand. I highly recommend visiting!
Nice local beach in Carpinteria. Less crowded than most. We walked over here from our rental. Great little beach town feel with local campgrounds, restaurants, etc. near by.

The beach is clean, and we'll protected. The beach had  some sort of jellyfish surface a couple of days ago. They were all dried up, hardly any rocks. The wave currents are really strong. Be safe when entering the water. There were lifeguards, police and state park constantly patrolling. The restroom, showers and eating area are very clean aswell. There is a camping area right across the parking lot connected to the beach.

A nice beach, not too crowded and very clean. There's plenty of space to set up for a day at the beach. I feel the parking and occasional tar you get on your skin are the only drawbacks here. 

There's minimal parking so unless you're renting a place nearby with parking, you'll be parking blocks away and lug all of your stuff there. I suggest packing lightly.
It's got to be better than Carpinteria State Beach.  So dirty at SB. Bathrooms disgusting at SB. I will try the city beach next time, thanks.

Since the flood of 2017, it is a lot better. They had to redo a lot of the area.
Bathrooms seemed a bit better. People love it so much there, that the tent camping area is wall to wall tents with people you don't know. We had no problems 2018.

Pretty beach with some tidepools but the high cost, close proximity to others, and transients (I.e. bums) using the showers for free when I'm paying $50 a night is not real cool.
I won't be back.

Stopped in this charming town on a whim, hoping to swim in the ocean, and ended up just north of the apparently fabulous state park at the very nice city beach. Had a great time even though it was a crowded summer Sunday afternoon, thanks to the ample free parking on the nearby city streets, the clean public facilities that made last minute beach preparations a breeze and the cute business establishments and apartment buildings lining the way to the shore. The shoreline was clean but crowded, as was the water. The waves were a little rough and churned up so much sand that it was impossible to see underwater, but thankfully there were lifeguards on duty. I could do without the slightly scary undertow and the potential embarrassment of swimming right into somebody, but neither of those things would stop me from returning to this beach or to Carpinteria in general. Three cheers for randomly pulling off of the highway and discovering this delectable little slice of the California coast.

I like hanging out with pelicans, dolphins and seals. Don't you? If you don't, then this beach isn't for you. If you do, this beach is for you. Nuff said.

Oh, and clean up after yourself. Don't be a jerkstore.

Gorgeous beach. Pet friendly by the rocks -- but not one foot before! Lifegaurds will stop you. 

Really great piece of scenic beach here!

This is one of my secret getaways when I'm tired of the LA hustle and bustle.  I come here to recharge on the small hometown feel of Carpinteria and like it more than the glamour and glitz of it's super-wealthy stuck up neighbor, Santa Barbara.  No having to keep up with the Joneses here nor having to watch your back as it's a super safe place.  Just enjoy staring out at the view and watching the dolphins that usually play here or watch the campers pitch their tents next door at the State beach camping grounds.

The City beach is located at the end of Linden and is usually clean and not too crowded compared to LA.  There are a few picnic tables present behind the small beach store and a large public beach restroom close-by and another new, lesser used one by the Amtrak station, that is preferable.  

Plenty of free parking around town, except summers and during the Avocado festival that takes place during the first weekend of October every year.  Don't miss out on their great taquitos with guac or the avocado ice cream.   

If you're going to do it up, then take a ride on the electric-powered shuttle that costs 25 cents.  I took the shuttle around the whole town for a mini-tour of the place and stopped by the wetlands, the town museum where a docent explained how the town got it's name, the residential areas east of the freeway, and finally the Carpinteria City Beach. 

Before you leave the town, stop by "The Spot" just up Linden from the beach for a snack and kick back on their outdoor patio.  

Ahhh, rest and relaxation!

I love it here...  I wish they allowed dogs but I get why they don't and there is another beach not far where you can take the pooch.  I always find this beach to be quite clean and the little area surrounding it isn't full of hotels so it somehow feels like a "locals beach" as opposed to the bigger touristy beaches in SoCal.  

Perfect for a beach walk or a picnic lunch at one of the provided tables.  Definitely a Southern CA treasure.

Don't walk your dogs off leash here, they often will ticket you and it's silly how expensive that ticket is. Other than that, great beach!

In the Summer you will never find a parking space here as it becomes total tourist central. Can you blame them though? Carpinteria has the best weather in the summer, the most delicious burgers at The Spot, local tunes and brews at Island Brewing Co right on the train tracks, the Snow Cone trailer, volleyball courts, the platforms you can swim out to, dolphins leaping through the waves, and awesome shopping down Linden Ave. It's beach town heaven!

During the months that aren't flooded with people camping at the campgrounds and natural day trip tourists, I tend to visit Tower 3 & 4 most often because the parking lots are most empty and the beaches have more shells from the lack of people walking over that way. The waves are always small and the sand always feels great between your toes.

Because there is a tar pit so close by you will most likely get little speckles of tar on the bottoms of your feet at this beach. It's totally worth it thought and so easy to get off with oil. So walk the beach, play some volleyball, layout and tan, and view the beautiful lowkey waves while watching for dolphins riding into the unforgettable sunsets that occur here.

When you show up at the beach in your shein bikini and hope the 12 year olds don't recognize it ;) I love this beach! It's so nice, such a relaxed family oriented chill vibe. Grab brunch then head down. The temp of the water is perfect! I can't keep out if it, a perfect breeze and vitamin sea - what more can you ask for?! There's usually neighborhood parking just drive around a bit. 

Good stuff right here!

And yes, the bathrooms are open.

Carpinteria City Beach is by far my favorite beach in SoCal. It is lauded as the safest beach in America and I have to believe that to be true. The beach and for that matter the town are hidden gems in a state that seems to be filled with hustle and bustle. 
  Most of the folks you'll meet at "Carp" are locals or almost locals, generally traveling from places like the nearby Santa Clarita Valley to camp at the beach. Unlike Huntington, Newport or Venice Beaches where there are a multitude of beach rats and surfers, Carp is a family spot. There is still a large teen population at the beach, but most of these kids are well behaved mainly because most of them are with their parents. There are a lot of younger kids as well, but the beach is big enough that they aren't right on top of you. Parking is usually easier than most of the beaches to the south and I've never been concerned about the safety of my vehicle. The beach at Carp is always very clean and there are a good number of lifeguards patrolling the beach regularly. Near the campground there is a large park, sand volleyball courts and a snack shop. Restrooms are clean and at regular intervals up and down the beach.
  One note I want to pass on is the presence of tar in certain areas of the beach. There are large oil deposits off-shore and a fair amount of natural seepage takes place as a result. These areas are mostly at the south end of the campground and the tar will stick to your feet. There isn't a ton of it and it is easily removed, but it can stain the carpet in your vehicle, rv or beach rental if not addressed properly. Just be aware of it and check your kids feet for sure.

It's a busy but lovely city beach. I recently caught a lovely sunset here. We found on street parking very easily and walked over. The beach was clean, filled with families and views of the Channel Islands for days.

It's easily accessible from downtown. So you can eat or shop before or after. I highly recommend a visit the next time you have an hour or so to spare or spend the day! You'll be glad you did.

One of my favorite beaches in SB. County,  go a little north of the campground,  sand is always clean,  good for swimming it's always calm,but of course cold, kind of a locals beach , where ever we travel we fit right in with the locals,  cool restaurants on the main drag.

name: Parma Park
formatted_address: 2066 Stanwood Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93108, USA
categories: Local Flavor, Parks, Hiking, Active Life

google_reviews.text: It was a beautiful spot!!! Expected it to be a higher altitude to see the sunset and ended up running the trails instead because it's kind of a valley. But absolutely stunning none the less!!! Highly recommend if you're looking for a nice nature trail without many people around!!!
A great place to hike .There are many trails to choose from. Places to sit at benches and tables.
Great park in the hills above Santa Barbara. Several hilly trails that take you around to the beginning. Beautiful views. Watch out for Rattlers and be prepared to hike uphill!
Nice trails just minutes away from downtown. Good mix of shade and sun depending on what routes you take. Parking is limited.
Great park for walking. This is also where the Parra gliders and hang gliders land. No bathroom though. Good for nice walks and dogs.

yelp_reviews.text: I'll say that I'm not a 'hiker' as many are here in SB, so I don't have the know how of the various trails and parks throughout Santa Barbara county, but I live across the canyon from Parma park and until recently, I'd only been there twice. It's a shame because, it really offers some beautiful views, nice steep hikes as well as some wide open and flat areas as well. On weekends, (starting in Friday) youll see many hand gliders use this area as a landing zone. Horse riding is allowed here and it has diverse trails that are great for everything from horse riding, dog walking, jogging or casual strolling. It's just far enough out of the way to get away from artificial parks within the city, but close enough that you won't feel you're going to run into coyotes on your way up. Check it out.
3 miles of hiking through dog shit all over the trail and foxtails. This is the worst park I've been to in Santa Barbara (perhaps in life?). Skip it. Go to Elings or hike Romero Canyon...

Took a hike on a very hot summer day.  Love the terrain, chaparral, and views.  Beautiful trails.  Did 1 hour full loop of the park and only saw one other person, which I can only assume was due to the heat, since I saw many bikes tire tracks and boot/shoe prints from recent days/weeks.  Only reason I gave it 4 stars instead of 5 is that I wish it went further up into the hills, although that's probably not possible due to the private property all around.

Very scenic "park". Prepare for heavy hiking, this is not by any means a flat easy stroll. Beautiful peaks and views. 

The maps of the trails could be useful if the actual trail signs indicated anything other than "trail". Budget extra time to account for losing your way a bit and guessing your way back. 

If you bring a dog, long haired dogs will absolutely need a dog shoe or some kind of covering for their feet. The ground is LOADED with filaree thorns. We didnt even allow our pup in the grass and yet his feet were so painfully packed with them we had to cut the hike short and spent over 2hrs pulling them all out.

I absolutely love this park and think it's a nice challenging, short hike. Most trails lead back to where you start and are clearly marked. There are a few paths that will lead you to a dead end and I'm not sure why there isn't a sign pointing it out. I hate having to backtrack up a hill after you think it's going somewhere. 

It's a lot of steep hills and dirt, so wear shoes with traction.

Watch for rattlesnakes and tarantulas if your dog isn't on a leash! There are many holes with them minding their business.

name: Carpinteria beach
categories: Beaches, Local Flavor, Hotels & Travel, Parks, Active Life, Campgrounds

google_reviews.text: Realmente bella playa
Clean

yelp_reviews.text: A wonderful beach to walk, play in the surf and just enjoy the beautiful waves.  We love it.
Such a great beach! Clean and soft sand!! Really tough to get reservations at the campgrounds. Only down side. And and the train. A little loud at night.

Been coming here for 20 years but the park rangers/camp police are the biggest jerks. They make up rules as they go like a 8:30 curfew for kids and other nonsense. Won't stop going here but I wish they would get new staff.

I love camping here, especially mid week, if you go to the most southern part of the campground, San Miguel. you get to be right near some nice open fields with trails to walk on and near a beach that's outside the campground that you can take your dog on...

I'd love to say this was the best campground we've ever stayed at, but there is some baggage to be aware of.  I really must give ratings of several items for this review:

Price: 1 star
Location: 5 stars
Layout: 2 stars
Hosts: 3 stars
Bathrooms: 3 stars
Campsites: 3 stars

$70/ night for a 20x20 slab of asphalt?!? Way overpriced in my opinion. Spots were tiny and the RVs were literally crammed in like sardines. 

I see a lot of complaints about the staff. We didn't have any issues with them. They don't go out if there way to assist you buy they have 2 people running 150 camp spots. They are busy.

Overall the kids loved the location and we really enjoyed ourselves, but as far as the campground layout and price, it was just OK. Probably will not visit again.

This state park is a dump.  The bathrooms have not been acid washed and power washed in years and are just disgusting.  That is the ones that are not "out of order."  I spent 3 days in this state park and the same "out of order" facilities never changed.    
If you have an RV or Trailer, good luck getting them into the tight sites outlined in white lines.  Yes, white lines on asphalt.    Once you get your trailer in, park your tow vehicle next to the trailer, you bang your door trying to get in or out as the sites are so narrow.   Camping at this state park is like being in the Walmart parking lot on "black Friday."  It is so crowded and nothing but asphalt  in half of the campground with electric and water hook ups.  At $70 a night for water and electric, you can do much better in a private campground. This place is a real dump.  It needs a good cleaning and paint.  They cram the RV sites so close to maximize the profits I am sure.  Many people had to buy 2 sites to get their rig in.  

The good thing is the location on the beach and in the town of Carpenteria. .  
A good cleaning, paint, and some landscape work would do wonders.  It's a shame at $70 a night and our tax revenue the place is so run down.  I have stayed in many other Ca state park campgrounds that were wonderfully maintained.  This one is NOT.  I would avoid camping there---it's just disgusting.  Camp else where and come to use the beach only.  Beach is great.  Rest is a rip off by the state of Ca.

If you are camping, Don't go here.
There are far better places to stay unless you like Asphalt, People and Trains.

It's an OK beach.  If you are into tidepooling, the tidepools are decent, but you have to time your visit just right with the current tide schedule.  If you go at any time other than the lowest tide point, a lot of the good stuff is underwater.

worst place we stayed on our surf trip down the coast. The only reason it gets a second star from me is because it is a nice area to stay in. if you want to just visit the beach with the family its fine, but if you want to camp here be ready to be let down. however to be fair there are hot showers here (when you can get hot water, because only the lucky first few get hot water. so shower early). you will be lulled to sleep by a roaring train charging right through the campground and laying on the horn. so peaceful you wont be able to here the waves crash as you fall asleep because your ears will be ringing and every dog will be barking. as for space, you might as well just reserve three camp sights for your self so your tent dose not have physically to be touching your neighbors. personally i like my space, what your doing in your tent is your business, i don't need to hear it.

This is such a beautiful place to come and go camping. I have been coming here for years and look forward to it every year. Your close to downtown and can ride your bike almost everywhere. The beach is very clean and the campground itself is kept up nicely.

Carpinteria State Beach has been our go to beach for the past 2 years for just a picnic day trip to relax on the beach. My friend and I wanted to get out of the Santa Barbara beaches that were filled with seaweed and sand flies. 

Last year we didn't know and ended up in the camping area day parking lot which we were charged $10. This year I did some research and found you can park on the street for free. We found an abundance of spots. 

I brought a picnic and we pitched an umbrella more near where the hotel are located. The beach was empty around this location and we spent a very relaxing day at the beach.
Great beach. Easy parking. Clean bathrooms.  Close to restaurants.

Famous for being "The World's Safest Beach", it definitely feels safe and friendly. 

In the summer, or really on any hot day, it is a little over crowded - but that's for a good reason :) It is a great beach area to mess around with a surf board, boogie board, or even just a bathing suit!
Not much privacy but very close to the beach. It's a little pricey and the showers don't have hot water and they charge you for it.

This is the best beach camping. EVER. Especially if you have kids.

I don't know why I'm telling you this, since you'll just be competing with me for campsites. But okay, I'll spill and give you the skinny on this place:

-This is the BEST place for families to camp anywhere. Families come here, and the place is overrun with kids. And that's a good thing. If your kid doesn't have a friend with them that's their age, it's virtually impossible not to quickly make a friend. There are more bikes than cars here on weekiends, as most families pack up the bikes. It makes riding into the town easier. 

-The campgrounds are located on a beautiful beach, and you are steps away. The sound of the waves put you to sleep at night.

-However, the campground is a victim of its own popularity, being the most popular in the state beach system. Rangers and yellow-jacketed volunteers must enforce the rules with a vengeance, or the crowds would overrun this place and spoil it for all. You are limited to 8 people per campsite, and must park your car on the pavement, so as not to kill the grass. Because of all the families here, there is a quiet time. After 10 PM, you are requested to keep the noise down. Kids riding bikes must have a helmet. Things like that.

-Back in the day, the first ones to the showers got all the hot water, and there used to be very long lines for the hot showers. However, they have implemented timers on the showers. This promotes conservation, and helps everyone have hot water. Bring your quarters, and avoid the "handicapped" showers. On those, the water does not get above 'tepid'. There's a sign that says so. Ye been warned.

-There are four campgrounds here: Anacapa, Santa Cruz, Santa Rosa, and San Miguel. The first two are mainly for tent camping, and the others mainly for RV'ing. So if you are in Santa Rosa or San Miguel, you are in a site designed for an RV. Don't reserve there and complain about the small space for your tent. Anacapa and Santa Cruz have more of a "free flow" to them. Sites are not clearly defined, and were designed to promote community and the group camping experience. If you want private sites separated by hedges, try McGrath or South Carlsbad. There are some sites that are a bit more private, but they are tough to get.

-Speaking of tough to get, you need to really think ahead. Reservations are all booked within nanoseconds on the first of each month. For Memorial Day, for example, you need to book November 1 at 8AM PST. 

-The little beach town of Carpinteria is as cute as they come. Little mom-and-pop restaurants, small shops, and a small town atmosphere are all part of the charm.
