 - There is a fee to park the car in the Carpinteria Beach parking lot, but there is free parking just on the neighboring streets
 - Make a trip to the Harbor Seal Preserve between December and May; it is very likely that you would see harbor seals basking on the beach sands (Harbor Seal Preserve is adjacent to the Chevron Oil Pier)

One of my favorite beaches!
Came with my girlfriends for Alumni weekend. We camped out at the campground. Soooo much fun!
It was the perfect weather for a camp night.
The people by our campground, we stayed in San Miguel campground, were very helpful!
We forgot a hammer to set up our tent and a guy nearby helped the 3 clueless girls on setting up the tent.
Definitely will be coming back.
We didn't make reservations because they're always booked way in advance but arrived by 8am and we got the last spot in the lottery.

I always love coming here.  The beauty and camp sites are unparalleled .  The town is fantastic.  But today we had a horrifying experience in the San Nicolas section of the campground.  He came and accused us, in a belligerent and intimidating way of breaking rules with OUR hose On our spigit.  We found out later that everything he said was NOT a rule.

Perfect early morning place to go out on a run along up to the bluffs which is about 4 miles. And you get the added benefit of the ocean to cool off in after. 

Or if you want you can hurl wet sand at the person who you thought was slower than you but held back as if they had been planning this maneuver. 

Maybe it was spite, maybe it was contempt, 
or maybe it was just because they could.

The gall.

 
Never been to this park before
Scenic.

I'll 
be back...

Beautiful beach. Good surfing and very safe for kids. I go here every other day with my boys to run free. The tide pools are fun to explore during low tide. Just remember it is illegal to mess with starfish and other marine life. Fantastic sunset every night :)  ahhhh

There are four parts here. If you have kids stay in the north side because the tar along the beach won't allow for fun at all. Very interesting tar pit park, tar literally is coming out of the ground here. Also there is an active railroad directly behind you! Good for overnight.

Our campsite was right on the sand and very comfortable.  The beaches are beautiful and have spectacular views of the sunset ! Lots of areas for the children to play. The restrooms were plentiful and clean and had all the amenities you could ask including shelter for your clothes and handicap seats.  There is also a spacious are to wash your dishes

What a lovely beach! 
Boogie boards for rent
Snow cones down the street
I would comeback here without a doubt!

Oh, I would live at this campground if I could!!
Sits right on the beach in quaint Carpinteria ("the Cute Little Beach Town that time forgot")...
Walk to food, boutiques, surf shops, antique shops, coffee, salons, etc..

My Personal Favs all walking distance from Camp:
Seafood/Steaks: Slys ($$$) or Teddy's-by-the-Sea
Pizza: Nutbelly (organic, reg/gluten-free crusts)
American: Zookers (Local fresh Organic), Rincon Brewery, Worker Bee Cafe 
Thai: Siam Elephant, 
Hamburgers: The Spot, Padaro Grill (if u drive)
Beer/Wine: Island Brewery, Rincon Brewery, Corks
Coffee: Starbucks, Coffee Bean, Lucky Llama 
Sweets: Robatailles candy shop, Rainbow ice cream, crushcakes cupcakes

I just RV camped here for the first time and from the reviews I expected to be dissapointed but I was pleasantly surprised.  Is it crowded?  Yes.  Every beach camp site is in Cali even in the winter.  All that said, it is worth it.  

My site was one row away from the beach.  Dolphins, sea lions, surfers, sun, night time strolls on th beach with lights was a hit with our kid, hiking and more.  I could see, smell, hear, and taste the fresh salt water in my small RV for three days straight and that is a wonderful thing.  

We played on the beach daily, walked for miles, and had lunch in town just a short walk away.  Family friendly for sure!  We even brought our dog as it seemed everyone else had as well.  The town of Carpenteria is a clean and friendly little place with shopping and dining galore.  

Your first time here can be tricky deciding what camping area has what between the four of them.  For example, one has trees, two of them are parking lots but have hookups, some rows in some areas have hookups while some don't, some allow tents and some don't.  Also, some spots are just for RV's and some just for tents and the sites all vary is size as well.  The reality is that the place is so busy all year long that you will either have to book months in advance or just get luck and get what you get.  

There is one dump station for the whole place and at check out time I only had one person in front of me.  The dump station however was nasty.  I stepped in actual poop on the ground that some moron let run out if his poop tube.  Skip it on a busy day unless you must use it.

We didn't use the showers or bathrooms.  They looked pretty clean but varied from area to camping area in cleanliness.  Some were older and more rustic.  

At night, the camp grounds were quiet and all I could hear was waves crashing.  I didn't see rodents as some reported.  If you have young  kids, there is a lovely interpretive park by the train tracks heading towards the Vons grocery store that your family will love.  Looks to be new!  If not, it is kept up nicely.  We will be back for sure!  So what did I hate?  Our specific site had a fire ring one foot away from both a fence, a bush, and our neighbor's trailer rendering it useless and even possibly a safety hazard.  I thought I would hate the train but it didn't bother me.

Nice state beach which gets crowded during the weekend. Parking may be tight near the beach but you can park on the main street(Linden) and just walk. Lots of places to eat and drink on Linden. The beach itself is very long and if you fancy a nice evening stroll this might be the place.
So, it's our first day & we've been robbed, tent attacked & now a pack of raccoons tried to jack some Oreos...not a great first day but, the beach is awesome...

I went tent camping with a church staff member as a reprieve from a 3-day weekend with a bunch of kids/teens. We were very fortunate to get one of the beachfront sites. 

The beach & campsite (C214) we were in was clean; however, It lacks privacy as we were right next to the other sites without a tree or bush to divide us.  Each site is your standard site with a BBQ pit & picnic table. 

You can't stake down your tent at this site & those next to it because there's cement underneath the sand. 

They had coined showers & it's well maintained for restrooms at the beach. Due to the drought, they shut-off the water to the rinse-off areas. :( 

I'm not a dog person but just an FYI - dogs aren't allowed on the beach according to their camp rules.

It was nice & quiet when we were there in late August on a weekday. A nice safe camping place for kids too. There are Lifeguards on duty during the day. We usually take some church kids/teens camping & we would feel safe bringing them here if we were able to get a few spots next to each other. 

I would definitely come back. We also checked out the following campgrounds along the coast such as Refugio, El Capitan, and Gaviota, but we preferred Carpenteria SB over the other ones because it was cleaner, more kid/family friendly, and closer walking distance to the beach for the overall campground.

Our church group used to camp at Jalama Beach, but I think I prefer Carpenteria SB. Jalama has gone down over the years & not as well maintained & the rules kept changing at least when we last went.

First of all I would like to say the beach is absolutely gorgeous. It is a wonderful place to come visit with paths to a charming town and eateries closeby . However attention must be called to the fact that this is our third year in a row we have encountered the same horribly petty, rude and abrasive State Park employees ( Some are elderly volunteers w attitudes) who seem to revel in perpetuating misery upon anyone who dare use "their" park. ( Isnt it "ours?") It is bad enough the facilities have gone completely down hill with little to no  upkeep or maintenance the last few years. The place is undermined by rodents (ground squirrels) the water has been turned off to  the showers, the spaces are literally the size of a small bedroom. Some completely covered by sand .Even if the campground is empty and you are paying $70 a night for  full service hook up , the employees will tell you that you are at their Mercy to wherever they "ASSIGN you" and on a whim  just tell you you can't park in an ocean front spot. Even if there are no other reservations for them. In this particular case my elderly parents ..my dad in a wheelchair my mom on her first vacation in 2 years after a double knee surgery were told they could not move into an ocean front slot even though it is the same amount of money even though the campground is not even close to full. When she asked why she didnt have a choice they said because "We Decide'... now my mom is one of the most easygoing and considerate people  that I know , but their imperious snarky  attitude  and their need to cause trouble and unrest at every turn completely ruins your festive mood and stay .Several others that were there at the same time experienced the same over-the-top rudeness. Instead of parking  a car at the site. They wanted them to park  almost 50 yards away in an isolated lot.. when 3/4 of the place is empty !!! On top of that they wait until you set up  to tell you that you can't park over the numbers that are  impossible to avoid  with such a small spot. When is the State going to take some responsibility for these  people.  Unhappy people mad at the world and taking it out on others around them, is  not a benefit even if they DO work for free. WE PAY! I see this is far from the only review about this problem... people should stand up this is not acceptable.

This a beautiful beach!  I enjoyed just stopping by for a short time, but it can no doubt bring hours of enjoyment.  It is conveniently located just blocks from downtown Carpinteria, so food and shopping is close by if you don't bring some.

Traveling with a pop up camper, family of four roadtripping from Ohio to California over a three week span. Being from the Midwest and camping a lot through there and the South, Gulf Coast and areas in between, I must tell you this has not been a pleasant experience for any of my family. I have never met so many unpleasant, unkind people in my life. Our kids are 15 and 10 and they can't believe how poorly they have been treated in these camp grounds. I pray it's been "Murphy's law" or a fluke but we can say with certainty, we will never camp in California again. We are currently at Carpinteria Beach State Park and it has been the WORST experience of my life. Camping? It's packed in like Walmart on Black Friday. The facilities are disgusting and the camp volunteers are ridiculous. We have felt singled out more than a few times because of out of state plates. We have received TWO citations on our vehicle while there over two days. One for being 2 inches over the parking line (we drove a Ford Expedition EL and we were given a walmart parking space to for our truck and camper in for 3 days. We had been in the campground 20 minutes enough time to unhitch, pop up the camper, try to adjust it to fit and sit down to figure out where to park. He didn't even attempt to say "welcome to Carpinteria, we are happy to have you camp here but we will need you to make sure your vehicle is inside the line" etc. He sat and watched us unhitch and then sped down to ticket us.  The other citation was for parking in the 15 minute Lot outside the pay showers to take a 5 minute shower. Wash, dry, dress and run to the car. We made it as the officer was writing the citation and he was a real jerk to us. The volunteer elderly woman on a powertrip golf cart gave us a verbal and written warning for our very small compact generac generator running at 10am because her watch didn't say 10 yet, one minute to go. She intentionally stalked out site by turninng around in front of it several times to take pictures and write her note. How about some welcome hospitality first?  Seriously people. Yet barking hound dogs, fireworks, screaming kids went on all night and the stuff that was jam packed into the park was insane. It looked like an episode of Hoarders. The place was filthy and unkept. Showers are so gross for being pay showers. $1.25 for 5 minutes should warrant being campground clean at least. The grounds we nasty and the smell was gross.   We have stayed now at 5 places and head to San Diego tomorrow. At this point we all want to get back to the Midwest. Just a heads up, if you really want to experience a true camping experience with fun, relaxation and amazing camp hosts and staff go to Florida and Alabama. We are a modern homeschooling family that travels very often, we are easily pleased with simple things and landscapes but this has been a dreadful experience. 

We have camped so far in Lake Tahoe at Camp Richardson, Yosemite Pines in Groveland, Mazda Seca Laguna Raceway, Seacliff, Carpinteria Beach and head to Los Rancho Cochas in San Diego tomorrow. We don't stay in the site in the daytime as we have visited San Fran, Yosemite, Lake Tahoe, LA, Hollywood, Ventura, santa Monica etc.....

Pretty beach with some tidepools but the high cost, close proximity to others, and transients (I.e. bums) using the showers for free when I'm paying $50 a night is not real cool. Oh, and the trains coming by every hour add to the sucktastic experience.
I won't be back.

This campground was a huge disappointment! We paid $70 for full hook ups for our 16ft trailer. The spot we were assigned 329 in the Santa Rosa Loop was so small and we couldn't even back into it due to all of the other campers being so close. We got a different spot 430 in the San Miguel Loop which we could more easily back into because of the angle. Despite this we felt like we were just in a parking lot. The staff was very unfriendly. In addition there has been no upkeep.

Worst campground I ever saw. It's basically a series of parking spaces with a spot for a tent behind your car. The one star is actually for the very nice beach but the camping is atrocious! It seemed like a shanty town. I cancelled my reservation five minutes after getting there but ranger was unwilling to give a refund. Avoid unless it's just a day trip. Continue north for much nicer state beaches!

Over the past 60 years, My family has been to numerous camp sites throughout California, and although this is one of the most beautiful, the park rangers created the worst experience for me both times I went. 

  You are immediately profiled the moment you register, followed by them taking notes on their camp site map in your spot section to check on you frequently when they feel your appearance warrants so.

  During my first time here I was told less than 10 minutes after arrival, that my truck was parked incorrectly, and that I would be fined if I did not move it. My front bumper was literally 2 1/2 inches over the line. Next, I was told to lower my music (during the day) when I was playing it through my phone only with without a speaker. Being that I had a spot in the back, I was not harassed frequently this time, but it got worse as the spot I had improved. 

  The next time I went, I had a spot up front. I was greeted as I mentioned before, but this time, was immediately given the impression that these are considered somewhat of their "flagship" spots. The park ranger gave me a double take, like, what am I doing with one of these, I take it because they are expensive. As I began to settle in, less than an hour after I arrived, two rangers came straight to the spot I rented with urgency and gave me a speech, basically reciting the park rules, regulations and consequences, then questioning my intentions as if I was already in some sort of violation, but was not. I was extremely embarrassed as my family and the tenants of the other near by spots watched. They made a spectacle out of me for absolutely no reason. Once it became dark, they came back and went though our tents with their flashlights, as if we were hiding something, and asked me multiple times when was I leaving. Again, was extremely embarrassed. I have gone my entire life without having a police record, but was treated otherwise here.

  I believe their actions to be unjust and will never camp there again. I also have not been camping period since. Our 60 year long family tradition has now been ruined. If you who are reading this, and know how it feels to be profiled by law enforcement, expect it to be taken to a whole new level at the Carpinteria camp grounds.

This beach serves a peaceful purpose. I love hearing, watching and enjoying the waves crash. I especially like walking the beach when it's not really crowded.  I've taken my art supplies and have just been inspired to paint whatever comes to mind at the beach.

This is honestly one of the ugliest hiker/biker campsites that I have ever been to.  I have been cycling the entire Californian coast and this is a truly pathetic example of a campsite. It's also one of the most expensive at $10 when most hiker/biker rates are $5 in California.  The staff here is ridiculous too... I rode a hard 70 miles and arrived beaten by the road only to receive a very impolite "We don't accept cards here." from the woman working the gate. She claimed that they are "old fashioned". That's pathetic... because every other state run campsite accepts credit cards.  As a bicyclist, I recommend you avoid this ugly place. Oh and if all that didn't convince you to avoid this place... I'm camping next to a bunch of homeless people that the park staff allowed to stay here. They shouldn't be considered hikers just because they walked here... One guy came to the campsite with a baby stroller filled with stolen bicycle parts :(

We just spent the weekend at this great camp site. We had a perfect beach site.  The grounds are well kept, the beach is clean and deep. We did not consider the trains at night to be an issue as the children were so tired, they slept right thru the train noise. All the camp staff were friendly, helpful and responsive.  We are experienced campers and just as we obey all camping rules and courtesies, we expect other campers to do the same.  It is nice that when someone is out of line, another camper need not get involved....the rangers handle the situation. The only negative I have is there are no dedicated trails to the beach outside the individual campsites, those campers on the inner circle walked thru campsites to get to the beach. I was surprised at first, but once I became accustomed to people walking between our tent and table, it was ok. It would be nice if there were marked dedicated trails between campsites, but since there are not....just go with it and have an wonderful camping weekend.

If you got a beach view it's great. The campsites are literally right on top of each other. Kinda hard to back a trailer in if the campsite it full. Also not really dog friendly. Camp host told us we needed to back our truck up more cause it was literally only 1 1/2 inches over the line. Showers were nice except for the homeless naked people walking around. And the trains...I wouldn't go back unless I absolutely had no where else to go.

Great area and could be an amazing place if it was privately owned. I have come here every summer since I was about 5 and every year the place is more and more neglected. Prices go up and up and quality down and down. $85 per night for sites that are buried under 2 feet of sand while the park staff sits there on their tractors not doing anything productive. Paid $1.25 for a cold shower and the camp host who was sleeping says sorry we don't deal with that. What exactly do the camp hosts do other that take up space for free?

Such a beautiful place ruined by poor state management. I wish the California Park system would just sell out to someone who could actually manage such a nice piece of real estate.

I have been coming here yearly for 29 years and have absolutely loved it.  This year we had to split up our campsites due to availability.  The first part of the week we stayed on the Anacapa side and it was amazing.  The camp hosts Bob and Judy were great and they kept the bathrooms so clean along with providing hand soap and flowers....such a fun touch and then we moved to Santa Cruz where the experience went from 5 stars to 1.  The camp hosts (2, side by side) were rude and it looked like they have been living here a very long time.  In addition. The bathrooms and showers were filthy with one handicap shower deemed "out of order".  I firmly believe the camp hosts keep that shower locked for their own personal use.  I cannot beleive the difference one side to another makes.

Camped here March 10-13, 2019. The worst thing about this place is the extremely TIGHT quarters! We have a smaller Trailer (28') and with my truck, there was NO privacy at all. It's borderline ridiculous. The town of Carpenteria itself is really nice. And, the State Park is really nice. We enjoyed walking around the paths. I'll also give a shout-out to Rori's Ice Cream in town! Holy cow! Best ice-cream I've ever had! Will we be back? Doubt it. Too many other camp spots that are WAY better.
Beach was nice but I had southern older small camp site.  bathrooms were nasty and campsite tiny.  biggest issue was train passing several times at nite

Small beach. Broken shells and seaweed. More crowded than I expected on the weekend before 4th of July, but lots more open space further down, I wish we had walked further before setting up camp. Not so bad like Santa Cruz since it is a smaller beach. 

It was pretty clean and small towny-feel on the walk down. Good amount of parking.

We have camped in a few places around California, and just tried Carp for the first time. It was an absolute dream. The weather was fantastic (mid-September), the beach was right outside our tent door, there are trees for shade, and easily accessible bathrooms. Only con is that the showers cost money (bring quarters) and the parking is $10 (cash only) per day. Still, this is par for the state beaches, and a small cost to pay for such a wonderful place. We will definitely be back next summer.
Nice place to enjoy ice cold beer. BYOB. Can't complain, restroom nearby, campground, outside shower

Went camping at Carpinteria on July 19-21. Hands down..one of our most enjoyable camping experience ever. Our campsite was right next to the beach (C031) where you hear the ocean waves and walk 20 feet to the sandy beach.  It's a very family friendly campground with lots of kids riding bike and playing on the beach so it was perfect for my two little ones. Although the sites were relatively small, everyone was really respectful of each other and kept the noise level down after quiet time @10pm.

Pros: sleeping to he sound of the waves, watching sunset on the beach, free movie night for the kids on Thursday, clean restrooms with hot showers.

Cons: only staying for two nights.

Can't wait to come back next summer.

The Carpinteria campground sits in an ideal location right on the beach. That being said only about 1/4 of the sites are actually on the sand the others are on dirt. No site is far from the beach, but only a handful are ON the beach (you can look up the site map on their website).

The staff seemed to do a pretty good job keeping the restrooms clean and the hosts provided hand soap in every bathroom and outdoor sink which is unusual and much appreciated! I have no complaints about my experiences with the staff, everyone was really kind to us. 

From the campsite not only can you walk to the beach, but also a small set of tide pools (at low tide, tide charts are posted throughout or pick up a guide at the kiosk), tar pits, and the "old town" portion of Carpinteria where you can get candy from Robitailles which was the creator of the Official 1985 Presidential Inauguration mints. 

Overall I highly recommend Carpinteria, especially for young families (there were tons of children to play with). My two complaints are that the sites themselves are not cleaned in between campers, there was trash throughout our campsite... foil, wrappers, straws, broken plastic ware. No one should leave a campsite in that condition, but they should also be checked and cleaned. Second is that the campground is OVERCROWDED. The spots are packed in so tightly you can hear EVERYTHING next "door" even when people are using normal voices. There is very little privacy.  Basically you HAVE to embrace your neighbors, which is not my favorite camping style.  So introverts beware!

We camped at the Santa Cruz campgrounds midweek in early August. It had it's ups and downs. Overall, I'd return, but it wasn't the best camping experience ever. There were clearly people here who return yearly and stay for a solid week or more. I'm not sure if we could handle that. But for local car camping, it certainly hits the mark.

Pros: You are in easy walking distance from the beach. You can hear the surf from your tent. There are relatively clean (though sandy) coin operated showers and bathrooms. There are large sinks for cleaning up after meals. There is a little walking trail that takes you right into town in case you need groceries (Smart & Final), a coffee fix (Coffee Bean), take out (Thai, Mexican, Pizza, etc), or you want to go antiquing (several cute looking antique and thrift stores). At the end of the walking trail, there's a great playground (ages 3-6?) and grassy field for play. We also attended the Ranger Program about stars, which was super cool. Afterwards, the Astronomical Society had telescopes set up for us to observe what we had just learned. We got to see Saturn through a telescope, which was amazing! They also have a visitor's center that is open Friday-Sunday, where you can check out naturalist backpacks with activities for you to complete (like tide pools, fossils, etc). They also apparently have a small tide pool tank set up, though it's new, so there's not much animal life currently. And the beach was fun. We saw sand crabs and seals frolicking. My children loved that part.

Cons: This place was crowded! And everyone had a camper, RV. We were in the campground without hookups but still. Each sit had 2-3 tents, a camper, and 2 cars. It was crazy. I felt like we were right on top of everyone else. The sea was freezing, much colder than our LA waters. On the website they say they have a "rodent" problem, meaning ground squirrels. Boy are they not kidding. The camp ground is littered with holes from their tunneling, and more are being added daily. We woke up one morning to find a new hole outside our tent, and our shoes partially submerged within it! And there's a train that literally runs right next to the camp, all day, and 1-2 times at night, like middle of night night. I'm talking clanging railroad crossing signals, horn blaring, lights flashing. For us, not a problem. Others might take issue with this.

Tips: Consider bringing a shade. Not all the camp sites have shade, meaning it can be quite hot in your site, especially around lunchtime. Bring baby oil with you to get rid of the naturally occurring tar. The beach was named Carpenteria by the Spanish due to the boat building operation by the Native Americans that existed on this beach. They used the natural tar to seal the boats.

I read that it is considered one of the safest beaches in California.  We quickly found out that is because you are not allowed to have any fun there.  If you wanted to surf or kayak, you had to walk a half mile from where we were to the "launch" area to take out your soft top surfboard and you had to stay out of the surf zone unless you were in the small surfing area.   This was on a Wednesday mid morning where you could count less than 10 people in the water between where we were and where they wanted us to launch.  Logic out the window with the head of lifeguards there.  I have been at considerably more populated beaches in southern cal and have never seen such ridiculous rules.

The campground is mediocre at best...if you like camping in the middle of the city.  I think there are parts of the campground that are better, but we were in the group sites at the north end and you are camping on the edge of a park with zero barriers to anyone walking through your site.  We had to have the park ranger call the police to remove a few homeless people nearby when we first got there, it was fine after that.

These camp sites are TINY!  If you are not on a beach site you are kind of screwed.  The fire pit was literally 5 feet from from our car which was parked as far back as it could go - it was covered in ash nightly.   The beach and general area are great but not worth being packed into these sites.   Your tent will literally be 2-3 feet from your neighbors or closer.   Oh and the 6:30 train wake up call is rough.
Carpinteria State Beach was a pleasant place to visit in December: lightly trafficked, quiet, and beautiful. I am very interested in staying here someday.

Beautiful views, clean, lots of trails, fun and definitely coming back this summer! We stopped here on our way to Solvang. Highly recommend this to any beach goers!! Bring your Mountain bike too for the trails on the bluffs.

Best kept secret south of Santa Barbara! My husband and son would come every year before the first day of school. It was their tradition. I was never allowed to go.  We finally VRBO'd a place for our anniversary and we started going every year on anniversary for 3 years. When a place where we stayed came on the market, we decided to go for it and made an offer! Best decision! It is wonderful beach!!! People walking on the beach are just nice!! It is not a surfing beach because there are hardly any waves. So it is good for young families. You can walk to see the tide pools and to the Seal Sanctuary or you can just sit in the beach and do nothing. I cannot say enough great things about this beach. We are proud and blessed to be part of this beach community.

Great family beach! Sandy and plenty of room. Very convenient to walk to the spot for a burger or get a snow cone from the truck. Plenty of bathrooms available and parking. Carpinteria is a cute town, fun to spend the day walking around and checking things out.

I was so excited THEN when we get there we are literally on top of each other, literally felt like I was camping on a parking spot. They were also workers making noise and that did not bother me too much but the worse were the rude retired workers who constantly come around telling you to scoot into your line of camp site and what topped it off is JUDY in her mask coming up to you at 10pm SAYING ITS TIME TO PUT YOUR CHILDREN TO BED AND EVERYONE NEEDS TO GO TO BED! NOT EXAGGERATING OR LYING! I would have left and asked for a refund but we had other friends there that just arrived. I will not go there again. I will have more fun at Walmart parking lot

Reservations are very hard to get. Plan and research ahead of time.  I usually call in late the morning of and then have several computers going. Reserve America has recently updated their website so become familiar with it.

It's a great location, but you are still camping in the city.  All sites are CLOSE to each other. It is what it is. But the beach is CLOSE too. Bathrooms are clean and separate from the showers. Showers are clean and coin operated.  There are sinks with hot water for washing dishes.  Anacapa and Santa Cruz loops are for tent or RV camping. Dirt sites.  Santa Rosa and San Miguel are primarily for RV.  Asphalt sites. There is a big grassy park where you can play. The beach is right there. The little town has all kinds of restaurants, grocery stores and a laundry-mat. They have a little shack where you can rent all kinds of beach items. It's a very charming beach town without being Santa Barbara aloof.

This is one of the best beaches ever!!! Really fun and safe.  
Carpinteria is the cutest beach town.

 Great stores and restaurants all within walking distance.

 Go to lunch at Sly's and the Robitaille's Candy shop!

Did I mention free beach parking!!

I want to say 5 stars cause this is a GORGEOUS beach! But I took 3 stars off because the people who work at the campground aren't interested in helping or making things easy for you. 

When we wanted a spot with hookups, they had us pay the $50, then sent us driving around to see if the one open spot would be big enough. It wasn't, so then we went back to the front, and they sent us to drive around the other section of the campground that's dry/no hookups and said if we find a spot NOT to setup and unhitch but to come back and tell them the spot, then go back and setup. Which then ended up also being $50 for the dry camp spot because I guess some of their spots range in price which they didn't tell us? And we didn't wanna drive around again to assess the cheaper spots, so we didn't get a refund. 

Doesn't sound so bad except the entire park is narrow winding roads like a maze and we are a 31 foot trailer, maneuvering was slow and difficult. So then after 45 mins and finally getting set up, they tell us some mysterious rule about having to be parked so our vehicle wasn't over the number on the pavement, not even a little. There goes another 45 mins to pack back up and rehitch, move a few feet, then unhitch and set back up again, just so I can spend a single night here, cause they don't allow you to stay more than one night unless you reserve in advance. And these spots are as narrow as a Walmart parking spot, and they expect to fit 3 RV's next to each other in these narrow spots... Not even close to worth $50 if I have to feel like a cow in a pen. 

So all in all, I'm left in a bad mood with my day mostly over which I had paid for to come relax at the beach. When travel is your life, these ridiculous rules and difficult employees make it quite a nuisance to stay at their parks. For charging an arm and a leg during the off season, the least they could do is be more helpful and less irritating.

Carpinteria beach is ok.  The small beach has volunteers to keep it clean.  You need to pay $10 to park the vehicle for the day.  Also, shower rooms will ask for coin deposits.  The campground has RVs and tents.  It might be an eyesore considering how the Southern Cal may have lots of homeless.  The bathrooms are well kept. The walkway deck protects the dunes. There are better beaches.

Beautiful day here and since we are camping nearby we thought we would check out this state beach. Told a the front gate that we would be able to drive through in October. I say bs to these guys for that comment!

Camped here this weekend with 4 friends, first time at Carpinteria. Campground space was SUPER tiny.  Had to squeeze 3 tents together in an extremely cramped space, literally up against each other.  Very little trees/shrubs, privacy, basically sharing all of the campsites with the other campers.  Perhaps there are better campsites, although we didn't see any larger ones.  I believe we stayed on M148 & M149 sites.

The tide-range here is quite dynamic (at least when we were there).  Neat tide pools and reef formations at low tide.

Pros:  The place teeming with wildlife, especially the ocean.  Saw several dolphins, grey whales, and birds.  Bathrooms are somewhat decent for a campsite.  The bluffs and cliffs are also very peaceful.

Cons:  Lack of privacy & space at campgrounds.  Basically a tailgate campout in the parking lot. Lame.

Two words, Park Ranger.........their jurisdiction is the park, and the park only. They are so bored , that they knit pick any little issue.  They make it very clear that they are real police, and can arrest you if need be.  Here's a quote "sir, how much have you had to drink" , Me "a hell of a lot, and I'm not even close to being done, thanks for asking". They are constantly watching you, and harassing you,   If they don't see your dog on a leash that's longer than 6 feet, trust me, the camp host will be sure to rat you out immediately. 
Beautiful area, ruined by Barney Fife park rangers who were beat up in high school.
P.s. if you do decide to go, be sure to use your library voice by the campfire, heaven forbid the empty campsites around you get disturbed.

Sigh...

This place had great potential. The sites were nice, a little small but OK, the beach nearby was a plus,  showers and bathrooms were reasonable, benches and fire rings were good amenities and the soft ground was just right for building a tent. 

So why the 2 star review you might ask? Well as another reviewer had said, this place falls victim to its own popularity: our fellow camping neighbors were TOOOOOOO LOUD AFTER QUIET TIME!!!!!!!

It's not the fact that they were loud, is the fact that there was NOTHING the park had in place to handle it other than a self enforced 10pm quiet time rule. Needless to say the quiet time rule was not well "self enforced" by our neighbors. They were not even our "next door" neighbors, they were about 30 yards away or so but I was still able to hear every single word spoken, spontaneously blurred out or sung.

I noticed their loudness early, but since it wasn't quiet time yet I let them be for the time being. As quiet time approached, I noticed they were making no preparations for quiet time, so as 10pm came and went, I decided to give them more time to settle down, hoping they would soon settle for the night. BTW at that point I hadn't slept in 30 hrs, so this explains why I needed the quiet time to sleep. I assumed there would be some type of night patrol enforcing rules like in other campgrounds, but as 11 pm arrived and the noise level did not drop, I was pretty sure there was no night patrol.

So now at this point (11:30 pm) I'm horribly annoyed by their noise and lack of respect for their neighbors, so I first decided to just "tone them out", I even used ear plugs, but then they busted out a guitar and the noise level jumped! So that was that, got to do something about it. I decided to walk in the cold, misty night to the ranger station at the entrance of the campground. Lo and behold it was empty. So I then decided to call the park rangers office (somebody, anybody that could help) with no avail, office closed. 

No choice left, I had to take matters into my own hands. So I went to strongly tell them to please end the noise. A soft apology from them followed and they toned down their noise from a 10 to a 7, not quite what I needed since they should've been at 0, but I had to take what I could.

Sorry to bore you all  with my story of trouble and grief, but I really wished it didn't have to come down to that. It pretty much ruined my camping adventure, I was only there for 1 night but glad I didn't stay longer cuz those people were still going to be there. I live in the big city (LA) and I really wanted to get away from all the noise. Sure the campground was full but everyone else was quiet like they were supposed to be. Really wished they had a night patrol to handle what I didn't have to handle. I don't plan to come here again until I hear of night patrols being used.

I spent a lot of my childhood at this beach and have very fond memories.  We started coming here in tents and quickly moved to an RV, and then pretty much all of our friends and family came along.  Great place to get a few spots and have a great time while the kids all play in a little (big) thing called nature.  
They have fire pits & a bench, showers & toilets on site which were usually very clean.  There are tide pools where you can look at all sorts of stuff and shops and food in town, in walking distance.  Many times I ran in to other friends I went to school with while camping there.  Can't wait to go back again

Great beach! Really great soft sand and beautiful mountain views with many tall palm trees in the foreground to add to the scenery. We camped here for one night and had a good time. It was also very peaceful sleeping here because you could hear the waves crashing, but it's far enough from the freeway that you couldn't hear anything but ocean!

I just returned from a 4th of July trip to Carpinteria State Beach after 4 days and it was great. I hadn't been there since I was 16...it's been over 4 decades. It was better than I remembered. It was crowded but not a problem. The newer showers were nice compared to 1974, and if you get there early ( 2pm) there was no waiting in line ( Santa Cruz campsites). The place was clean, the town was clean, and there is so much to do if you take your bikes with you. Living out of state was a drive, but it was the best time I've had in ages. I want to return!

My review is going to be for the campgrounds at Carpinteria State Beach. My Girlfriend, her parents, and myself camped out for the weekend of 9/23/2019 at Carpinteria State Beach. We traditionally have always camped out at Leo Carrillo State Beach, but because of the recent fires had to make a plan "B" and this was it. My Girlfriend and I definitely expected more or at least equal in comparison to our traditional spot.


It's pretty funny as we all looked at their website, pictures, etc. and really was excited about the camping trip, green grassy areas, shady trees, nature / eco-systems by the beach. We drove up to the guard's station Friday afternoon. We looked around. We looked at each other and said "They lied!".


The ranger/guards to begin with let's just say "Total lack of a personality" not personable at all. The one's we dealt with during our stay, just weren't friendly. Unlike, Leo Carrillo State Beach, they are amazing. They are always super smiles and super friendly.


Now back to the grounds, nope nope nope can't go by the website. There is very little grass, mostly dirt, and it looks like trees have been cut back. At least in the A section aside from where you enter, almost no shade what so ever!


The beach is a beach, but there are no natural eco-systems. The beach looks as if it has been flattened out and sterilized. However, it it still a nice beach.

Only go if you get a waterfront spot....#s 434 to 446, 125 to 146, and the beach spots only in Santa Rosa quarters.  I won't go again unless I acquire one of these. You don't want to sit in the dirt or set camp on an uneven tiny dirt slope or open meadow with hundreds of peoples and bikes.  Half the park has no shade. The reservation system is wrought with inefficiensies and I have a feeling that there is all kinds of ....shit and unfairness going on cause they (the experienced regulars) know how to work the system.  There is a fight for beach sites and you better figure out how to wrangle one. 
 Some of the hosts are not that good at customer service and have an unusual way of welcoming you to the park.  Usually. it is to give you a citation.  Some of them are not "people persons" and it is foolish that you have to think of making "nice nice' with them. They always have their eyes on you! Some push their weight around and do not know how to approach the campers in a fun way nor have the skill to correctly tame down certain situations.  
It is a beautiful beach and I saw many happy people enjoying their surfing, boogie boarding, fishing, walking/strolling, building sand castles, surfing, kayaking, watching the beautiful sunsets and just plain having fun.  Much luck getting a space on the water.  I lucked out for a few days (lottery pick) and took away wonderful images in my memory.

My wife and I have been coming to this beach for over 20 years from San Diego area . It's has always been one of our favorite places . Unfortunately this will be the Last time !!! It's so sad how dirty the campsite has become the bathrooms are disgusting and don't flush properly, you probably could get ringworm or some type of bacteria!!
Don't even get me started on the showers !!
The people on either side of us didn't camp they used a 5th wheel ,got drunk and used The F word till midnight ,thank you park rangers , 
The park front desk was extremely rude !!
Trash was piled all over dumpsters and raccoons all over because of the lack of cleanliness. Hopefully some improvements can be made until then ABB for me and the Fam . I will be asking for my money back for 2 Terrible nights !

This is a great campground. We were right on the sand. Our site was so close to the ocean, you could hear the waves! Mostly, that is Wonderful! It just adds to the experience of beach camping, and it can also lure you to sleep. Although, it can be a bit annoying when it's 1AM in the morning and that HUGE wave jolts you awake.

We went to the beach SIX separate times in our 2 days of camping here so far (one left to go!), the beach is simply PERFECT for swimming or beginning bodyboarding. Honestly, this has to be second best beach I've ever been to, and that's saying something considering we live 15 mins from the ocean. But that #1 spot has to go to some hidden beach that isn't on Yelp yet, near Oxnard. But, they need to have a separate area for the fishers, someday someone is going to get pushed into the area where the guy is fishing and will get hooked (in a bad way).

The campground is basically a parking lot. Only the beach sites are good, you can get away from all the vehicles and just relax from the sand and have a scenic view. But the inland sites just aren't that good.

Our electric hookup wasn't working, but the mechanic fixed that overnight and we are not those type of campers where we need all the hookups, for us they are just an extra, so it was fine.

Today we went to see the Tar Pits, and they were really cool but I learned how I got tar all over my body after swimming in the ocean. I had to take five, (yes five, I am a clean freak) showers to get all that tar off me! At least there were a lot of nice & cool places to watch the sunset, or just rest with a scenic review. Also, bring your phone because I warn you that all the scenery will simply force you to take a photo or a selfie.

We liked the campground so much that we booked a site in March just minutes ago. Thankfully, another beach site because the very inland spots are just not worth it.

Oh, and you can deliver pizza to your campground, just dial up Rusty's Pizza and they'll deliver delicious pizza!

STAY AWAY!!  We camped in Carp's Santa Cruz loop and had terrible CA ground squirrel issues. The guy in #220 had $1,500 damage done to his engine wiring during a 2 day stay! I kept my hood open but they still chewed on my air conditioning hoses. We will NOT be back after many years of loving Carp. We cannot take on this kind of financial risk to our rig. If you have a problem, you are then stuck in Carp until a local shop does the repair. It took the guy in #220 a number of days to get his vehicle repaired. The worse thing was when the rangers lied to us, saying that they had never heard of the squirrel problem!! Guess they never talk to their camp hosts who freely discuss all the squirrel damage done to their own rigs. DON'T RISK YOUR RIG DUE TO THE STATE PARKS NOT DEALING WITH THIS INFESTATION. IT EXISTS IN EACH CARP CAMPING LOOP. This park makes hundreds of thousands each year. They need to spend some of it protecting the campers expensive rigs. I was told Refugio SB is even worse.

Great space to camp overnight. The only downfall is the santa Cruz and most sights are right along the train tracks so you will occasionally have the trin go by. The camp grounds quiet hours are at 10pm so plan accordingly.

This is a fairly nice and cozy camping area.  Lots of chilled-out folks seemed to be visiting and it's right near the beach.

The train noise can be bothersome to some people, and I'm not used to having to pay for showers while camping (I usually go to fresh water sites and bathe in lakes/streams/rivers), but these are minor complaints.

If you like camping, but don't want to rough it too much, this is a great spot for you :)

It's a parking lot at the beach.  Zero privacy.  Unsupervised children all over the place. People walk through your " camping site" to and from the beach. A train runs through and blasts horn at 5:30am.  You will have no peace or relaxation here.  The beach is nice.  I would come here like any other beach to enjoy the day.  Camping? No way!

I love Carpinteria. This small town is a gem. Lindon Ave is great with little shops and great restaurants. And the whole town is right on the beach. My girfriends and I were about to move in to our apartment on Holly Ave when our landlord said it wouldn't be ready for another week. We are a pretty flexible group so the option of camping sounded like an advanture. It was the end of August but we still didn't have any trouble getting a tent only spot for the week and set up camp! The first night we were playing cards and listening to music from our ipod speaker. A park ranger came over to tell us we got a noise complaint. Amazing!! A noise complaint when we were vertually the only group in the tent only section. Tough crowd. We didn't make that mistake again. I would recomend this site for an RV you can park right on the beach and if you brought your dog then youre in luck because the beach is dog friendly! If you walk south down the beach you will reach the seal sancuary and depending on what time you go you can either walk right down to Rincon a famous surfing beach or you wont be allowed to go any further because the town protects the seal sanctuary during mating season :) This is a lovely community and the people that live here are great.

We have been camping here for a long time. It used to be wonderful with the location, trees, the beach and just raccoons that you had to be weary of, but it's been declining over the years with the prices continuing to increase. The first con is in getting a site; it is like a two minute lottery months out online. It is very stressful and often you don't get the whole week that you're trying to secure your vacation. The selected dates are cut short and you have to move sites plus pay for a second reservation fee. On our most recent visits, the campgrounds and bathrooms were INVESTED with ants. The camp host was quite rude to us in our interactions and demanded exact change for the firewood she sells. At $6 a bundle, is quite expensive. We go siren the road for $3/bundle. I would understand if this serves a conservation effort or regular ground maintenance but so far don't have anything to support it. They should be fixing the sewage station on the grounds because waves of fecal odor plague the air and it's super gross. The price of showers has gone up which I wouldn't mind if they were consistent and warm. Finally, the rangers are quite different than they used to be; they used to be young men that were polite and welcoming in the park, keeping on eye on things, now it's a bunch of irritable women. Generators run all day from the RVs. When they shut off at 8pm you can finally try to settle into your spot. I hate to say it but we probably won't be returning in the years to come based on the decline and condition of this park.

The good stuff.

Close to the beach. If you pick your spot right, you can almost be on the beach.
Close to downtown Carpinteria with cafés, restaurants...walkable/bikable  distance

The bad stuff

A train goes by all day and all night. 
The smell of poop was all over the campground. No way you could avoid it. maybe a problem with their sewer system?
if you have an RV you will feel like a sardine laying in a box. The RVs are just in parking spots next to each other's. The area for tents or People who don't need to plug in is way better. 
The shower were advertised hot but were broken.
If you have any issue with your reservation, the park rangers are useless. They will tell you they are not responsible for anything because they hire a company to deal with the reservations. 

I would not go back.

What a cute little beach town! I live about an hour away & have been threatening to visit here for years. I finally made good on my word. Not sure what took me so long. We had such a lovely time walking around the beach & town & sampling local fare. It's coined "the safest beach." A statement that I would not try to challenge. It's very clean, cute, & people are generally nice and happy. So excited to have this gem relatively close by.

I know everyone seems to think that Carpenteria is some kind of "religious-experience" amazing place.  To me, it is California beach camping at it finest:  60 foot trailers and campers, stacked up practically on top of one another, in an asphalt parking lot adjacent to the beach.  It's a stretch to call this camping, as you could effectively do the exact same thing in a Walmart parking lot.

The place is perpetually crowded, and finding reservations within a year is damn near impossible. Only by stalking for cancellations can anyone reasonably expect to find a spot here.

That said, a few sites have hook ups, and most are very close to the beach.  The beach itself is pretty clean, although the stretch of beach adjacent to the campsite is covered with easy-ups the night before, effectively blocking off people's spaces.  The culture of making reservations here means people actually stake out their spots on the beach the night before.

The beach is mostly Sand, although there are some rocky stretches, particularly at the south end where an asphalt seep creates tide pools and a rocky outcropping, with mussels, starfish, anemones, and  crabs. 

If you enjoy surf fishing, you will find that at most times of the year, there is a lot of algae, seaweed, and eel grass churning around in the surf.  This affectively gets caught all over your line within a few seconds of casting, and makes fishing here a little bit of a pain in the butt.  Still, there are perch here in late winter on the spawn, and on our most recent trip, we managed to land a few yellowfin croaker and a Corbina.

For a brief weekend getaway, this place is not bad... pretty clean restrooms and shower facilities, with showers starting at a quarter.  If you're looking for a particularly untouched and natural area, better keep driving.

Es una playa salvaje en toda regla, lo más seguro es que te encuentres con delfines, leones marinos... incluso algún tiburón pequeño. Pero no te asustes, no se acercaban a las personas, cada uno respeta su espacio. En la playa puedes encontrarte de hasta una cabaña hecha de madera como un balancín! El agua estaba bastante fría pero estaba muy limpia y muy cómoda porque son de las que andas y no cubre.

Nosotros nos alojamos 3 noches en el RV park que ahi allí. El camping está genial, pero cuidado con no equivocarte! Las dos primeras noches nos alojamos en Santa Cruz, pero si vas en caravana no tiene ni agua ni electricidad y duchas van con monedas! Intentamos cambiarnos en cuanto nos enteramos pero eran más de las 12 por lo que nos hacían pagar la primera noche doble, al día siguiente volvimos a preguntar pero como era más de una noche debíamos cancelar la reserva y hacer una nueva con lo que el reembolso tardaba 3 MESES! La última noche ya por fin simplemente pagamos la diferencia. Nos movimos ha Santa Rosa donde la playa estaba hasta mas cerca. 
Cada sitio dispone de una mesa y un pequeño bidón para hacer barbacoas! Prepara la leña, la carne y los algodones de azúcar! 

Ten cuidado de no equivocarte a la hora de reservar tu spot! 

En nuestro instagram podrás ver mas fotos sobre Carpinteria: @juntos.viajes

Camped here for first time this weekend and it was crowded. I wasn't expecting that, but it IS the beach. So. We paid for premium spot right on the water. Spot 376. We were moved because the website double booked but the ranger was nice and found us this spot which was great. So if questions or concerns on booking- they seem on it and accommodating!!! There is a ton of drift wood, fyi, so don't bring wood. And the firepit is right there on the sand. The hook ups worked great, neighbors were nice, raccoons do come out at night- so keep your food locked up!! Tide pools slightly disappointing but lots of shells and smooth rocks on the shore. And tar! Who knew this place was booming back in the day for tar! 

This place looks cool also for day camping! There is a nice "walkway" when you enter and go to the right of the entrance shack. Parking is 10.00 for day or if you are overnight camping- an extra car... dogs are NOT technically allowed on beach and a ranger will tell you. (But I saw everyone having their pups on the beach) lol. My two dogs camped with us and it was great. 

If you camp and want to walk around or stay after the noon booting you out- the ranger lets you move your trailer, not pay for parking to a little spot just past the shack. It worked well for us....

Last thing to say, walking on the sand barefoot- slightly difficult here on the beach. The rocks, beach glass, shells, in many places- it is rough. Bring sandals!

Do you enjoy being attacked by a drunken host?
Do you enjoy not having a full spot, but paying full price? 
Do you enjoy paying extra for what you should have gotten in the first place?
Then this place is for you!
I have been coming to Carpinteria for years. We've never had a problem until this trip...if you can call it a trip since we didn't stay more than two hours. We arrived to find that not only was our spot under construction, but we had no room to park our car because they had the table bolted in the part of our spot where we usually park. When we parked (part of) our car in the spot next to us (MANY OPEN SPOTS) because we didnt have enough space, we were confronted by a drunken, belligerent host, named BILL, and his wife. He came to us from the start with an aggressive attitude. When we tried to explain the situation, he decided the appropriate reaction to our reasonable complaint was to lunge at my husband. His wife tried holding him back, screaming, "Bill, no stop!" This tells me this is not the first time it's happened since she seemed to be expecting it. I doubt this will be the last time due to how the park and Deputy Wilson handled the situation. We went to the main kiosk to voice our concerns and upset about the situation and they offered us another spot...FOR FIFTY DOLLARS. Shortly after we went back to our original spot, we were approached by Deputy Wilson. We explained what happened and then we were told (by Wilson) that we must've given him a reason to physically attempt assault on us. This is coming from a law enforcement officer, paid by tax payers and the guests of this establishment to protect you. But apparently he was only concerned with protecting the drunken  buffoon (Full Definition of buffoon. 1 : a ludicrous figure : clown. 2 : a gross and usually ill-educated or stupid person.). I believe that sums him up nicely. 
Instead of doing his job and taking care of the problem, he offered us a full refund, which we won't receive for weeks. Even though they take the money as fast as they can get it.
RESERVE AMERICA should NEVER rent out spots they know to be inadequate.
So, campers beware. If you go here, you may be attacked.
(REVIEW WILL BE GOING UP ON YELP AS WELL.)

It is OK. Nice beach with some tidal areas. The camping parking lot is huge. Cute little town to walk into for food and things. However there is lots of tar and during holidays and summer it is crowded. There is also a train that runs through multiple times a day as well as late at night when trying to sleep. There is a weird gas or oil equipment on the grounds that smells like sulphur or rotten eggs sometimes too. Otherwise it is pretty popular with out of towners. Us locals go to the other beaches not so over used just up and down the coast a few minutes with less tourists.
Not that crowded plenty of places to stop in and window shop and eat and enjoy the scenery...nice small town atmosphere.

We stayed in site 201, near the ranger kiosk and beach access. The site
is huge and shaded with bathrooms and showers within 50 feet. Arrival
May 31, 2016, departure June 5, 2016. Site has squirrels and you need
to protect your food. They will tear bags and boxes open.
Weather was very pleasant with an overnight mist that left everything wet,
so cover things up.
Bathrooms are attended to first thing in the morning with the campground 
host checking on things during the day. The crew and the host Doug do a 
great job. Doug also sells firewood and is knowledgeable about things if
you need info. B,J, at the kiosk checked me in and wasvery busy with phones
and questions from folks, He juggles his responsibilities as a Park
Ranger Assistant and takes his job seriously and does a good job. 
The park is located close to many restaurants and shopping. We had a very nice trip to a well managed park.

Again only negatives were the squirrels and also a mockingbird that sang in the middle of the night for like three hours above where we were sleeping, Camping is getting close to nature, so be it.

The beach is beautiful and safe, but the bathrooms are horrendous. During a pandemic you'd at least hope for soap, but that was the least of my problems when I couldn't find a single restroom with toilet paper! Not sure who is maintaining the restrooms, but PLEASE take note. And to anyone visiting, bring loads of hand sanitizer!

First off, you are going to pay $10 to park in the main parking lot and ticketing is enforced. They have you download a parking app and you pay through the app. You are out of luck if you don't have a smart phone. They are not accepting cash or cards at this time. 

There are public restrooms available and outside showers to rinse off. The bathrooms started off strong with being clean but then I used them 3 hours later, they were pretty disgusting and being depleted of toilet paper. Oh, and there is no hand soap in them; just a sink and a quick shot air dryer for your hands. So make sure you have your hand sanitizer close at hand. 

There are so many great things about this beach! For one, the location. You can walk from the beach up to several restaurants and ice cream shops and bring your food back to your spot on the beach. 

The beach itself is very nice. There are no dogs allowed so you don't have to worry about avoiding dog poop or any stinky smells. Hooray!

There is next to no seaweed being washed up on the beach or floating in the water. So again, no stinky rotting seaweed to worry about and no sand fleas. 

All in all, it's a nice family beach and worth the steep parking fee. The parking is good for 5 hours. After that, the app will ask you if you need to extend your time. We stayed 4 hours. That was enough for us.

Okay, so this review is probably (okay, it is) a bit on the negative side because it was so freakishly hot. While yes, it was freakishly hotter in the LA area, we thought it might have been exponentially cooler while camping by the beach. Such was not the case. 

My partner in crime,Yelper JL, booked this site about 6+ months ago when the state parks opened up their reservations for the upcoming months. Being an avid beach camper, he made sure to nab as many locations as possible and within reason. Little did he know that this weekend would melt our face off. On the bright side, we ended up exploring this great little town and discovering fantastic breweries and eateries while finding ways to escape the oppressive heat. 

We nabbed slot 417 on the San Miguel Loop of the grounds. Super close and within seconds to the restrooms and hot showers. Always a plus! Of course, there's the downside of the bathroom traffic, but I'd rather be close to a restroom. The showers take quarters, so bring your own or make the trek to the main kiosk upon entry. Other than that, decent beach access and fantastic views. If you can, stay on the Anacapa grounds, as the showers and restroom are private. After that, the Santa Rosa and San Miguel loops are communal.

Now for the beach: Lots of sand fleas (yuck) and very little surf. We didn't bother to bring the boards, as they weren't worth the weight for flat conditions. Additionally, due to the recent oil spill, there was tar all over the beach. This was terrible. We didn't realize it until we looked at the bottom of our feet and saw the huge globs. One fellow camper said mayonnaise would take it right off. We didn't pack any in our coolers so we ended up using olive oil, plastic knives, and Dr. Bronner's peppermint soap to scrape that toxic sludge off. That tested our patience to no end. 

Maybe it was the heat that that got to me, maybe it was the tar, but I'd wait a long while before camping here again. As noted above, the discovery and accessibility of a great town made this experience tolerable..

The single star is for the beach/sunrise. On an early morning walk here two days ago, I saw a used condom on the walking path! Now, I know this is SoCal and all but being alone and early in the morning I was both scared and disgusted.

Absolutely beautiful beach except a lot of tar which can really mess up your feet. Was there less than 24 hours and harassed by old volunteer workers twice about not having my truck behind the white line in our space while unloading the car and about our car not having the tag hanging in the windshield correctly. Nothing more than ex HOA board members walking around making sure every rule is enforced. Also paid a state ranger $60 cash to park my car for the night otherwise he was going to write me a ticket because I was in an open RV spot. I've never been asked to pay cash to cops before but looks like that's how they roll there. Very limited parking, a lot of rules and a lot of people making sure they're enforced.  Won't be going back.

LOVE the tide pool action here but HATE the tar! 
LOVE the camping spots in the San Miguel area of the state beach and bathrooms were pretty clean. The surrounding area of carpenteria is also fun and welcoming but damn that tar! Yuck!

I camp here with my family a few times every year so this is my favorite beach by far.
I was just camping a few days ago and the hosts took our wood that our friends left for us and when we told the hosts it was ours they replied "looks like we got to it first." And they proceeded to walk away with it and never give it back. So yeah, if the hosts ever see this... stop stealing wood from people who clearly have it in their possession. 
Thanks ;|

Our first time camping in Carp.  Stayed in spot A20 the weekend of 5/15/10 with a large group of other families.  We had a mix of trailers & tents and took most of the Anacapa loop.  The area was grassy and clean and very good for children and pets.  Beware if you have a trailer that some of the parking is right next to the next spot.  For instance A20, A19 and A18 are all one large parking spot.  There are lines, but if you have a trailer on A19, your door would open up to the car(s) parked in A20.  Not a lot of space if you pull out the canopy.

A20 was in my opinion the prime spot for trailer or tents.  We easily had our 2 tents and could have placed more.  This spot has a fence between the campground and the beach, so ppl don't walk through your site.  Water was right at the street for washing, etc.  There is a large (huge) grassy field just to the right (if looking towards the beach).  The kids could take their frisbees, softballs, kites, etc right there and it was only 100 feet from the campground.  Nice open area.

Here's the scoop on the sites to "group" together if going with others.  A18, 19 & 20 are 3 in a row.  A10, 11 & 12 are also 1,2,3 with the parking right next to each other.  A24 & 25 are close and should be shared.  A13 & 14 are a pair.  A5 & 8 are together.  A16, 17, 21, 22, 23, 26 & 27 are single spots with their own parking.

Also note that the north side of the camp has the train running by.  A10 would be the closest to the train, but the horns are loud enough to wake everyone in the camp when it comes by.

Overall, great place to camp.  Kids can ride bikes, scooters, etc with little or no issue.  Do make sure you wear a helmet, as the park rangers will patrol and make you walk you bike back to camp if a kid doesn't have their helmet.

One other note:  We were warned that people will come through at night and steal unlocked bikes and coolers.  Best to bring locks and stash the coolers in you locked car at night.  Otherwise you could wake up sad and hungry.  Good thing there is a Vons and drug store right up the street, so you can restock any food supplies you need.  (You can easily walk to these if you wish to wait and buy food when you get up there).

This beach has the longest, most mesmerizing sunsets! Enough said?? 

Considering it's geographic location is has primarily low tides almost all year round which is great for boogey boarding with the family. It's relaxing, romantic atmosphere is also wonderful for a long stroll on the beach over conversation and discussion over where to drink/dine on Linden Ave afterwards. (The adorable little town walking distance away.)

It's generally pretty easy to find parking, more so than a lot of other beaches in SB County, however if you are looking to stop by for the day with a large party I'd definitely recommend arriving at an early hour on the hottest and sunniest of days. 

Overall a great beach for singles, lovers, families, and beyond. Tide pools and campgrounds to the way left and a peaceful, artsy garden area to the right. There's ping pong, snow cones, volleyball, and a 5 star burger and milkshake stop (The Spot) 2 minutes walking distance away.

I think the thing that makes it most unique beach in Carp is the platforms they put out for kids to swim out to and jump off of in the summer. Those things are a hoot! Definitely worth visiting multiple times throughout the summer.

This place use to be so good. The camp host and parking people will harass you and ruin your whole trip. Not worth it. I got this spot six month in advance and was looking forward to our stay for weeks. Until we got there and everyone around us were harass every single time they could. Horrible horrible horrible

If you're the type of camper who enjoys solitude, overwhelming nature, and a respite from society, this is **NOT** the campground for you.

But if you're looking for a taste of the California coast, the archetypal beach town, and full cell signal, this campground serves its purpose.

Despite being the former, my family camped here over a holiday weekend. We slept in a tent surrounded by RVs, the scent of salty sea air overpowered by the noxious fumes of generators. There are hot showers and clean, flushing toilets. There were a TON of kids riding bikes, scooters, and skateboards in a loop throughout the campground. And the Amtrak rumbles through about every hour.

If you're camping with young kids, stay in the Anacapa loop. It's a short walk to the Tomol Interpretive Play Area, a really cool playground with some area history thrown in. Grab lunch at The Spot across the street, then head back on the boarded trail to the sweet little nature center in the campground. (They have a little tidepool feature with a big ol' sea slug.) If you're running into town for firewood (which you shouldn't, since it's no cheaper than the camp host's $7 a bundle), hit Happy Llama for coffee on the way back. 

So, this campground is not for me. But it gets four stars for what it is: a place to pitch a tent with creature comforts and a lot of neighbors. Just know what to expect before you book.

This is a beautiful beach that has volleyballs nets, bathrooms nearby, boardwalk paths to explore and even a nearby kids park.   It is a short walk to the meat of the restaurants and shops just up the road.  I'd love to stay here for a weekend sometime!  This is the perfect day trip from LA!

Damnit I can't BELIEVE I haven't written a review about this beach yet.  It is AMAZING.  Know why?

I lived in LA where at the beach, 
it was too crowded.  
Too much going on.  
Too much to pay for parking.  
And on top of that, NO PARKING, goddamn.  

This beach doesn't have that drama.  Or those wanna-be celebrities.  Or that pain in the ass parking enforcement.  This beach is just peaceful.

I do everything here: think, cry, clear my mind, meditate, walk, write.  Everything.  This beach is truly a blessing.  What a gift to be able to park and walk right out onto the sand.  I will never take this for granted

Safe and clean beach. Easy parking. Walking is fun. Lots of space so you're not sitting on top of other people. Plenty of restrooms. Food nearby. Great way to have time with the family! Social distancing is enforced.

Very good service 
Well done 
I am well satisfied 
I will definitely be recommending this company to family and friends !

Thank you

Spent a night tent camping by the beach and had a blast. We were able to walk to a local burger joint and a liquor store to grab some yummy locally brewed beers. 

Everything about this camping spot is well kept. And for those who like to rough it but still smell nice, they have free showers. ;)
Fell in love with this small town. Glamping with our RV before the boys have to go back to school. A short but peaceful getaway

I camped here as a child & not much has changed. We had a beachside campsite which we booked six months ago & trailer from RV 101. Fit right into our space, a comfortable, nice way to camp. Set up was done for us, all we had to do is move in for the week!

I'm not a fan of tents but the people beside us camped in their minivan & they had a tent on the beach to accommodate everyone. I thought this was the RV/trailer site? 

They brought a baby & too little dogs so the entire campground could enjoy their yipes while the family was off somewhere. They were up late around their smoky fire, which blew right into our trailer every night. 

The beach looked pretty. The sand was so soft as I walked out to the shoreline. Then, ouch! It was full of pistachio & peanut shells, straws, plastic bottles, old chunks of wood from past campfires, loads of plastic odds & ends. I did a lot of clean up around our spot, for the benefit of the earth & my feet!

How about a beach clean up, Carpinteria State Beach?! 

The seals played every morning in the waves. We saw dolphins swimming north & south just beyond the surfers. 

If they could clean up the beach, I'd give it more stars but as long as my beach at home is cleaner, I'll stick with it.

This is a great spot for camping. At least the spot we picked was. While 60$ is a little much for one night, the location is hard to beat. Our site had our own direct access to the beach and was super close to the bathrooms. The bathrooms were crazy clean. Someone came by multiple times a day to clean. Sinks with hot water are outside and there is access to showers as well. The campsite is doggie friendly, sadly the beach is not. But if you walk a couple blocks down you can find a beach for pups to play. The town close by had everything you need from a convenience store to tons of places to eat. Can't wait to come back again in the future to spend more time.

A friend of ours was able to get a couple spots to camp here and passed one of them to us. We were very excited to meet up with our friends and their families. Fast forward to our first night stay there, we were told generators need to be cut off at 10pm. No problem at all. I go to help a friends husband back in leaving my wife, his wife, and our 3 kids back at our trailer. Well apparently while I'm gone (at 9pm) a ranger by the name of C. Phelps came by to talk to my wife about the generator being on. He said they are supposed to be off at 8 (not a problem) but my wife told him we were told 10 and apologized for the misunderstanding and let him know as soon as I get back that I'd turn it off because she didn't know how. He replied (in front of the other wives) that since it's our first time and that's the info we were given that he'd let it go til 10. Fast forward we're get back start a fire and we're hanging out with the kids and the ranger C. Phelps comes up and tell me (the owner of the trailer and spot were in) to "come stand over here" so I complied and as soon as I got there he said "no, over here" which was two steps to my right then proceeded to harass me over the generator and lie about what he said the the girls! In front of them then (without straight calling them liars) he kept saying he didn't say that. Then I reached into my pocket to grab my keys and he tried to act as if we were a bunch of criminals and treat us like he's LAPD and try to tell us "it makes us officers nervous when you people reach in your pockets". I said " look around you. We are family having a good time. We have 5 kids playing inside the trailer, there are 6 adults sitting by the fire, and we're getting ready to roast s'mores with the kids until this guy decided to show up and treat us like criminals! Well that ruined our first night there. We were so excited to go to this campground for so long and this guy bursted our bubble really quick. So that ended our first night maybe the next day would be ok and make up for it. 

Day two, we got all the kids ready for the beach then we all took off and got a spot around 1pm and hung out. We had a couple drinks and had fun with the kids but weren't out that long as it was packed and very warm out so we all decided to go back and get some lunch and hang out. Well we split up to get showered and ready to eat. While I was outside we got a new neighbor which the old neighbor told us right when we pulled up the day before that they were only staying for 1 night because they couldn't get the site for the weekend. In comes C. Phelps and I know he's got something to say. So he now comes and tell us our mat was in another campsite. Well at carpenteria, they have like spots that are three parking spots. The outside spots have grass (dead grass) areas and there are grass areas behind the three spots. We figured the outside spots had those areas and the middle spots had the areas behind the spots. Apparently C. Phelps begs to differ. He came and threw our mat behind our trailer and that's what he came for. I said ok sorry again we have never been here be on top of us not being able to have our slide out or our stairs down, now we only have a sliver of a campsite while the other two have two very large "L" shaped areas and we all paid the same. So it's becoming very clear ranger C. Phelps has something against us and we don't have any idea why. We never argued with him on anything at any time. He now says that all of the sudden, they received countless number of calls the night before about noise, I assumed he meant the generator (which we immediately turned off and quieted down because we didn't have to talk over it). So I asked him a simple question, "then why did you not come back? You came for the generator noise but if you had another "countless number of calls" then why wouldn't you come back to tell us to be quiet?" That's when he says we are being ejected. I said ejected for what? He says because we we being "loud" and had our generator on past 8. Well he told us 10 but denied it again. This time my wife and my buddies wife heard that and came out and told him what he said. He then ADMITS he told them 10pm was ok. THEN WHY ARE WE GETTING EJECTED FOR THAT!!! Then he tried to say that the neighbor next to us left because of us, another LIE flat out lie. He then says we have to leave.
